title: Tesla Says Company Sold a Record 1.3 Million Vehicles Last Year, Falls Short of 50% Growth Pledge
summary: Tesla announced it sold a record 1.3 million vehicles in 2022, a 40% increase year-over-year, but this figure still fell short of CEO Elon Musk's ambitious 50% growth target for the company. The shortfall occurred despite significant year-end discounts and challenges like rising COVID-19 cases in China impacting production. The company also faces increasing competition and criticism regarding Musk's focus on Twitter, which contributed to a substantial decline in Tesla's stock value last year.

title: Better Chinese EV Stock: Nio vs. Li Auto
summary: This article compares two rapidly growing Chinese electric vehicle makers, Nio and Li Auto, to determine which is a better investment. It highlights their different product offerings, growth rates, and financial metrics, concluding that Li Auto is the more compelling choice due to stronger growth, higher vehicle margins, and profitability, making it a potential hidden gem for investors looking for an alternative to struggling U.S. EV makers.

title: Tesla stock suffers worst-ever year in 2022 despite deliveries hitting record high
summary: Tesla's stock experienced its worst year in 2022, plummeting 65% despite recording an all-time high of 1.3 million vehicle deliveries. This decline was attributed to logistical challenges, fears of slowing demand, Elon Musk's stock offloading, and his acquisition of Twitter. Analysts project improved logistics and continued delivery growth in 2023, though concerns about demand amid economic slowdowns persist.

title: Foxconn to use Nvidia chips to build self-driving vehicle platforms
summary: Electronics manufacturer Foxconn and chipmaker Nvidia Corp have partnered to develop autonomous vehicle platforms. Foxconn will manufacture electronic control units (ECUs) based on Nvidia's DRIVE Orin chip for self-driving cars, aiming to address challenges in the global automotive market. Nvidia views this collaboration as a way to scale its efforts and meet the increasing demand for chips in autonomous and connected vehicles, projecting a $300 billion market opportunity in the automotive sector.

title: Lucid, Rivian and others should explore distribution deals with established brands
summary: The article suggests that emerging EV manufacturers like Lucid and Rivian should consider forming distribution partnerships with established automotive brands. This strategy could help these newer companies overcome challenges related to scaling production and expanding market reach. By leveraging existing dealership networks and infrastructure, they might achieve greater success in the competitive EV market.

title: Tuesday's ETF with Unusual Volume: IWL
summary: The iShares Russell Top 200 ETF (IWL) experienced unusually high trading volume on Tuesday, with shares down about 0.8%. Tesla and Apple, major components of the ETF, saw significant trading volume and share price drops, while PayPal Holdings performed best, rising 4.3%.

title: GM ships 2.2 million U.S. vehicles in 2022
summary: General Motors (NYSE: GM) delivered 2.2 million vehicles in the U.S. in 2022, securing its position as the industry leader in total sales and key market segments like full-size pickups and SUVs. The company plans to expand its electric vehicle market share in 2023 with nine EV models, including the bestselling Chevrolet Bolt EV and Bolt EUV series. GM's strategy centers on the Ultium battery platform to power a wide range of vehicles for an all-electric future.

title: Market Insights: EVs and plug-in hybrids to represent 81 percent of new U.S. vehicle sales in 2045
summary: A forecast predicts that electric vehicles (EVs) and plug-in hybrids (PHEVs) will constitute 81% of new U.S. light vehicle sales by 2045, making up 42% of all vehicles in operation. The projection also highlights an anticipated reduction in the total cost of EV ownership due to decreasing battery costs. However, infrastructure challenges, including the availability and reliability of charging stations, remain significant hurdles for EV adoption.

title: Hyundai, BYD, Polestar adopt Nvidia’s cloud gaming service
summary: Hyundai Motor Group, BYD, and Polestar have adopted Nvidia's cloud-based gaming service, GeForce NOW, for their vehicles. This integration allows drivers and passengers to stream games, with plans to expand the number of available titles in Hyundai's electric vehicles. Hyundai has collaborated with Nvidia since 2020 on AI-powered connected car technology and previously adopted the Nvidia Drive platform.

title: Report: South Korea fines Tesla $2.2M over cold-weather range
summary: South Korea has fined Tesla 2.85 billion won ($2.2 million) for allegedly misleading consumers about the driving range of its electric vehicles, particularly concerning performance in cold weather. The Korea Fair Trade Commission (KFTC) found that Tesla's website did not adequately disclose the significant range reduction—up to 50.5%—that its EVs experience in cold temperatures. This decision follows a 2021 finding by a consumer advocacy group highlighting similar issues with EV range in winter conditions in South Korea.

title: Sony and Honda unveil Afeela EV as part of CES 2023
summary: Sony Honda Mobility unveiled the Afeela EV prototype at CES 2023, showcasing a software-defined vehicle with 45 sensors, 5G connectivity, and entertainment features. The joint venture, which became official in October, aims to deliver premium EVs in North America and Japan starting in 2026, leveraging expertise from Sony and Honda. Collaborations with Qualcomm and Epic Games underscore a focus on advanced in-car experiences and continuous updates for the vehicle.

title: The ARK Innovation ETF: Tesla Is Cathie Wood’s Latest Problem
summary: The ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK) is experiencing a difficult start to 2023, largely due to the continued selloff of Tesla Inc. (TSLA) stock, its most heavily weighted investment. Despite averaging down on Tesla, its weighting in ARKK has decreased to 6.6%, though overconcentration remains a concern for the fund.

title: Tesla's stock lost over $700 billion in value. Elon Musk's Twitter deal didn't help
summary: Tesla's stock plummeted by 65% in 2022, losing over $700 billion in value due to a tough year for the auto industry, increased competition in the EV market, and investor concerns over Elon Musk's distraction with his Twitter acquisition. Despite the significant stock drop, Tesla still leads the EV market and remains the world's most valuable automaker. The article highlights that while Tesla's sales volume will likely continue to grow, the stock market is re-evaluating its valuation from a tech company to an auto manufacturer.

title: Chinese companies lead in LiDAR tech
summary: Chinese companies are leading the global market in LiDAR technology, a key component for autonomous driving, with the potential to make cars safer and smarter. Companies like Hesai and RoboSense showcased advanced LiDAR products at CES 2023, highlighting their significant developments, market partnerships, and plans for mass production and expansion into the US market. The article emphasizes that these advancements are bringing fully autonomous consumer cars closer to reality.

title: XPeng stock just misses a record selloff, snaps 5-day win streak
summary: XPeng Inc. (XPEV) stock plunged nearly 15% to close at $10.11, narrowly missing its record one-day drop. This selloff broke a five-day win streak for the company's U.S.-listed shares. The decline was attributed to negative sentiment among China-based electric vehicle makers following Tesla Inc.'s price cuts in China.

title: CES 2023: AMD, Nvidia, auto applications get the hype, but analysts say this one chip maker ruled
summary: At CES 2023, while companies like Advanced Micro Devices Inc. and Nvidia Corp. received significant attention, analysts suggest that a lower-profile chip maker is better positioned post-convention. Morgan Stanley analyst Joseph Moore noted ongoing caution regarding overall chip demand, particularly due to softness in China, but highlighted autos as a strong theme at the event.

title: CEO Carlos Tavares says Stellantis might shutter more auto plants, cites high EV costs
summary: Carlos Tavares, CEO of Stellantis, warned at CES 2023 that more car factories might close if high electric vehicle (EV) prices deter consumers and sales fall to pre-pandemic levels. He expressed concern that automakers could lose pricing power as chip supplies recover. Stellantis previously announced the permanent closure of its Belvidere, Illinois plant due to high EV costs, reinforcing Tavares's stance on potential future plant closures if market demand for EVs shrinks.

title: Pepsi turns on Tesla semis
summary: PepsiCo plans to deploy 100 heavy-duty Tesla Semis in 2023 for deliveries to customers such as Walmart and Kroger, becoming the first company to extensively use these electric trucks. The company is purchasing the Semis outright and investing in charging infrastructure at its California plants, partially supported by state grants and federal subsidies. This move marks PepsiCo's commitment to reducing its environmental impact through early adoption of electric truck technology.

title: Tesla: what 2023 holds for the electric vehicle company and why it might be time for Musk to go
summary: Despite Tesla's strong financial performance and advanced technology, its market leadership is threatened by increasing competition and challenges like production delays and software recalls. The article suggests that CEO Elon Musk's divided attention between Tesla and Twitter, coupled with his controversial management style, could become a liability for Tesla, indicating a need for the company to mature beyond its "sales only" approach and possibly for a change in leadership.

title: Ford U.S. Vehicle Sales Fell 2.2% In 2022 As Industry Suffered Weakest Year Since 2011
summary: Ford Motor Co. experienced a 2.2% decline in U.S. vehicle sales in 2022, marking the second consecutive year of decrease, primarily due to parts shortages and economic challenges. Despite the annual drop, December saw strong sales, particularly for the profitable F-150 pickup, and Ford highlighted its position as the second-largest U.S. producer of battery electric vehicles. Analysts are projecting a significant increase in Q4 EPS for Ford, anticipating $0.59 compared to $0.26 a year prior.

title: Disney asks employees to work from office four days a week - CNBC
summary: Walt Disney Co.'s top boss, Bob Iger, has mandated that employees return to corporate offices four days a week starting March 1, as reported by CNBC. This move aligns Disney with other companies like Snap, Tesla, and Uber, which are also asking employees to return to the office after widespread remote work during the pandemic. The decision follows Iger's return as CEO and coincides with Disney's efforts to boost investor confidence and profitability in its streaming media unit.

title: A look back at the year in ETFs
summary: The Canadian ETF industry saw continued product expansion in 2022 despite market challenges, with roughly 120 new ETFs launched. Popular themes included ESG mandates, leveraged ETFs, and funds focused on disruptive technologies, alongside several firsts like single-stock ETFs and target-date families. This article reviews the key trends and new offerings from major providers in the Canadian ETF landscape throughout the year.

title: ARK Invest's Cathie Wood Bets Big on Crypto
summary: Cathie Wood's ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK) continues to invest in Coinbase (COIN) shares, acquiring an additional $1.45 million worth despite recent layoffs, bringing ARK Invest's total holdings to about 8.6 million shares across its ETFs. Wood believes Coinbase will emerge "very strong" from the crypto bear market due to CEO Brian Armstrong's focus on regulatory clarity and the recent downfall of FTX. She remains bullish on high-growth stocks and the resilience of the Bitcoin and Ethereum blockchains amid economic tightening.

title: Tesla to spend $775 million expanding its Gigafactory Texas electric vehicle plant near Austin
summary: Tesla plans to invest $775 million in expanding its Gigafactory Texas, located near Austin. The expansion will include new facilities like a cathode facility, cell test lab, die shop, drive unit, and an undisclosed 693,093-square-foot facility, with construction starting soon and continuing into early 2024. This growth initiative comes after a challenging year for Tesla's stock and ongoing production struggles at the factory, despite Elon Musk's efforts to build his Texas-based empire.

title: EV Startup Lucid Beats Production Target, Struggles to Deliver Cars
summary: Lucid Group Inc., a luxury electric-vehicle maker, announced it exceeded its 2022 production objective by manufacturing 7,180 Air sedans. However, the company only managed to deliver 4,369 of these vehicles to customers, facing logistics issues. This comes after Lucid had previously lowered its production forecast due to supply-chain and manufacturing challenges.

title: Tesla slashes prices on new electric vehicle models in the U.S.
summary: Tesla has significantly cut prices on its new electric vehicle models in the U.S., including the Model 3, Y, X, and S. These price reductions could enable some buyers to qualify for the $7,500 federal tax credit and come as the company faces declining market share and a "significant demand problem," with its stock dropping 65% in 2022. While some investors see the price cuts as a way to boost volume amidst growing competition, others worry about a potential pricing war and Musk's divided attention with Twitter.

title: Tesla cuts prices across the board up to 20%
summary: Tesla has announced significant price cuts across its entire model lineup, with some reductions as high as 20%. The Model Y received the largest cut, bringing its effective price for many U.S. buyers below $47,000 after tax credits, which could be a strategic move to qualify for new clean-vehicle tax credits. These price adjustments reverse years of increases and come amidst softening demand for used Teslas and increasing competition in the EV market, despite a record sales year for the brand in 2022.

title: Pulte Lawsuit Update: A 2nd Exec Named In Twitter Activity Inquiry
summary: A second PulteGroup official, General Counsel Todd Sheldon, has been drawn into a lawsuit filed by William J. Pulte against a former executive. The lawsuit centers on whether Sheldon deactivated or deleted Twitter accounts after evidence surfaced that former executive Brandon Jones engaged in disparaging tweets, potentially violating PulteGroup's code of conduct. The subpoena requests Twitter to release details on Sheldon's activity, though PulteGroup maintains the lawsuit is private and does not involve the company or its employees.

title: Ryder layoffs: 800 jobs to be cut at Applied Materials site
summary: Ryder System Inc. will cut 800 jobs and close its operations at the Applied Materials site in Austin, as disclosed in a letter to the Texas Workforce Commission. The transportation and logistics company expects "one or more other operators" to take over its responsibilities. This move comes despite Ryder reporting significant revenue increases in Q3 2022.

title: Ev Charging Stations Market to See Massive Growth by 2028 | Siemens, Tesla, Schneider Electric
summary: A new research study by HTF MI evaluates the Worldwide EV Charging Stations Market, forecasting massive growth by 2028. The report provides an in-depth analysis of market trends, growth drivers, technologies, and investment structures, segmenting the market by residential and commercial applications, as well as AC and DC charging stations. Key players analyzed include ABB, Tesla, Schneider Electric, and Siemens, with a focus on market share, size, and future growth outlook across various global regions.

title: Chinese EV maker Xpeng joins Tesla, Seres in price cuts
summary: Chinese automaker Xpeng announced price cuts for several of its electric vehicle models, including its best-selling P7 sedan, P5 sedan, and G3i SUVs. This move follows similar price reductions by Tesla and Seres, intensifying competition and fueling expectations of a wider price war in China's EV market. Xpeng aims to make intelligent vehicles more accessible and will offer free extended maintenance services as compensation to existing car owners who purchased before the cuts.

title: Xpeng Follows Tesla With Major Price Cuts on China Electric Cars
summary: Xpeng Inc. has joined the electric vehicle price war in China by reducing the prices of its models by up to 12.5%. This move follows Tesla's earlier price cuts at the beginning of the year. The price of Xpeng's G3i SUV dropped significantly, while the P7 sedan also saw price reductions, though the G9 model remained unchanged.

title: 2023 Tesla Model Y Review, Ratings, Specs, Prices, and Photos
summary: The 2023 Tesla Model Y is an electric crossover SUV praised for its excellent range, performance, and spacious interior, but sometimes criticized for inconsistent build quality and styling. It offers Long Range and Performance versions with impressive acceleration and efficiency, high safety ratings, and a minimalist interior centered around a 15-inch touchscreen. While its "Full Self-Driving" mode is noted as a misnomer, the vehicle provides good value, especially in its Long Range variant, compared to other electric crossovers.

title: Tesla video promoting self-driving was staged, engineer testifies
summary: A senior Tesla engineer testified that a 2016 video promoting the company's self-driving technology was staged, showcasing features like stopping at red lights and accelerating at green lights that the system did not possess at the time. This testimony, given in a lawsuit concerning a fatal 2018 crash, confirmed that the video was produced at Elon Musk's request to demonstrate "what was possible to build into the system" rather than what was available to customers. Tesla is currently facing lawsuits and a criminal investigation over its driver assistance claims.

title: Cotações de BDRs: Bolsa de Valores ao Vivo e últimas notícias
summary: This article provides live BDR (Brazilian Depositary Receipts) quotes and the latest financial news from Money Times. It features an extensive list of BDRs available for trading, along with recent news touching on BDR recommendations from financial institutions like BTG Pactual and Empiricus, B3 trading volumes, and company updates that impact BDRs. The content caters to investors interested in international stocks through the Brazilian stock exchange.

title: Frito-Lay Focuses on Sustainability in Transformation of California Facility
summary: Frito-Lay has transformed its Modesto, California facility into a showcase for sustainable manufacturing, warehousing, and distribution technologies. This project, which began in 2019, aims to demonstrate the benefits of zero-emission and near zero-emission technologies. The upgrades have already resulted in a 91% reduction in greenhouse gas emissions from direct fleet operations, significantly reducing the environmental impact.

title: "Hertz Electrifies" Launches in Denver; New Public-Private Partnership between Hertz and Cities aims to Transform the Rental Car Industry and Accelerate Mainstream Consumer Adoption of Electric Vehicles
summary: Hertz has launched "Hertz Electrifies," a new public-private partnership initiative in Denver, Colorado, aimed at accelerating the transition to electric vehicles (EVs) and fostering economic and environmental benefits. This program intends to integrate Hertz's EV fleet into American cities and support charging infrastructure development. The initiative will also focus on creating educational and job training opportunities in EV mobility and engaging with local organizations to bring the benefits of electrification to underserved communities.

title: EV Charging Firm Volta Acquired by Shell
summary: Shell's unit is acquiring EV charging firm Volta in an all-cash deal worth about $169 million. Volta operates 3,050 EV chargers in the U.S. and Europe, with another 3,400 in development, making it Shell's second EV charging company acquisition. This move highlights increasing investment in EV charging infrastructure by major energy and automotive companies.

title: Silicon Valley 2.0? 5 US cities that could be the new tech start-up central
summary: Several southern US cities are emerging as new tech hubs, drawing companies like Tesla, Apple, Oracle, Microsoft, and Google away from California's Silicon Valley. High tax rates, labor costs, regulations, and a declining quality of life in Silicon Valley are pushing these companies to look for more affordable and business-friendly environments in states such as Texas, Arizona, Georgia, and Florida. These new tech centers offer incentives, lower costs of living, and new luxury residential developments to accommodate the influx of workers.

title: Should You Invest in the iShares U.S. Consumer Discretionary ETF (IYC)?
summary: This article evaluates the iShares U.S. Consumer Discretionary ETF (IYC) as an investment option. It details IYC's objective to track the consumer discretionary sector, its expense ratio, top holdings like Amazon, Home Depot, and Tesla, and its performance. The article also provides alternatives such as the Vanguard Consumer Discretionary ETF (VCR) and the Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLY).

title: Ford to cut up to 3,200 European jobs, union says, vowing to fight
summary: Ford Motor Co. plans to cut up to 3,200 jobs across Europe, primarily in product development and administrative roles, with Germany being the most affected, according to the German IG Metall union. The company attributes these structural changes to the shift towards electric vehicle production and the need to become more competitive amidst rising costs and economic slowdowns. IG Metall has vowed to fight these cuts, threatening continent-wide disruption if negotiations do not secure the future of the workers.

title: ‘The Nasdaq is our favorite short.’ This market strategist sees recession and a credit crunch slamming stocks in 2023.
summary: Market strategist Keith McCullough predicts a U.S. corporate profit recession and a credit crunch in 2023, leading to a significant downturn in stocks, particularly the Nasdaq. He argues that the Federal Reserve's interest rate hikes will exacerbate these issues. McCullough advises investors to short growth stocks and shift to defensive assets like gold, silver, and defensive ETFs.

title: ‘The Nasdaq is our favorite short.’ This market strategist sees recession and a credit crunch slamming stocks in 2023.
summary: Hedgeye CEO Keith McCullough predicts a U.S. corporate profit recession and credit crunch in 2023, causing a slide in the S&P 500, junk bonds, and previous market favorites like Tesla. McCullough, who has been critical of the Federal Reserve's handling of inflation and interest rates, recommends investors consider gold, silver, and defensive ETFs as a hedge. He views the Nasdaq as a prime short opportunity due to these anticipated market conditions.

title: Bad Week of Earnings Pulls Q4 Growth Rate Lower
summary: The S&P 500's blended EPS growth for Q4 decreased to -4.6% due to a week of mixed earnings reports from major financial institutions and other companies. This marks the lowest growth rate in over two years, driven by factors like increased loan loss provisions and consumer credit deterioration. Despite the decline, the earnings season is picking up, with key companies like Microsoft, Tesla, IBM, and Intel expected to report, and some companies like Danaher and Weyerhaeuser signaling positive results.

title: Stocks Making the Biggest Moves After Hours: Tesla, Chevron, ServiceNow, Levi Strauss, IBM and More
summary: This article details the after-hours stock movements of several major companies following their latest earnings reports. Tesla saw a slight rise despite volatile trading, while Chevron's shares advanced after announcing a large stock repurchasing program. ServiceNow tumbled, and IBM fell despite beating forecasts due to job cuts, while Levi Strauss and Las Vegas Sands both experienced gains.

title: STMicroelectronics Tops Estimates. The Tesla Supplier’s Stock Is Rising.
summary: STMicroelectronics, a European chip maker and Tesla supplier, saw its stock rise after reporting profit and revenue that exceeded analyst expectations. The company also announced plans to invest approximately $4 billion in capital spending to boost its manufacturing capacity this year.

title: Toyota CEO and President Akio Toyoda to step down
summary: Akio Toyoda, CEO and President of Toyota Motor Corp., will step down from his role on April 1, 2023, after 13 years at the helm. He will be succeeded by Koji Sato, the current Chief Branding Officer, while Toyoda transitions to become the new chairman of the board. This change aims to drive Toyota's transformation and further its efforts in areas like electric vehicles and carbon neutrality.

title: Por toma de ganancias, concluyó la racha positiva de la Bolsa
summary: The Merval index in Argentina concluded its positive streak with a 2.8% drop due to profit-taking, cutting short a five-session bullish run. Major losses were seen in companies like Central Puerto and Banco Macro, while Argentine ADRs also mostly declined in Wall Street. Meanwhile, Wall Street itself ended the week with gains, particularly driven by tech stocks, amid hopes for moderating inflation and positive quarterly results, with the Nasdaq experiencing its best weekly performance since July last year.

title: Used Tesla Prices Take a Dive; Should I Buy One?
summary: Used Tesla prices have been declining significantly since their peak in July, a trend that accelerated recently and was likely influenced by Tesla's new-car price cuts. While new Teslas are also more affordable, the new federal EV tax credit rules complicate eligibility for both new and used EV purchases. Shoppers considering a used Tesla should weigh the decreasing prices against tax credit limitations, battery degradation, and fluctuating inventory levels.

title: Ford slashes cost of EVs as it plans to boost production of Mustang Mach-E
summary: Ford announced it is cutting the prices of its electric vehicles, including the Mustang Mach-E, to remain competitive in the market, following similar price reductions by Tesla. The automaker plans to increase production of the Mach-E to shorten customer wait times, attributing the cost reduction to streamlined supply chain dynamics. Ford emphasized its commitment to making EVs more accessible and boosting its market share.

title: WSJ News Exclusive | TravelCenters of America to Add EV Fast-Charger Stations
summary: TravelCenters of America Inc. and Electrify America LLC are partnering to install approximately 1,000 EV fast chargers nationwide, starting this year. This initiative will add fast-charging stalls to about 200 TravelCenters' TA and Petro Stopping Centers, addressing the current shortage of highway fast chargers crucial for wider EV adoption.

title: Generac introduces residential EV charger
summary: Generac Power Systems has entered the electric vehicle charging market with its new Generac EV charger. This Level 2 home charger can fully charge a car battery in four to six hours and is compatible with most EVs. The move expands Generac's presence in the clean energy sector, building on its previous introductions of microinverters and battery storage solutions.

title: TravelCenters of America to deploy 1,000 EV charging ports by 2028
summary: TravelCenters of America (TA) plans to install approximately 1,000 electric vehicle charging stations at 200 of its TA and Petro Stopping Centers by 2028. This initiative will be carried out in partnership with Electrify America, with the first locations expected to open later in 2023. TA joins other major travel center chains entering the EV charging market, like Pilot Co. and Love’s, further expanding the accessibility of EV charging infrastructure for long-haul travelers.

title: Ford to cut prices of Mustang Mach-E, following Tesla's lead
summary: Ford Motor Co. has announced price reductions of up to $5,900 for its Mustang Mach-E electric SUV, following Tesla's recent global price cuts. This move aims to make some Mach-E models eligible again for the $7,500 federal tax credit and is part of Ford's strategy to increase production and share cost savings with customers. Analysts suggest this could signal the beginning of a price war in the EV market.

title: Top Performing Leveraged/Inverse ETFs: 01/29/2023
summary: This article highlights the top-performing leveraged and inverse ETFs for the week ending January 29, 2023. Key drivers for these strong performances include positive Tesla earnings, growth in electric and autonomous vehicles, and hopes for a slower pace of interest rate hikes from the Federal Reserve, boosting technology and consumer discretionary sectors. The list provides specific ETF tickers, their names, and weekly returns, along with brief explanations for their performance.

title: Austin company lays off 250 janitorial, maintenance workers after Tesla cancels contract
summary: A facilities services company, ABM Texas General Services, is laying off 255 janitorial and maintenance employees in Austin after their contract with Tesla was terminated. Tesla ended the contract on January 16, with a new vendor, SBM, set to take over services. The layoffs affect workers at Tesla's Austin property, including its corporate headquarters and manufacturing facility.

title: CEO longevity pays off for JPMorgan Chase’s Jamie Dimon and other chiefs of World’s Most Admired Companies
summary: This article highlights how CEO longevity, exemplified by Jamie Dimon of JPMorgan Chase, contributes to a company's success and reputation, as seen in Fortune's World's Most Admired Companies list. Long-tenured CEOs develop deep pattern recognition, build strong internal cultures, and foster innovation, leading to greater efficiency and financial performance. The article also touches on other long-serving CEOs like Warren Buffett and Jensen Huang, and notes Elon Musk's recent "most overrated CEO" distinction.

title: NIO, XPeng, and Li Auto Deliveries Struggle in January. The Stocks Rise Anyway.
summary: Despite a significant drop in January deliveries for Chinese EV makers NIO, XPeng, and Li Auto, their stock prices increased. This counter-intuitive market reaction suggests that investor expectations for January were even lower, and the reported figures were "better than feared." XPeng's sales, for instance, were down 60% from the previous year.

title: Tesla Investors Lost $12 Billion After Musk Tweet, Experts Tell Jury
summary: Tesla Inc. investors lost $12 billion after Elon Musk's 2018 tweet claiming he had "funding secured" to take the company private, expert witnesses testified in a trial. Lawyers for Musk contention that the initial part of the tweet was truthful was countered by testimony that emphasized the collective impact of the "interwoven bundle" of statements, the expert stressed the "funding secured" aspect was the new, impactful information. The case, now in its third week, delves into the impact of Musk's statements on Tesla's stock prices and potential damages to investors.

title: Geely’s Lotus going public via SPAC at US$5.4 billion valuation
summary: Lotus Technology, the electric-car maker owned by China’s Zhejiang Geely Holding Group, is merging with a blank-check company, L Catterton Asia Acquisition, in a deal valuing the combined entity at approximately US$5.4 billion. This move comes after Lotus Tech has been seeking to go public for some time, opting for a SPAC merger rather than a traditional IPO. Geely and other owners are expected to retain a significant 89.7% shareholding in Lotus Tech following the merger.

title: Rivian to lay off 6% of its workforce as EV price war concerns grow
summary: Rivian announced it will lay off 6% of its workforce to conserve cash amidst growing concerns of an EV price war, following price cuts by competitors like Tesla and Ford. CEO RJ Scaringe stated that these cuts would not affect manufacturing jobs at the company's Illinois factory. Despite a successful IPO, Rivian's shares have significantly dropped, prompting a focus on operational efficiency and profitability while ramping up production of its vehicles.

title: A battle may be shaping up between the two richest men in the world over who controls the luxury electric car market
summary: Bernard Arnault, the world's richest man, is backing a SPAC deal to take luxury carmaker Lotus Technology public, valuing it at $5.4 billion. This move positions Lotus, majority-owned by Geely Holding, to expand its electrified luxury vehicle offerings, potentially creating competition with Elon Musk's Tesla in the premium EV market although on a much smaller scale. Lotus boasts a rich European heritage and luxury cachet that appeals to Arnault, despite its relatively small production volume compared to Tesla.

title: Ten Questions About the Disruptive Materials at the Center of the Clean Energy Transition
summary: This article addresses ten key questions about disruptive materials essential for the clean energy transition, such as copper, nickel, lithium, and rare earth elements. It examines the current supply and demand dynamics, geopolitical impacts, and potential shortages that could drive price movements and create opportunities for companies and investors. The piece highlights how increasing demand for clean technologies like EVs and renewable energy systems is creating a supercycle for these critical materials.

title: General Motors Files to Trademark ‘GM Financial Insurance Company’ Name
summary: General Motors has filed to trademark "GM Financial Insurance Company," indicating a potential expansion into the insurance sector. This move follows GM's increasing involvement in usage-based insurance solutions, such as its OnStar Insurance launched in late 2020, which tailors rates based on individual driving behavior. While OnStar Insurance is underwritten by American Family Insurance subsidiaries and is distinct from the new trademark filing, it highlights GM's strategic interest in leveraging vehicle data for insurance purposes, similar to Tesla's insurance offerings.

title: Enphase (ENPH) introduces bi-directional EV charging for an all-in-one solar home energy system
summary: Enphase Energy (ENPH) has announced the successful demonstration of its bi-directional EV charger, which will allow electric vehicles to power homes during outages or support the grid during peak demand. This technology integrates with Enphase’s existing microinverter-based solar and battery systems, establishing an all-in-one solar home energy system. The charger supports vehicle-to-home (V2H), vehicle-to-grid (V2G), and Green charging capabilities and is expected to be available in 2024.

title: Tesla sales of China-made electric vehicles up 18% in January
summary: Tesla sold 66,051 China-made electric vehicles in January, an 18% increase from December and 10% higher than January last year. This increase comes after intentional production cuts in December and an extended Lunar New Year holiday in January to manage inventory. Tesla plans to boost output at its Shanghai plant in the coming months following price cuts that spurred demand, making it the second-best selling EV maker in China last month behind BYD.

title: State Street Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLY) Stock Price, Holdings, Dividend Yield
summary: This article provides a detailed overview of the Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLY), including its stock price, key statistics, top holdings, and dividend information. It highlights XLY's portfolio value, turnover rate, and its largest positions in companies like Amazon, Tesla, and Home Depot, based on its latest N-PORT filing. The report also includes historical performance data, fundamental charts, and recent trading actions within the ETF's portfolio.

title: Why Tesla Suddenly Raised Model Y Prices After Suddenly Lowering Them
summary: Tesla recently raised the prices of its Model Y Long Range and Performance models after an earlier price cut. This shift is primarily attributed to relaxed IRS rules regarding EV tax credit qualifications for SUVs, allowing Tesla to price vehicles above a previous $55,000 cap. The company's strategy also involves leveraging its market dominance to influence the competitive landscape for other EV manufacturers.

title: Hertz has fewer Teslas in its fleet than planned
summary: Hertz's rental fleet has less than half the number of Tesla cars it planned to order by the end of 2022, according to a recent regulatory filing. The company's fleet in the Americas contained 48,344 Tesla EVs, significantly less than the 100,000 electric cars initially scheduled from the automaker. Despite this, Hertz forecasted optimistic revenue and profit for its upcoming quarters, anticipating increased demand for rental cars due to a weak economy.

title: Cathie Wood's Ark Invest has loaded up on these 2 small-cap names for the last 7 trading sessions
summary: Cathie Wood's Ark Invest has consistently bought shares of two small-cap companies, Velo3D and Repare Therapeutics, over the last seven to eight trading sessions. Despite previously focusing on tech giants like Tesla, Ark now holds stakes of over 6% in both the metal 3D printing firm and the oncology treatment company. This move follows a strong January for Ark's flagship ETF, ARKK, which saw a 28% increase, recovering from a challenging 2022.

title: GM reaches computer chip supply deal with GlobalFoundries
summary: General Motors has signed a multi-year deal with chipmaker GlobalFoundries to secure a dedicated supply of semiconductors amidst a global shortage affecting auto production. GlobalFoundries will expand production capacity at its Malta, New York

title: General Motors signs deal with GlobalFoundries for exclusive U.S. semiconductor production
summary: General Motors has entered a long-term agreement with GlobalFoundries to establish exclusive U.S. production capacity for semiconductor chips, a move aimed at addressing the ongoing global chip shortage. This "industry first" deal will see GlobalFoundries dedicate production capacity at its upstate New York facility for GM's key auto suppliers. The agreement is expected to provide GM with a resilient supply of critical technology and ramp up over the next two to three years.

title: Buy Hertz Stock. It’s Cheap, Well-Run, and Renting Teslas.
summary: Hertz is presented as a strong investment opportunity, described as cheap and well-run. The article highlights Hertz's strategic move to include Teslas in its rental fleet, along with other electric vehicles, indicating a move towards modernization and future growth.

title: Toyota’s CEO-to-be outlines leadership team bullish on EVs
summary: Koji Sato, Toyota's incoming president and chief branding officer, unveiled a new management team that will prioritize the aggressive development of electric vehicles. Sato emphasized "electrification" as a core theme, with plans to launch a new, next-generation Lexus EV by 2026 and expand Toyota's overall EV offerings. He acknowledged that Toyota has been perceived as lagging in the EV market compared to rivals like Tesla, Nissan, and BYD, despite having battery electric vehicle technology.

title: Class 8 Truck Sales Reach 19,932 in January
summary: U.S. Class 8 retail sales in January increased 34.2% year-over-year to 19,932 units, despite a month-over-month drop from December's record high. Freightliner led in market share, while Peterbilt Motors Co. saw the largest year-over-year sales increase. Analysts attribute the strong sales partly to supply chain improvements and manufacturers clearing backlogs, rather than solely increased market demand.

title: These Stocks Are Leading the Growth Rebound
summary: Growth stocks, particularly in the tech sector, have seen a significant rebound in early 2023 after a difficult 2022, outperforming value stocks. This surge is likely driven by investor expectations that the Federal Reserve may soon pause interest rate hikes. Despite strong recent gains, the Morningstar US Growth Index still needs a substantial rally to recover fully from last year's losses.

title: bp plans to invest $1 billion in EV charging across US by 2030, helping to meet demand from Hertz’s expanding EV rentals
summary: BP announced plans to invest $1 billion into EV charging infrastructure across the United States by 2030, a move set to support the increasing demand for electric vehicles. A key part of this investment involves a collaboration with Hertz to install fast-charging stations at Hertz locations in major US cities, including gigahubs at high-demand areas like airports. This initiative aligns with BP's strategy to become an integrated energy company and Hertz's goal to make one-quarter of its rental fleet electric by the end of 2024.

title: US Foods Takes Initial Delivery of Battery-Electric Trucks
summary: US Foods has taken delivery of its first battery-electric Freightliner eCascadia trucks at its La Mirada, California distribution center, with plans to add 30 more in 2023. This initiative is part of the company's commitment to reduce its carbon footprint and achieve its near-term emissions reduction goals, specifically targeting a 32.5% decrease in Scopes 1 and 2 GHG emissions by 2032. The deployment of these zero-emission trucks, supported by various organizations and programs, also aims to reduce harmful particulate matter and NOx emissions in Southern California.

title: Tesla opens its EV charging network to the masses
summary: The Biden administration announced plans to infuse money into the nation's EV charging network, revealing that Tesla will open parts of its Supercharger network to other car manufacturers. This move could significantly expand charging options for non-Tesla EVs, as Tesla's network is considered reliable and widespread. The administration also detailed requirements for federal aid, including domestic sourcing of components, and new funding streams for charging infrastructure.

title: How Detroit’s Big Three cashed in on coachbuilders
summary: This article examines how Detroit's Big Three automakers (Cadillac, Chrysler, and Ford) repurposed the names of historic coachbuilders like Fleetwood, LeBaron, and Ghia to add perceived luxury and exclusivity to their high-line models after World War II. It details the origins of these coachbuilding firms, their eventual acquisition or partnerships with the major automakers, and how their names evolved from signifying bespoke craftsmanship to becoming trim levels on mass-produced vehicles, sometimes losing their historical connection. The piece concludes by noting that despite the dilution of their original meaning, these names helped keep the memory of coachbuilding alive and offered customers a sense of prestige.

title: Tesla workers at New York plant fired after labor push, union says
summary: Several employees at Tesla's Buffalo, New York plant were fired shortly after starting union organizing efforts, according to Tesla Workers United. The union claims the firings are retaliation and that a newly implemented policy prohibiting recording of workplace meetings violates labor laws. A complaint has been filed with the National Labor Relations Board, and the White House has commented on workers' rights to organize.

title: Tesla Fires Unionizing Autopilot Workers, Complaint Alleges
summary: Tesla Inc. fired dozens of Autopilot workers in Buffalo, N.Y., one day after they announced a union campaign, leading to a complaint filed with the U.S. National Labor Relations Board by Workers United. The union alleges that the terminations were in retaliation for union activity and aimed at discouraging further organization, with several terminated employees having been involved in the labor discussions. Organizers believe this move is a "collective retaliation" to intimidate workers, while the union aims to organize more employees and sees the firings as backfiring, galvanizing support for their cause.

title: Regulators force Tesla to recall 363,000 'Full Self-Driving' vehicles
summary: U.S. safety regulators have compelled Tesla to recall nearly 363,000 vehicles equipped with its "Full Self-Driving" system due to concerns about its behavior at intersections and adherence to speed limits. The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) stated the system can make unsafe moves, though Tesla plans to fix the issues with an online software update without fully agreeing with the agency's analysis. This action intensifies scrutiny on CEO Elon Musk's claims about the system's safety and capabilities, with NHTSA's investigation into Tesla's automated driving systems remaining open.

title: Musk undercuts Biden’s pro-worker climate push
summary: Tesla fired dozens of workers at its Buffalo gigafactory, including union organizing committee members, just one day after President Biden announced a deal with Elon Musk to open Tesla's EV charging network. This action contradicts the White House's pro-worker stance within the Inflation Reduction Act, which aims to create union jobs in the clean energy sector. The firings have led to a complaint with the National Relations Labor Board, with workers alleging retaliation for union activity.

title: Lithium miner Sigma jumps on report Tesla considering buyout
summary: Shares of Sigma Lithium Corp rose 21% in extended trading after a Bloomberg News report indicated that Tesla Inc was considering a takeover of the Canadian battery metals miner. Tesla has been exploring various mining options and speaking with potential advisers regarding a bid for Sigma Lithium, which is constructing a hard rock lithium mine in Brazil set to open by April. Elon Musk has previously stated Tesla's openness to acquiring a mining company to secure its supply of electric vehicle metals.

title: Tesla recalls 362,000 U.S. vehicles over Full Self-Driving software
summary: Tesla is recalling over 362,000 U.S. vehicles due to issues with its Full Self-Driving (FSD) Beta software, which U.S. regulators found could allow vehicles to act unsafely and increase crash risk. Despite Tesla disagreeing with the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration's (NHTSA) assessment, the company will issue an over-the-air software update. This recall adds to previous regulatory clashes and investigations surrounding Tesla's driver assistance systems.

title: Tesla Consider The Acquisition Of Logistics Mining Company Sigma Lithium Corp
summary: Tesla is reportedly considering acquiring Sigma Lithium Corp, a Canadian-based battery metal mining company, causing Sigma Lithium's share price to rise significantly. Sigma Lithium is finalizing a hard rock lithium mine in Brazil, expected to open in April, which will produce spoduemene concentrate for lithium hydroxide. This potential acquisition aligns with Tesla CEO Elon Musk's previous statements about buying mining companies to secure metal supplies for electric vehicles and accelerate clean energy technology adoption.

title: Tesla appoints former Dish Network attorney as general counsel
summary: Tesla has announced the appointment of Brandon Ehrhart as its new general counsel and corporate secretary. Ehrhart brings over two decades of experience from Dish Network, where he held the position of executive vice president and corporate secretary for Dish Network and general counsel for Dish Wireless. His background also includes a tenure at EchoStar Corporation and legal work at DLA Piper.

title: Resources Top 5: Elon Musk’s Tesla continues battery metals buying spree
summary: This article highlights five small-cap resources companies making news, focusing on Elon Musk's Tesla securing battery metals. Magnis Energy Technologies (ASX:MNS) signed a significant deal to supply Tesla with active anode material, while New Century Resources (ASX:NCZ) received a takeover offer from Sibanye-Stillwater. The piece also covers updates on Taruga Minerals (ASX:TAR), North Stawell Minerals (ASX:NSM), and Volt Resources (ASX:VRC), discussing their project developments and market positions.

title: EV maker Nio to build 1,000 battery-swap stations in China in 2023
summary: Chinese EV startup Nio Inc. announced plans to build 1,000 new battery-swapping stations in China during 2023, expanding its original goal of 400. This initiative aims to increase the total number of Nio's battery-swapping facilities to 2,300 by year-end, with a focus on urban areas and third- and fourth-tier cities. Nio stands out among EV makers for its commitment to battery swapping, a technology its rival Tesla previously dismissed.

title: My Lucid (LCID) Stock Price Prediction for 2025
summary: This article provides a Lucid (LCID) stock price prediction for 2025, suggesting it could reach $15. Despite significant backing from BlackRock and Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund, the author raises concerns about Lucid's ability to compete with Tesla and Ford, especially given its high-priced EVs during a period of elevated inflation. The analysis highlights that even with company-funded credits, Lucid's vehicles remain expensive compared to competitors who benefit from government incentives.

title: Tesla may get into the lithium-mining business, and these stocks are cratering
summary: Tesla is reportedly exploring entering the lithium-mining business, a move that is already negatively impacting the stock prices of established lithium producers. Shares of Albemarle Corp. and Livent Corp. have seen significant declines recently, suggesting investor concern about increased competition or a disruption in the supply chain dynamics.

title: Sunrun Solar Installations Topple Record Set by Tesla’s SolarCity
summary: Sunrun Inc., the leading US residential solar company, has surpassed a seven-year industry record for solar panel deployments. In the fourth quarter, Sunrun deployed 275.4 megawatts of panels, exceeding the previous record of 272 megawatts set by SolarCity in late 2015 before its acquisition by Tesla. While residential solar installations have seen a surge due to incentives and rising energy costs, Sunrun anticipates a slowdown in growth for 2023 to 10% to 15% due to headwinds like higher interest rates and subsidy cuts in California.

title: Gentherm Reports 2022 Fourth Quarter and Full Year Results; Provides Strategic Update with 2026 Outlook
summary: Gentherm announced strong financial results for the fourth quarter and full year 2022, achieving record annual revenue of $1.2 billion and securing $1.8 billion in new automotive business awards. Despite macroeconomic challenges, the company posted product revenue increases of 38.3% for Q4 and 15.2% for the full year, driven by strategic acquisitions and growth in automotive products. Gentherm also provided a strategic update with a 2026 outlook, projecting revenues between $2.05 billion and $2.35 billion and high-teens Adjusted EBITDA margin, while outlining key imperatives for continued growth and technological leadership in both automotive and medical industries.

title: Tesla to Open HQ in Palo Alto Less than Two Years After Loudly Ditching California
summary: Tesla is establishing a new global engineering headquarters in Palo Alto, taking over the former HP headquarters, despite having moved its official HQ to Austin, Texas, in 2021. CEO Elon Musk announced this alongside California Gov. Gavin Newsom, highlighting California's continued strength in engineering talent and manufacturing. The move signals a renewed investment in the state, where Tesla maintains significant operations, including its Fremont manufacturing plant and a large workforce in the Bay Area.

title: The EV transition needs lithium. Can a decommissioned mine help provide it?
summary: Albemarle Corporation is aiming to reopen the decommissioned Kings Mountain mine in North Carolina to extract lithium, a critical component for the burgeoning EV battery industry. This initiative faces challenges including a lengthy permitting process, environmental concerns from local communities, and the significant time and investment required for mining projects. Despite these hurdles, the demand for lithium is surging, driven by the global EV transition and US policies promoting domestic battery production, making the mine a potentially vital domestic resource.

title: 2023 Clean200
summary: The 2023 Clean200 report identifies the top 200 public companies globally leading in sustainable practices and clean energy transition. These companies, from 35 countries, demonstrate that prioritizing sustainability can lead to strong financial performance, outperforming broad market and fossil fuel indices. The report highlights the growing importance of clean energy investment and the need for significant financial shifts to meet climate goals.

title: Vertical Integration is all the Rage in the EV Industry, is Musk the New Ford?
summary: The EV industry is seeing a renewed trend towards vertical integration, reminiscent of Henry Ford’s strategy, as companies like Tesla, General Motors, and Ford seek to secure their supply chains for critical battery materials like lithium. Tesla, led by Elon Musk, is exploring various options for lithium sourcing, including potential acquisitions and direct mining, while GM and Ford have entered into significant investment and offtake agreements with lithium producers. This strategic shift aims to ensure reliable material availability and potentially reduce costs amidst growing demand for electric vehicles.

title: S&P Global Mobility: February 2023 US auto sales holding the line
summary: S&P Global Mobility projects a mild advance in US light vehicle sales for February 2023, expecting a 5% year-over-year growth to 1.1 million units. Despite a projected sales pace of 14.4 million units (SAAR), a decline from the prior month, fleet sales and improving production levels are contributing factors. The forecast for the full calendar year 2023 remains at 14.8 million units, a 7% increase from 2022, with battery-electric vehicle sales expected to reach record levels, estimated at 8.0% BEV share for February.

title: TikTok, Angry Birds, Zoom to Appear on Touchscreen in Newest Mercedes
summary: Mercedes-Benz's 2024 E-Class will feature advanced in-car technology, allowing users to access apps like TikTok, Angry Birds, and Zoom directly from a new, larger touchscreen that extends to the passenger side. This system operates independently of smartphones and includes a selfie camera for video conferences when parked. The vehicle also incorporates a driver monitoring system that dims the passenger screen if the driver is distracted.

title: Tesla to open global engineering HQ in Palo Alto
summary: Tesla is establishing its global engineering and AI headquarters in Palo Alto at the former Hewlett Packard Enterprise campus on Hanover Street and Page Mill Road. This move marks a "poetic transition" from HP, a Silicon Valley pioneer, with CEO Elon Musk emphasizing the importance of California to the company's future despite his previous criticisms of the state. California Governor Gavin Newsom highlighted the state's leading role in zero-emission vehicle manufacturing and its contributions to sustainable energy.

title: 7 Super Speculative Stocks That Could Make You Very Rich
summary: This article identifies seven highly speculative stocks—Materion (MTRN), Magnite (MGNI), Lithium America (LAC), Sunrun (SUN), Allegro MicroSystems (ALGM), iCAD (ICAD), and Stem (STEM)—that have the potential for significant long-term gains. The author argues that despite their high risk, these stocks are well-positioned to capitalize on strong, ongoing market trends such as the growth of EVs, renewable energy, and digital advertising, especially as institutional investors increasingly re-enter growth sectors.

title: Lucid tumbles on dour forecast as Tesla's price war takes toll
summary: Lucid Group Inc's shares slumped over 14% after the electric vehicle maker's 2023 production targets fell below analyst estimates, largely due to waning demand and a price war initiated by Tesla Inc. Aggressive price cuts by market leaders like Tesla and Ford are making it difficult for loss-making rivals such as Lucid and Rivian to gain market share. BofA Global Research downgraded Lucid, predicting it may not break even until 2027.

title: Top Stories This Week: Gold M&A Spree Continues, Tesla Mulls Bid for Lithium Company
summary: This article covers significant M&A activities in the gold and lithium sectors. Hecla Mining is set to acquire Yukon-focused gold explorer ATAC Resources, offering C$0.14 per share, while Tesla is reportedly considering an acquisition in the lithium space, with Sigma Lithium being a potential target. Gold prices saw fluctuations following the US Federal Open Market Committee meeting minutes, indicating continued concern over inflation, while Sigma Lithium's stock rose on acquisition rumors.

title: POSCO Chemical Rebrands as POSCO Future M
summary: POSCO Chemical Co. is rebranding to POSCO Future M Co. to emphasize its mission of becoming a green company focused on sustainable industrial material production. This change, approved by the board and to be finalized by shareholders on March 20, expands the company's focus on environmental safety, renewable energy, and battery technology, particularly materials for EV lithium-ion cells. The company, originally a refractories and burnt lime producer, is making a significant shift towards secondary battery components.

title: Nio plans new 40 GWh battery plant in Hefei, report says
summary: Nio reportedly plans to build its first battery plant in Hefei, China, with an annual capacity of 40 GWh, capable of powering about 400,000 long-range EVs. This move aims to reduce reliance on suppliers like CATL, with Nio already having a 400-person battery research team. The company plans to produce its own battery pack by 2024, supporting 800V fast charging, and will also produce LMFP batteries for its sub-brand ALPS.

title: EVs will still get a break when DTE switches to new electric rates in March: What to know
summary: DTE Energy is implementing new residential electric rates in March, designed to encourage customers to reduce usage during peak hours (3-7 p.m. weekdays). Despite these changes, DTE will maintain its discounted rates for electric vehicle charging, which are intended to promote overnight charging. The utility aims to manage demand and optimize its existing generating capacity, preparing for increased electric loads from EVs and other electrified products.

title: From McDonald’s to Ralph Lauren, U.S. Companies Are Planning China Expansions
summary: Despite lingering economic and geopolitical concerns, major American companies like McDonald’s and Starbucks are planning significant expansions in China. These firms still view China's vast consumer market as a promising long-term investment, anticipating a strong post-pandemic economic rebound.

title: S&P Global Mobility Recognizes General Motors as Top Manufacturer; Tesla as Top Make in its 27th Annual Automotive Loyalty Awards
summary: S&P Global Mobility announced the winners of its 27th annual Automotive Loyalty Awards, with General Motors recognized as the 'Overall Loyalty to Manufacturer' for the eighth consecutive year and Tesla winning 'Overall Loyalty to Make'. Tesla also received awards for 'Highest Conquest Percentage' and 'Alternative Powertrain Loyalty to Make', and 'Ethnic Market Loyalty to Make', highlighting its strong retention among diverse consumers. Mercedes-Benz and Subaru also secured wins for loyalty improvement and dealer loyalty respectively, indicating shifting dynamics in the automotive market affected by inventory shortages and evolving consumer preferences.

title: Tesla takes customer loyalty title from Ford, ending Dearborn automaker's 12-year streak
summary: Tesla has unseated Ford for "Overall Loyalty to Make" after Ford held the title for 12 years, according to S&P Global Mobility's annual Automotive Loyalty Awards. General Motors, meanwhile, maintained its "Overall Loyalty to Manufacturer" title for the eighth consecutive year. The shift reflects Tesla's strong electric vehicle sales and active consumer base, while overall industry loyalty has declined due to supply chain issues.

title: SpaceX could soon be getting its steel from South Korea
summary: South Korean company SeAh Changwon Integrated Special Steel Co. is reportedly in talks to supply SpaceX with alloy steel for its rockets and satellites. If the deal is secured, SeAh would be the first Korean company to become a SpaceX supplier, with the contract potentially lasting 3 to 5 years. This development follows previous speculation about SpaceX's steel sources, including Steel Dynamics Inc. (SDI), which had denied any supply relationship with Elon Musk's companies.

title: Who is Xpeng and why should you be excited about its expansion into Europe?
summary: Xpeng, an electric vehicle startup founded in 2014 and backed by major tech firms, is expanding its presence in Europe, starting with Norway, Sweden, Denmark, and the Netherlands. The company aims to compete with established brands by offering technologically advanced, feature-rich models at competitive prices, focusing on digital marketing and showroom experiences. Xpeng has introduced two refreshed models, the G9 SUV and P7 saloon, for the European market.

title: Cisco works with Mercedes-Benz to create mobile office
summary: Cisco is partnering with Mercedes-Benz to integrate its Webex conferencing tools directly into the dashboards of the 2024 E-Class vehicles, transforming cars into mobile offices. While driving, users are limited to audio, but when parked, they can access all Webex features including video meetings, content sharing, and automatic transcription. This move allows Cisco to expand its collaboration tools into new markets, following a boost in video-conferencing platforms during the pandemic.

title: Arbe Robotics: Buy The Rumour (NASDAQ:ARBE)
summary: Arbe Robotics, a manufacturer of 4D radar-on-a-chip technology, is rumored to be on the verge of landing a substantial high-profile customer, potentially Tesla. Despite a recent stock drop, the company's unique market-leading product, strong funding, and manufacturing partnerships position it for high-volume demand. The latest earnings call featured guest speakers from major tier 1 OEMs, indicating strong industry interest.

title: XLY Top Holdings List & Exposure (Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR Fund)
summary: This article details the top holdings and exposure breakdown for the Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLY). It lists the top 25 stock holdings by weight, including Amazon.com and Tesla, which together comprise a significant portion of the fund. The report also outlines XLY's major geographic, currency, sector, industry, and sub-industry exposures.

title: As Tesla prepares a Mexico plant, a look at auto plants in Mexico
summary: Mexico is a major North American manufacturing hub for automakers, and is strategically positioning itself for the electric vehicle (EV) wave. With Tesla Inc recently announcing plans for a plant in Mexico, the article provides a comprehensive list of major automakers and their existing manufacturing facilities in the country, highlighting the vehicles produced at each location. This underscores Mexico's crucial role in the automotive supply chain and its growing importance in EV production.

title: Tesla CEO Musk to unveil Part 3 of Master Plan at Investor Day
summary: Tesla CEO Elon Musk is set to unveil "Part 3 of his Master Plan" at an Investor Day event, following his previous plans from 2006 and 2016. This article outlines the key points and progress of the first two master plans, which included building sports cars and affordable EVs, providing zero-emission power options, expanding across vehicle segments, integrating solar roofs and battery storage, and developing self-driving capabilities and robotaxis. The previous plans highlighted Tesla's efforts in vehicle development, sustainable energy, and autonomous driving, with some targets met and others facing delays or regulatory scrutiny.

title: Aehr Comments on Recent Tesla Statements on Silicon Carbide and Reiterates its Annual Guidance
summary: Aehr Test Systems clarified recent statements made by Tesla regarding a 75% reduction in silicon carbide for new drive units, asserting that this reduction applies only to a new, lower-cost platform still under development and will not significantly impact the overall silicon carbide wafer market. Aehr believes the shift to larger, more integrated die in custom modules for new platforms will increase the need for their wafer-level test and burn-in solutions, affirming their fiscal year 2023 revenue guidance of $60 million to $70 million.

title: On Semi Has a Tesla Problem. That’s Just One Reason the Stock Is Falling.
summary: On Semiconductor (On Semi) stock experienced a decline after Tesla announced cost-saving measures for semiconductors. This development suggests a potential reduction in revenue for On Semi from its dealings with Tesla. The article indicates this is one likely reason for the stock's fall.

title: Tesla union labor battle shows need for church’s leadership and teaching voice
summary: A labor dispute at Tesla's Buffalo plant, where employees allege retaliatory firings for unionizing efforts, highlights the need for the Catholic Church to reassert its social teaching on labor rights. Despite general public approval for unions, membership rates are historically low nationwide. The article argues that the Catholic Church, with its rich history of advocating for workers' rights, has a crucial role to play in guiding the modern labor movement.

title: News | Tesla To Bring Lithium Processing Plant Online in South Texas Later This Year
summary: Tesla Inc. plans to open a lithium processing plant near Corpus Christi, Texas, later this year to meet the increasing demand for electric vehicle battery materials. The plant will focus on refining rather than mining lithium, with an anticipated annual capacity of 50 gigawatt hours, enough to power up to 1 million electric vehicles. This strategic move aims to secure Tesla's supply chain given the significant rise in lithium costs and global demand.

title: Tesla's plan to slash silicon carbide use sends some chipmakers' shares down
summary: Tesla announced plans to reduce its use of silicon carbide transistors by 75% in its next-generation powertrains to cut costs, causing shares of chipmakers ON Semiconductor, ST Microelectronics, and Wolfspeed to fall. While analysts from Bank of America and New Street Research acknowledge the potential impact, they also suggest that increased EV adoption from cheaper chips could offset content losses for vendors, and that the new hybrid architecture may be limited to lower-cost models. The announcement also affected MP Materials, as Tesla aims to build motors without rare earth metals.

title: Elon Musk's 'Master Plan' for Tesla fails to charge up investors
summary: Tesla's shares dropped about 7% after Elon Musk's "Master Plan 3" presentation failed to excite investors, who were anticipating an announcement about a more affordable electric vehicle. While the presentation detailed plans to halve assembly costs and invest in a new Mexican plant, it lacked specific timelines and new product revelations. Analysts noted the event was disjointed and more aspirational than a concrete roadmap, also causing semiconductor makers' stocks to fall due to Tesla's plans to reduce silicon carbide use.

title: Tesla's plan to slash its use of rare-earth metals in future vehicle production sends semiconductor stocks tumbling
summary: Tesla announced plans to significantly reduce its use of silicon carbide in its next-generation powertrains, causing semiconductor stocks like Wolfspeed, ON Semiconductor, and STMicroelectronics to fall. The company aims to use 75% less silicon carbide to enable more efficient scaling of vehicle production. Analysts, however, noted Tesla's history of delayed timelines and suggested that reduced silicon carbide costs might ultimately boost EV adoption, benefiting semiconductor manufacturers in the long run.

title: Honda CEO touts EVs, but combustion engines could last until 2040
summary: Honda CEO Toshihiro Mibe stated that while the company is rapidly accelerating its transition to electric vehicles (EVs), combustion engines could remain in use until 2040 and potentially beyond. Honda is investing at least $40 billion through 2030 to push hybrid and fully electric vehicles to 40% of sales by the decade's end, and is also exploring technologies like e-fuels and advanced batteries. Mibe highlighted the importance of addressing charging infrastructure limitations and separating personal feelings from business needs regarding the shift to EVs.

title: Tesla moves to cut SiC use in electric vehicles shakes market ...
summary: Tesla's announcement that its new powertrain for lower-cost EVs will use 75% less silicon carbide (SiC) power MOSFETs has sent shockwaves through the SiC market. While analysts initially expressed concern, some companies like Aehr Test Systems and STMicroelectronics believe this move aligns with existing industry trends toward miniaturization and higher volume in lower-cost vehicle segments, potentially increasing the overall addressable market for SiC rather than reducing it. The change is expected for new, lower-cost models from 2025 and will not affect current high-performance Tesla models.

title: Empresas de sector automotriz en NL buscan ser proveedoras de Tesla
summary: Companies in Nuevo León's automotive sector, such as Vitro, Nemak, and Ternium, are preparing to become suppliers for Tesla's new gigafactory in the region. Vitro aims to provide automotive glass, Nemak is ready to supply structural and other components, and Ternium looks to expand its steel supply to Tesla from its Pesquería plant. These firms emphasize their readiness to meet Tesla's demands and highlight the importance of competitive energy costs for industrial processes.

title: South America looks at creating “lithium OPEC”
summary: Argentina, Chile, Bolivia, and Brazil are exploring the formation of a "lithium OPEC" to boost South America's lithium processing capacity and engage more deeply in the battery and electric vehicle manufacturing sectors. This initiative aims to coordinate production, pricing, and best practices, drawing inspiration from the oil cartel. The region, particularly the lithium triangle countries, holds a significant portion of the world's lithium resources, and this cooperation seeks to maximize the benefits of increasing demand and prices for the critical mineral.

title: Bank of America sees lithium surplus in 2023 as demand eases
summary: Bank of America Securities forecasts a lithium surplus in 2023 due to increased supply volumes set to outpace slowing demand for the metal. Matty Zhao, head of Asia Pacific basic materials, expects China's electric vehicle demand growth to decelerate significantly this year. Despite global EV demand still growing, the rapid increase in lithium supply from new and expanding mines, incentivized by previously elevated prices, is largely contributing to the anticipated surplus and a projected drop in lithium prices.

title: Silver mines will likely be bought by automakers like Tesla, silver to $125 per ounce - Keith Neumeyer
summary: Keith Neumeyer predicts that automakers like Tesla will likely acquire silver mines due to the increasing demand for silver in electric vehicles. He also forecasts a significant rise in silver prices, potentially reaching $125 per ounce. This highlights the growing importance of precious metals in the automotive and technology sectors.

title: Tesla Inc. stock falls Monday, underperforms market
summary: Tesla Inc. (TSLA) shares fell 2.01% on Monday, closing at $193.81, and underperformed a mixed broader market where the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose and the NASDAQ Composite Index declined. The stock is significantly down from its 52-week high, and trading volume was below its 50-day average.

title: Consider Mexico ETF FLMX as U.S. Industries Look South
summary: As the U.S. market faces potential interest rate hikes and stress, investors are looking to international equities, with Mexico emerging as an attractive option. The Franklin FTSE Mexico ETF (FLMX) is positioned to benefit from U.S. industries moving operations south, exemplified by Tesla's new gigafactory and Mattel's expanded manufacturing. Proximity to the U.S., resilient demand, and domestic consumption contribute to Mexico's bright outlook, making FLMX an appealing investment with strong year-to-date performance.

title: Tesla's next generation smaller car to operate mostly autonomously-Musk
summary: Elon Musk announced that Tesla's next-generation small car would primarily operate autonomously, reiterating a 2020 promise. He also stated there's a clear path to building a car that costs half as much as the Model 3, though no timeline or specific models were disclosed. In 2020, Musk had expressed confidence that Tesla would produce a fully autonomous $25,000 electric car within about three years.

title: News | Salesforce Executive Calls In-Office Work Critical to Tech Giant’s Profitability
summary: Salesforce's Chief Operating Officer Brian Millham states that in-person work is crucial for the company's future growth and profitability, reversing a previous stance on remote work. He noted a drop in productivity among remote employees during the pandemic. The company's shift back to a hybrid model, requiring sales and service employees in the office four days a week, appears to be positively impacting revenue growth.

title: U.S. opens Tesla probe into reports steering wheels fell off
summary: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) has initiated a preliminary investigation into 120,000 Tesla Model Y vehicles following two reports of steering wheels detaching while driving. Both incidents involved 2023 model year vehicles with low mileage, where the steering wheels completely detached due to missing retaining bolts. The NHTSA is assessing the scope, frequency, and manufacturing processes related to this issue, which could lead to a recall.

title: Cathie Wood Is Buying Tesla Again
summary: High-profile fund manager Cathie Wood of ARK Invest is once again buying shares of Tesla. Wood has historically been a strong supporter of Tesla, and this latest purchase indicates her strategy of "buying the dip" in the stock.

title: Tesla's next phase: Manufacturing powerhouse
summary: Tesla aims to become a manufacturing powerhouse by significantly cutting its production costs. The company is specifically targeting a 50% reduction in manufacturing costs as it enters this next phase of its development, highlighted by a proposed gigafactory in Mexico.

title: Australian lithium miners plunge as prices hit one-year low
summary: Australian lithium miners experienced significant share price drops, with some falling over 6%, as benchmark lithium prices hit a one-year low. This decline is attributed to a decrease in electric vehicle sales in China and competitive discounts from battery giant CATL. Analysts anticipate continued price volatility and a decline through 2024 due to supply growth outstripping demand.

title: Four Ways to Cluster based on Correlation
summary: This article explores four methods to cluster 20 companies based on the correlation of their stock prices. It aims to see if these methods can accurately classify companies into categories like tech or oil and gas, as an alternative to clustering based on raw stock prices. The objective is to validate whether correlation-based clustering reveals meaningful industry groupings.

title: Moore recommits Maryland to highest national climate goals for electric vehicle sales
summary: Governor Wes Moore announced Maryland's recommitment to the Advanced Clean Cars II Standards, aiming for all new cars and trucks sold in the state to be electric vehicles by 2035. This policy, which was paused by the previous administration, is expected to significantly reduce greenhouse gas emissions and provide health benefits. The move is also intended to position Maryland as a leader in climate policy and create economic opportunities within the state, though it faces criticism from House Republicans regarding cost and reliance on California's standards.

title: Opinion: Layoffs like Meta’s show corporate social responsibility is just flowery words and big talk
summary: The article argues that many profitable companies, despite publicly espousing strong corporate social responsibility (CSR) goals and social purposes, are engaging in mass layoffs, which calls into question the sincerity of their CSR claims. Author Paul Klein labels this phenomenon as "corporate social responsibility zero" or "fraud," highlighting examples like Meta, Blackbaud, Tesla, Oracle, Microsoft, and Ford. He contrasts these actions with companies like Apple and Palo Alto Networks, whose CEOs took pay cuts or relinquished salaries to avoid layoffs, despite not building their brands on social impact.

title: China battery giant CATL's $5 bln Swiss listing delayed amid Beijing regulatory concerns - sources
summary: Chinese battery giant CATL's plan to raise at least $5 billion through a Swiss listing has been delayed due to concerns from Beijing regulators regarding the offering's large scale and the company's prior fundraising. Authorities are scrutinizing CATL's use of proceeds and the need for such a significant capital raise after a substantial domestic share placement last year. This delay occurs amid President Xi Jinping's expressed "mixed feelings" about CATL's rapid global expansion and market dominance.

title: “GMC” doesn’t mean what you think it means
summary: The article debunks the popular automotive legend that "GMC" stands for Grabowsky Motor Company. It clarifies that while brothers Max and Morris Grabowsky were early automotive pioneers who founded the Rapid Motor Vehicle Company, which was eventually acquired by General Motors, the "GMC" brand officially appeared in 1912 standing for General Motors Truck Company, later General Motors Corporation. The author details the Grabowskys' actual contributions to early truck manufacturing and distinguishes them from the brand's official history.

title: Tesla hit with 'right to repair' antitrust class actions
summary: Tesla is facing two proposed antitrust class-action lawsuits alleging that the company uses exclusionary practices to limit competition in the market for its vehicle maintenance and replacement parts. These lawsuits claim that Tesla's design and repair policies force owners to rely solely on Tesla for services, leading to higher costs and longer wait times. The plaintiffs are seeking to dismantle Tesla's alleged repair monopoly and demand that the company make its repair manuals and diagnostic tools available to independent service providers.

title: General Motors’ Deborah Wahl to retire from position as CMO
