summary: Deborah Wahl, General Motors' global chief marketing officer, is retiring from her position after joining the company in 2018. A GM spokesman confirmed her retirement, stating she would transition out by March 31, 2023, and a new CMO would be hired externally. Wahl was credited with advancing Cadillac's brand image and playing a key role in GM's marketing efforts, including their focus on electric vehicles and a recent Super Bowl campaign with Netflix.

title: Tesla owners sue over parts-and-service monopoly
summary: Tesla is facing two class-action lawsuits alleging it has unlawfully monopolized replacement parts and repair services. Owners claim Tesla's policies force them to use company-approved service centers and parts, leading to lengthy delays and inflated prices for repairs. This legal action coincides with a growing push for right-to-repair laws across the United States.

title: Workhorse Group Inc. stock underperforms Thursday when compared to competitors
summary: Workhorse Group Inc. (WKHS) stock fell 1.96% on Thursday, closing at $1.50, despite a strong overall market performance by the NASDAQ Composite and Dow Jones Industrial Average. The stock underperformed compared to competitors like Tesla Inc. and General Motors Co. and was $3.89 short of its 52-week high.

title: Vietnam's VinFast says three sales executives have left EV company
summary: Three senior sales and customer-service executives have left Vietnamese electric-vehicle (EV) start-up VinFast this week. This follows a string of executive changes and a delayed, costly rollout of VinFast's first EVs in California. The company cited "personal reasons" for one departure and "changes in the management model" for the other two, as it navigates a competitive U.S. EV market.

title: For Broad-Based Large-Cap ESG Exposure, See CVLC
summary: The article highlights the Calvert US Large-Cap Core Responsible Index ETF (CVLC) as a significant option for investors seeking broad-based large-cap ESG exposure, particularly focusing on climate change and net-zero goals. It emphasizes CVLC's ability to identify companies taking actual steps towards net-zero objectives, citing examples like Apple, Amazon, and Tesla. The ETF offers a tangible way for investors to align their portfolios with values related to environmental sustainability and responsible investing.

title: The 3 Best Electric Vehicle ETFs to Buy for the Future of Transportation
summary: This article highlights three top electric vehicle ETFs for investors looking to capitalize on the growing EV market. It discusses the Global X Autonomous & Electric Vehicles ETF (DRIV), Amplify Lithium & Battery Technology ETF (BATT), and iShares Self-Driving EV & Tech ETF (IDRV), detailing their holdings, expense ratios, dividends, and performance. The author suggests these ETFs offer a safer and more affordable way to invest in the EV sector compared to individual stocks.

title: Consider CVLC for Broad-Based, Large-Cap ESG Exposure
summary: The Calvert US Large-Cap Core Responsible Index ETF (CVLC) offers investors broad exposure to environmental, social, and governance (ESG) principles, particularly focusing on climate change and net-zero trends. Launched in February by Morgan Stanley Investment Management, CVLC includes companies like Apple, Amazon, and Tesla that are actively pursuing net-zero goals. The ETF helps investors identify companies genuinely committed to ESG, making it a relevant option as net-zero becomes an increasingly important investment criterion.

title: Ford is about to break out big EV losses for the first time
summary: Ford is poised to reveal its financial results by business unit for the first time, separating its traditional internal combustion engine business ("Ford Blue") from its electric vehicle unit ("Model e"). This new reporting structure will provide an unprecedented detailed look into the financial performance of Ford's EV operations, which analysts widely expect to show significant losses. While traditional ICE vehicles are currently seen as the company's "profit engine," the new transparency aims to set a baseline for Ford's transformation into an EV powerhouse, despite anticipated negative gross and EBIT margins for its Model e segment.

title: Sprott Launches Nickel Miners ETF
summary: Sprott Asset Management has launched the Sprott Nickel Miners ETF (Nasdaq: NIKL), which focuses on nickel mining companies crucial for the clean energy transition. Nickel is a vital component in EV batteries, and its demand is expected to increase significantly by 2040. NIKL joins Sprott's existing suite of energy transition-focused ETFs, catering to advisors prioritizing clean energy investments.

title: Sprott Launches Nickel Miners ETF
summary: Sprott Asset Management has launched the Sprott Nickel Miners ETF (Nasdaq: NIKL), focusing on nickel mining companies crucial for the clean energy transition. Nickel is a vital component in EV batteries, with demand projected to increase significantly by 2040. NIKL joins Sprott's existing suite of energy transition-focused ETFs, aiming to capitalize on the growing need for critical minerals.

title: Albemarle to invest $1.3B in South Carolina lithium processing facility
summary: Albemarle will invest $1.3 billion to build a lithium hydroxide processing facility in Chester County, South Carolina, aiming to produce 50,000 metric tons of battery-grade lithium hydroxide annually. This facility is intended to meet the growing demand for lithium-ion batteries and support U.S.-based material sourcing for EV companies, capitalizing on incentives from the Inflation Reduction Act. The project is expected to create 300 jobs with an average annual wage of $93,000 and an additional 1,500 construction jobs, with work slated to begin in late 2024.

title: Tesla Started a China Price War That May Destroy Some Carmakers
summary: Tesla's significant price cuts in China have initiated a price war within the world's largest car market. These discounts, making some Teslas nearly 50% cheaper than in the US and Europe, threaten to drive some competing automakers out of business. This aggressive strategy by Elon Musk's company is expected to lead to profound consolidation in the industry.

title: How Ford plans to turn a profit on EVs in under four years
summary: Ford aims for its Model e EV unit to achieve an 8% EBIT profit margin by the end of 2026, despite projected losses of up to $3 billion in 2023. The strategy involves economies of scale, energy-efficient designs, in-house battery production using cheaper LFP cells, and incremental gains from software and services. However, Wall Street analysts remain skeptical about the ambitious target given current market conditions and competitor projections.

title: Direxion Daily TSLA Bull 2X Shares (TSLL) Stock Price, News, Quote & History
summary: This page provides detailed financial information for Direxion Daily TSLA Bull 2X Shares (TSLL), including its current stock price, historical data, and performance overview. TSLL aims to provide 2X leveraged exposure to Tesla (TSLA) stock. The fund's overview, holdings, and related tickers are also presented.

title: Cathie Wood Just Sold Xpeng (XPEV) Stock
summary: Cathie Wood's Ark Invest has sold over 128,000 shares of Xpeng (XPEV) stock, using a recent rally to lighten her position. Despite a five-day winning streak causing XPEV to be up 2% year-to-date, the stock is still down over 60% in the last 12 months and more than 85% from its all-time high. The sale follows disappointing Q4 earnings, a substantial revenue drop, and a weak outlook on future deliveries and revenue, which makes Wood's decision to sell understandable.

title: China's Nio opens trial for high-speed EV battery swapping stations
summary: Chinese EV maker Nio Inc. has commenced trial operations for its new Power Swap Station 3.0, designed to facilitate faster and more efficient battery swaps in under five minutes. This initiative aims to establish battery swapping as a competitive alternative to rapid-charging technologies, despite skepticism from rivals like Tesla. Nio plans to expand its global network to 2,300 battery swapping stations by year-end, with 900 of the latest versions expected to be operational this year.

title: EV startup Lucid to cut about 18% of its workforce
summary: Electric-vehicle manufacturer Lucid Group Inc. announced plans to reduce its workforce by approximately 18%, impacting about 1,300 employees, as part of a restructuring effort to cut costs. This decision follows a lower-than-expected 2023 production forecast and a significant drop in fourth-quarter orders. The company expects to incur $24 million to $30 million in related charges and aims to complete the restructuring by the end of the second quarter, while also reviewing non-critical spending.

title: Traction Battery Market 2023: leading Countries, Consumption, Drivers and leading companies – Panasonic, Contemporary Amperex Technology Limited (CATL), LG Chem
summary: The global Traction Battery Market was valued at US$34.19 billion in 2023 and is projected to grow at a CAGR of 8.3% through 2029. The report highlights increased demand driven by electric vehicles, government policies supporting EV adoption, and applications in industrial vehicles like forklifts. Key market players include Panasonic, CATL, and LG Chem, with Panasonic planning mass production of new Tesla batteries by early 2024.

title: Canada takes small step forward in critical mineral ambitions with restart of Quebec lithium mine
summary: Sayona Mining Ltd. and Piedmont Lithium Inc. have restarted commercial production of lithium at the North American Lithium project in Quebec, marking a significant step for Canada's critical mineral ambitions. Despite recent price volatility, the project is expected to be profitable as the capital expenditure has already been made. There are plans to ramp up production and potentially construct a chemical plant for refining lithium concentrate, supporting Canada's goal to reduce reliance on countries like China for critical minerals.

title: Quebec lithium mine up and running to supply Tesla, LG Chem
summary: A Quebec lithium mine has restarted operations and is now supplying Tesla and LG Chem, marking a significant milestone for the North American market. The restart was announced by Piedmont CEO Keith Phillips. This development aims to bolster the regional supply chain for critical EV battery materials.

title: Gen Z, Memes, Digital Revolution Kicked to Curb in Fad-ETF Purge
summary: Thematic ETFs focusing on popular trends like Gen Z, memes, and digital revolution are rapidly closing down as rising interest rates negatively impact their performance. Many of these niche funds, which often targeted technology names, had assets under $30 million and negative returns, making them vulnerable to shutdown. This trend indicates a shift away from speculative, high-growth strategies toward more stable investments in the current economic climate.

title: ARKW: Right Approach for Futuristic Internet Investing
summary: The ARK Next Generation Internet ETF (ARKW) is highlighted as a suitable investment for capitalizing on emerging internet trends, despite its age. The article discusses ARKW's positioning in response to the acceleration of digital consumption, with holdings in crypto, fintech, and innovative tech companies like Coinbase, Block, and Tesla. It also points to the potential growth from emerging social media platforms and the expanding online sports betting and iGaming industries as future drivers for the fund.

title: News snapshot: Talon Metals, Steppe Gold, PyroGenesis Canada, Jushi Holdings, Amaroq Minerals, FansUnite Entertainment UPDATE...
summary: This news snapshot provides an update on various companies and market trends. The report also covers a decline in global IPOs, Aurora Cannabis's share jump after repurchasing notes, and oil price increases.

title: GM says it expects some EVs to receive $7,500 US tax credits
summary: General Motors (GM) anticipates that several of its electric vehicles, including the Cadillac Lyriq and upcoming Chevrolet models like the Equinox EV SUV and Blazer EV SUV, will qualify for the full $7,500 U.S. tax credit after new Treasury Department guidance takes effect on April 18. This comes as the U.S. aims to reduce its dependence on China for EV battery supply chains, leading to stricter requirements for critical minerals and battery components. Other automakers like Stellantis and Tesla are also reviewing the impact of these new rules, with some vehicles expecting reduced or eliminated credits.

title: Automotive News Top 150 Dealership Groups (2023)
summary: This article presents the 2023 list of the Top 150 Dealership Groups compiled by Automotive News. It also includes links to related stories covering topics such as tariffs impacting new vehicle prices, dealership buy-sell updates, and manufacturing news.

title: Best-Performing Stocks of Q1 2023
summary: The first quarter of 2023 saw a significant rebound in technology and communication services stocks, with Nvidia, Meta Platforms, and Salesforce among the top performers. This resurgence was driven by factors such as better-than-expected earnings, cost-cutting measures, and growing enthusiasm for artificial intelligence, contrasting sharply with the downturn experienced in 2022. Other sectors like entertainment and media, cryptocurrency, and electric vehicles also saw notable gains.

title: Tesla posts record quarterly deliveries after price cuts
summary: Tesla achieved record quarterly deliveries, a feat largely attributed to recent price cuts. The company's sales figures demonstrate the impact of its pricing strategy on market performance.

title: 2023 Hyundai Ioniq 6 Garners 3 World Car Awards
summary: The 2023 Hyundai Ioniq 6 won three prestigious World Car Awards: World Car of the Year, World Electric Vehicle, and World Car Design of the Year. This marks the second consecutive year Hyundai has swept the awards, following the Ioniq 5's triple win in 2022. The World Car jury, comprised of over 100 automotive journalists, also recognized Kia EV6 GT as World Performance Car and Lucid Air as World Luxury Car.

title: Stellantis unveils Ram EV pickup, to debut late in 2024, at New York auto show
summary: Stellantis (Chrysler's parent company) unveiled its new electric Ram pickup truck at the New York Auto Show, with plans for a late 2024 market debut. The 2025 model year Ram 1500 REV will offer up to 500 miles of range with an optional larger battery and can tow up to 14,000 lbs. Stellantis CEO Carlos Tavares emphasized the truck's superior performance compared to rivals and aims for significant EV sales targets by 2030 amidst increasing demand for electric vehicles.

title: Elon Musk Makes Plea to Mine More Nickel
summary: Tesla CEO Elon Musk has urged for increased nickel mining to meet the growing demand for electric vehicle (EV) batteries, with Indonesia identified as a crucial source. Investors can gain exposure to nickel mining through the Sprott Nickel Miners ETF (NIKL), which offers global diversification and targets companies involved in nickel production, development, and exploration. The ETF focuses on small-cap equities and is positioned to benefit from the anticipated surge in nickel demand due to its critical role in EV and clean energy storage.

title: YieldMax Announces Monthly Distributions on TSLY and OARK ETFs
summary: YieldMax has announced its monthly distributions for April 2023 for its TSLY and OARK Option Income Strategy ETFs. TSLY will distribute $0.8286 per share with a current yield of 61.41%, while OARK will distribute $0.5103 per share with a current yield of 37.64%. The article also provides historical distribution data and important disclaimers regarding investment risks and yield variability.

title: Elon Musk Makes Case to Mine More Nickel
summary: Elon Musk has urged increased nickel mining due to rising demand for electric vehicles (EVs), as nickel is a crucial component in EV batteries. Tesla is reportedly interested in nickel mining opportunities in Indonesia, the world's largest nickel producer. Investors can gain exposure to nickel mining through the Sprott Nickel Miners ETF (NIKL), which provides diversified access to global nickel miners.

title: Niu MQi+ Sport - Cycling News | electric bike reviews, buying advice and news - ebiketips
summary: The Niu MQi+ Sport e-moped is reviewed, highlighting its rapid acceleration, comfortable ride, and robust features suitable for urban commuting. Despite its 72kg weight, it offers a nippy 28mph top speed, a 2kWh battery providing a real-world range of about 38 miles, and excellent lighting for visibility. The review concludes that it's a leading performer in its category, with the brand's global presence ensuring good service and parts availability, though extra costs for registration and dealer fees should be considered.

title: TMW: the integral and beyond
summary: TMW, founded in 1949, is a Japanese specialist in plastic-injection molds for automobile parts, serving major vehicle manufacturers globally. The company emphasizes partnerships and maintains a global network to stay competitive. TMW is adapting to the automotive industry's shift towards EVs and FCVs, anticipating changes in design complexity rather than a fundamental alteration of its core business.

title: American Airlines, Bed Bath & Beyond, Tesla and other stocks on the move
summary: This article highlights several U.S. stocks that experienced significant movement on a particular Wednesday. It specifically mentions American Airlines, Bed Bath & Beyond, and Tesla as key movers. Additionally, National CineMedia's stock is noted for nearly tripling despite the company declaring bankruptcy.

title: Bloomberg plans to integrate GPT-style A.I. into its terminal
summary: Bloomberg LP is developing its own AI model, Bloomberg GPT, similar to OpenAI's GPT, specifically trained on financial data. This AI will be integrated into Bloomberg's terminal software to automate financial tasks, such as answering company-specific questions, assessing market sentiment from headlines, and generating financial headlines. The initiative highlights a trend of specialized AI development beyond general models, leveraging proprietary financial datasets to enhance accuracy for industry-specific applications.

title: BHP wins over shareholders of Australia's Oz Minerals in $6.4 billion deal
summary: Global miner BHP Group has secured shareholder approval for its A$9.6 billion ($6.4 billion) takeover of Australian copper producer Oz Minerals. This acquisition, which received 98.33% shareholder votes in favor, is one of the largest recent deals in the mining sector. It will significantly boost BHP's copper assets, including Oz Minerals' Carapateena mine, and enhance its nickel prospects through the West Musgrave project, a key supplier to companies like Tesla.

title: Lifecycle Cost: BEV vs. ICEV — Pickup Truck Edition
summary: This article analyzes the full lifecycle costs of Battery Electric Vehicles (BEVs) versus Internal Combustion Engine Vehicles (ICEVs) in the pickup truck segment, expanding on a previous analysis for cars. The author, a management consultant, developed a modeling program to compare various truck models, considering factors like purchase price, longevity, mileage, towing habits, fuel costs, and repair expenses. The findings suggest that BEV trucks offer significantly lower total ownership costs, primarily due to fuel and maintenance savings, especially over longer lifespans and with increased towing.

title: Hesai unveils ultra-thin LiDAR ET25 that can be placed behind windshield
summary: Hesai has launched the ET25, an ultra-thin LiDAR designed for in-cockpit placement behind a vehicle's front windshield. This innovation, created in partnership with Fuyao, aims to improve vehicle aesthetics by eliminating roof-mounted LiDARs and addresses signal attenuation issues with a specially designed windshield. The ET25 boasts powerful performance, including a 250-meter detection range (225m behind glass) and high resolution, while maintaining a compact and quiet operation suitable for luxury electric vehicles.

title: Isida Tushe Joins Microvast as General Counsel
summary: Microvast Holdings, Inc. has announced the appointment of Isida Tushe as General Counsel and Corporate Secretary. Tushe brings extensive domestic and international experience from the power generation and renewables sectors, having previously served in legal leadership roles at a publicly traded technology company focused on EV charging and an equity-owned developer of hydroelectric energy. Her expertise in traditional and alternative energy will be valuable as Microvast continues to innovate in lithium-ion battery solutions for decarbonization.

title: Rich matches boost employees' savings, support recruiting
summary: The article discusses how generous employer matching contributions to retirement plans, exemplified by Abbott Laboratories, help boost employees' savings for retirement and serve as a recruiting tool. It also includes brief summaries of other featured stories related to private credit, endowments, private equity, and pension funds.

title: China’s Xpeng takes cues from Tesla for its new EV platform
summary: Chinese EV maker Xpeng has unveiled its Smart Electric Platform Architecture 2.0 (SEPA 2.0), which closely mirrors Tesla's digital vehicle architecture, featuring large single-piece underbody castings and integrated battery packs. The first model using this platform, the G6 compact SUV, is similar in design to Tesla's Model Y but priced significantly lower. This move is expected to help Xpeng and other automakers restore margins amidst ongoing price wars and streamline production processes, much like Tesla's "unboxed assembly process."

title: Hertz and Mayor Dickens Launch "Hertz Electrifies Atlanta"
summary: Hertz and Atlanta Mayor Andre Dickens have launched "Hertz Electrifies Atlanta," a public-private partnership aimed at increasing electric vehicle (EV) adoption and expanding EV infrastructure and education in the city. The initiative involves bringing up to an additional 4,000 rental EVs to Atlanta, installing public fast chargers, and partnering with Atlanta College and Career Academy to offer EV education and job opportunities. This effort aligns with Atlanta's goal to achieve 100% clean energy by 2035 and supports the Mayor's "Year of the Youth" initiative.

title: Chevron, Exxon pursue cleaner gasoline as alternative to EVs
summary: Chevron and Exxon Mobil are developing and testing renewable gasoline blends that they claim can significantly reduce emissions from conventional vehicles, offering an alternative to electric vehicles. These cleaner fuels, derived from non-fossil feedstocks like soybeans, could extend the lifespan of the gasoline market, but their commercial viability depends on government support and incentives to reach affordable pricing and scale. The initiative comes as the U.S. administration proposes new pollution standards that favor EVs, highlighting the oil majors' efforts to innovate within the evolving energy landscape.

title: Musk counts on full self-driving as next Tesla profit driver
summary: Elon Musk is betting on full self-driving (FSD) technology and other innovations to drive Tesla's future profitability and justify its high valuation, despite recent price cuts impacting margins. Although Tesla's stock has dropped, its valuation remains significantly higher than traditional automakers. Critics express skepticism regarding the timeline and current capabilities of FSD, while some analysts and investors remain confident in Musk's vision, with Ark Invest predicting a significant rise in Tesla's shares by 2027 based on robotaxi plans.

title: Chinese EV dominance hastens end of gasoline engine era
summary: The Auto Shanghai show this year highlighted the dominance of Chinese EV brands in their home market, signaling the end of the internal combustion engine era in China. Supported by government policies and consumer interest, these companies are now looking to expand overseas. Analysts suggest Chinese EVs can compete with traditional automakers in performance and quality, while new technologies like sodium-ion batteries are driving down costs.

title: Tesla drops starting price of Model Y below the cost of the average U.S. vehicle
summary: Tesla has lowered the starting price of its Model Y SUV, making it $759 less expensive than the average new car or truck in the U.S. This move follows previous price cuts on other Tesla models and comes amid slowing sales, despite a general rise in electric vehicle sales and increasing consumer interest in EVs. While Tesla's U.S. market share has declined from its peak, its sales still increased by 40% last year.

title: Tesla broke U.S. labor law by silencing workers, official rules
summary: A U.S. labor board official ruled that Tesla supervisors at a Florida service center violated U.S. labor law by instructing employees not to discuss pay or working conditions. The ruling stems from complaints that new hires were paid more than existing workers. This decision adds to a series of legal setbacks for Tesla regarding labor practices, including prior rulings involving CEO Elon Musk's tweets and banning union t-shirts.

title: Elon Musk Threatens to Sue Microsoft Over Alleged Illegal Data Usage
summary: Elon Musk is threatening to sue Microsoft, alleging unauthorized use of Twitter's data for training its AI models. This comes after Microsoft stopped supporting Twitter on one of its advertising platforms due to Musk's high asking price for API access. Musk claims Microsoft "trained illegally using Twitter data" and sees this as an opportune moment for legal action, especially given Microsoft's $10 billion investment in OpenAI and the integration of GPT-4 into Bing.

title: Constellium to provide closed-loop recycling for the all-new Megane E-TECH Electric
summary: Constellium SE has partnered with Renault Group to implement a closed-loop recycling process for the all-new Megane E-TECH Electric, ensuring manufacturing scrap is recycled back into production without loss of material properties. This collaboration utilizes Constellium's advanced aluminium Auto Body Sheet products and supports both companies' sustainability targets and the decarbonization of the automotive industry. Constellium is also expanding its recycling capacity with a significant investment in a new recycling center in France.

title: Here’s an Early Glimpse of the Lucid Gravity SUV
summary: Lucid Motors is preparing its second EV model, the Gravity SUV, for a 2024 production debut, with initial testing underway in the US. The Gravity SUV will share many underpinnings with the Lucid Air sedan and is expected to offer similar performance specs, though its longer range capabilities may be challenging due to its larger size. Lucid aims for the Gravity to seat seven adults comfortably, have sports car-like dynamics, and offer the greatest electric range among SUVs, capitalizing on market demand for SUVs amidst stagnating Air sedan sales.

title: REE Automotive Unveils P7-C Chassis Cab, a New Addition to
summary: REE Automotive has unveiled its new P7-C Chassis Cab, a Class 4 electric vehicle designed for the fast-growing and incentivized market, on track for Q4 2023 deliveries. This addition expands REE's P7 electric truck lineup, which also includes the P7-B box truck and P7-S stripped chassis, now available for order and built for urban environments. The company is also growing its U.S. authorized dealer network with the addition of Monarch Truck Center, The Truck Shop, and RY-DEN Truck Center, with certification progress underway for the P7 electric lineup.

title: Ryder Introduces Electric Vehicle Service
summary: Ryder System Inc. has launched RyderElectric+, a new electric vehicle program offering comprehensive solutions for fleets, including electrification consulting, vehicle leasing, charging, telematics, and maintenance services. The service, initially focusing on light-duty electric vans, aims to simplify the integration and operation of EVs for customers through a single provider and cost. Ryder plans to expand its EV fleet with thousands of new electric vans and continues to partner with various manufacturers.

title: Here’s How Some Unusual ETFs Performed Against the S&P 500
summary: Exotic exchange-traded funds (ETFs) have gained popularity, allowing investors to make unusual bets, such as against CNBC's Jim Cramer's stock picks or tracking congressional stock trades. However, this year, many of these unusual ETFs have underperformed the S&P 500. Popular funds betting against Tesla and Cathie Wood's ARK Innovation fund have particularly struggled.

title: Ford's profit rises on strong truck demand but EV outlook weighs
summary: Ford Motor Co reported strong first-quarter revenue and profit driven by high demand for trucks and SUVs, beating analyst expectations. However, the company's full-year outlook is tempered by anticipated losses from its electric-vehicle unit. CEO Jim Farley emphasized that Ford will not pursue EV sales volume "at any cost" and aims for its Model e unit to be EBIT margin-positive by late 2024.

title: Who says water and electricity don’t mix? For today’s navies, their radars, weapons, and propulsion depend on it
summary: This article features a Q&A with Ed Thaxton of Leonardo DRS Naval Power Systems, discussing the increasing electrification of naval platforms for improved radar capabilities, directed energy weapons, fuel economy, and anti-submarine warfare (ASW). Thaxton highlights the benefits of permanent magnet motor solutions over other electric technologies due to their smaller size, higher efficiency, and the ability to leverage a vast commercial infrastructure investment. He also details Leonardo DRS's involvement in the US Navy's Columbia-class submarine and South Korea's FFX-II destroyer programs, emphasizing the need for greater commitment to all-electric drive in naval applications.
