The list includes prominent players like BP Pulse, ChargePoint, and Tesla, showcasing their diverse approaches to meet the increasing demand for EV charging solutions.

title: The 3 Most Promising Lithium Mining Stocks for the EV Revolution
summary: This article highlights three promising investment opportunities related to the electric vehicle revolution: Albemarle (ALB), Lithium Americas (LAC), and the Global X Lithium & Battery Tech ETF (LIT). It emphasizes the expected surge in lithium demand by 500% to 700% between 2021 and 2030, driven by the booming EV market. The article positions these selections as top contenders in the thriving lithium industry for investors seeking long-term growth.

title: 3 Solid-State Battery Stocks for Electrifying Long-Term Gains
summary: Solid-state batteries are emerging as a significant technological breakthrough, promising improved performance, longer lifespan, and enhanced safety for electric vehicles. The global solid-state battery market is projected to grow from $47.2 billion in 2022 to $177.4 billion by 2033. This article highlights three investment opportunities: Solid Power (SLDP) for its direct involvement in development, the Global X Lithium & Battery Tech ETF (LIT) for diversified exposure, and Nissan (NSANY) due to its ambitious plans for solid-state battery production by 2028.

title: YieldMax™ Launches NVDY, the YieldMax™ NVDA Option Income Strategy ETF
summary: YieldMax™ has launched NVDY, the YieldMax™ NVDA Option Income Strategy ETF, which seeks to generate monthly income through a synthetic covered call strategy on NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA). This actively managed ETF由ZEGA Financial aims to provide substantial monthly income to investors, joining other YieldMax™ ETFs like TSLY, OARK, and APLY, and adheres to a gross expense ratio of 0.99%.

title: George Soros bet on railroads, fitness, gig economy and lightened load on Disney, Amazon, dumped Tesla
summary: Soros Fund Management made new investments in railroad operators CSX Corp. and Norfolk Southern Corp., and the gig economy during the first quarter. The fund also adjusted its holdings in the media sector and divested from electric-vehicle maker Tesla Inc. These railroad investments occurred amidst ongoing labor tensions and increased scrutiny over safety in the industry.

title: [증권상품 풍향계] 달러 파킹형 ETF 연이어 출시 주목
summary: This article highlights the recent trend of financial product releases in the investment market, focusing on the consecutive launch of US SOFR-linked ETFs designed for dollar parking. It also covers new funds investing in domestic blue-chip bonds until maturity and several ETFs related to electric vehicles, including a leverage ETF for Chinese EVs and an ETF investing in the Tesla value chain. These products cater to investors seeking stable returns from dollar investments, higher yields than bank deposits, and opportunities in the growing EV market.

title: Vehicle network startup Ethernovia raises $64 million
summary: Ethernovia Inc., a startup focused on vehicle networks, has successfully raised $64 million in Series A funding. The investment round saw participation from notable investors including Porsche Automobile Holding SE, Qualcomm Ventures, and AMD Ventures. The company aims to develop cutting-edge network architecture, high-performance computing, and high-bandwidth communications for the next generation of software-defined vehicles, addressing the increasing data transmission demands in the automotive industry.

title: Implications and Updates From China’s National People’s Conference
summary: China's annual "Two Sessions" parliamentary meeting revealed strategic policies for economic growth, trade, and technology, targeting a 5% GDP growth in 2023 driven by domestic consumption. The conference highlighted key implications for sectors like EVs, clean energy, cloud computing, biotechnology, and semiconductors, emphasizing self-reliance and technological innovation. Government restructuring and increased focus on digital infrastructure and R&D are expected to significantly impact various industries and investment themes in China.

title: The EV Revolution and Going Public in the US: IPO Edge Q&A with CEO Jake Dingle
summary: Carbon Revolution CEO Jake Dingle discusses how their carbon fiber wheels are revolutionizing the EV market by reducing weight and extending range, attracting significant OEM interest. The company plans to list in North America through a merger with Twin Ridge Acquisition Corp. (NYSE: TRCA) to scale production and meet growing demand for their patented technology. This strategic move aims to leverage US capital markets for further growth and mainstream adoption of their innovative wheel solutions.

title: GM's 2025 EV output could be crimped by battery bottleneck
summary: General Motors' target of producing 1 million electric vehicles in North America by 2025 might be difficult to achieve due to a slow ramp-up of its three US battery plants. According to AutoForecast Solutions, actual EV production could be limited to less than 600,000 at mid-decade, as existing battery capacity and securing raw materials pose significant challenges. Despite GM's assertions of not changing targets and securing raw materials, the staggered startup schedules of its Ultium Cells joint venture plants with LG Energy Solution could constrain output.

title: Views From the Ground: Mexico
summary: This article highlights Mexico's impressive economic performance in 2023, driven by nearshoring, a resilient peso, pension reforms, and an independent central bank. It argues that nearshoring presents a transformative growth engine, potentially surpassing the impact of NAFTA due to companies diversifying supply chains away from China. Despite political and security concerns, the author anticipates continued strong performance for Mexican equities supported by structural tailwinds.

title: Blue Bird is an EV Company that Actually Makes Money
summary: Blue Bird (NASDAQ: BLBD) is the largest manufacturer of school buses in the U.S., holding a 40% market share and leading in EV school bus production. Unlike many EV companies, Blue Bird is profitable and is experiencing significant growth, with a 44% YoY revenue increase in Q2 2023 and substantial backlogs for both EV and conventional buses. Federal incentives, such as the EPA's Clean School Bus Rebate Program, are further driving its growth, helping the company raise its full-year 2023 revenue guidance.

title: 4 Base Metal ETFs to Buy Right Now
summary: The global base metals market is experiencing a significant recovery, driven by increased demand from the automotive and construction industries, especially the electric vehicle sector. This article identifies four key base metal ETFs—VanEck Vectors Steel ETF (SLX), United States Copper Index Fund (CPER), Global X Lithium & Battery Tech ETF (LIT), and iPath Series B Bloomberg Nickel Subindex Total Return (JJN)—as prime investment opportunities to capitalize on this boom. It details how each metal plays a crucial role in current industrial trends and why their respective ETFs are poised for growth.

title: New materials increase EV battery performance and protection
summary: Huntsman Corp. has introduced new polyurethane, carbon nanotube, and epoxy materials designed to enhance EV battery integration, protection, and performance. These materials enable high-performance composite battery structures, quicker production, reduced weight, and improved strength for components like underbody and top cover protection. Additionally, Huntsman has developed solutions for cell-level lightweighting, improved conductivity, and thermal management.

title: Ford offers investors big bets but finds few takers
summary: Ford CEO Jim Farley is struggling to convince investors that the automaker's ambitious electric vehicle (EV) targets for 2026 are achievable, despite outlining plans to compete with BYD and Tesla. Although Farley received praise for his strategy, Ford's shares remain down from their 52-week high, indicating investor skepticism about the company's transformation, especially concerning EV profitability and market focus shifts. The company aims for 10% pretax profit margins by 2026, relying heavily on its commercial vehicle business, Ford Pro, and leveraging combustion vehicles for cash flow well into the next decade.

title: Tradr 2X Long Innovation ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:TARK)
summary: The Tradr 2X Long Innovation ETF (TARK) aims to provide twice the daily return of the ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK) through swap agreements. The ETF, managed by AXS Investments, was launched on May 2, 2022, and currently has $21.15 million in assets under management. TARK's shares have decreased by 18.8% this year, trading at $39.42 as of the article, and underwent a 1-5 reverse split in December 2022.

title: Fort Collins-based Prieto Battery unveils 'game-changer': Battery can charge in 3 minutes
summary: Fort Collins-based Prieto Battery has unveiled a new 3D lithium-ion battery technology that CEO Amy Prieto calls a "game-changer." The nonflammable battery can charge in three minutes, even in extreme temperatures, and has been validated by a third-party lab. While the company aims for the electric vehicle market, initial applications are expected in power tools, with investment from Stanley Ventures.

title: Quanta Computer to invest US$1 billion in Mexico
summary: Quanta Computer Inc is set to invest US$1 billion and create 2,500 jobs in Nuevo Leon, Mexico, expanding its operations in the state. This investment, announced by Nuevo Leon Governor Samuel Garcia, highlights the growing trend of nearshoring and contribute to Mexico's significant increase in foreign direct investment, with Nuevo Leon attracting a substantial portion of this capital. The company is a key supplier for Tesla, which also has a large factory planned in Monterrey.

title: 강남 부자들이 줄섰다는데…年19% 배당에 차익 노리는 이 상품은
summary: 서학개미들이 최근 미국 증시에서 장기채 ETF, 특히 ‘아이셰어스 만기 20년 이상 국채 바이라이트(TLTW)’를 집중 매집하고 있다. 이는 채권 금리 횡보 전망에 따라 매매 차익보다는 연 19%에 달하는 고배당 수익을 노린 투자 전략으로 분석된다. TLTW는 월 지급 배당 상품으로, 현재 금리 변동성이 큰 시기에 투자자들에게 매력적인 상품으로 부상하고 있다.

title: Ford EV Customers To Gain Access to Tesla Superchargers
summary: Ford EV customers will gain access to Tesla's Supercharger network, starting with an adapter in early 2024 for those with CCS ports. Future Ford EVs will integrate the North American Charging Standard (NACS) port directly, eliminating the need for an adapter and providing access to 12,000 Superchargers across the U.S. and Canada. This collaboration significantly expands charging options for Ford EV owners.

title: Ford strikes deal with Tesla to gain access to rival charging stations starting 2024
summary: Ford has struck a deal with Tesla to allow its electric vehicle owners to access over 12,000 Tesla Superchargers in North America starting in early 2024, addressing a significant hurdle to broader EV adoption. This makes Ford the first major automaker to adopt Tesla's proprietary charging standard, with future Ford EVs directly integrating the standard starting in 2025. The collaboration was announced by Ford CEO Jim Farley and Tesla CEO Elon Musk, with Musk emphasizing an open network to support electrification.

title: ICZOOM Group (IZM) Short Interest & Short Float | Updated Dec 2025
summary: This article details the short interest data for ICZOOM Group (IZM) as of December 15, 2025, showing 40,656 shares sold short, a 37.12% decrease from the prior report. The short interest represents 0.50% of the public float with a short interest ratio of 0.8 days to cover. The report includes historical short interest data, frequently asked questions, and comparisons to competitors in the retail industry.

title: China was top market for Tesla Model Y, world's best-selling car in Q1
summary: The Tesla Model Y became the world's best-selling car in the first quarter of 2023, with China being its largest market, accounting for 94,469 units sold out of a global total of 267,171. This success is attributed to price cuts, public incentives for EVs, increased production from new Tesla plants, and easing supply chain issues. The Model Y was the only pure electric car to rank among the top global sellers, surpassing popular models like the Toyota Corolla.

title: For the First Time Ever, Single-Stock ETFs Will Close Soon
summary: Seven single-stock ETFs, primarily offering leveraged or inverse exposure to individual stocks, are set to close in June, marking the first time such products will cease trading. Despite initial high expectations when they launched in July 2022, most single-stock ETFs have struggled, with minimal assets under management for those slated for closure. AXS will close five of its single-stock ETFs tied to companies like Nike, Pfizer, and PayPal, while GraniteShares is closing a Tesla-related fund, and AXS will also close two other leveraged/inverse ETFs targeting China internet and de-SPAC companies.

title: American Express Is Approaching Generative AI Technology with Caution
summary: American Express is cautiously approaching generative AI technology, aiming to integrate it for enhanced personalization, proactivity, and predictive capabilities through partnerships rather than developing its own large language models. This cautious stance aligns with broader industry concerns, highlighted by an open letter from tech leaders urging a pause in "giant AI experiments" to establish safety protocols. Amex Digital Labs, established in 2017, serves as the company's innovation hub for product prototypes and predictive analytics.

title: Tesla doubles discounts on Model 3 cars in US inventory
summary: Tesla has increased discounts on its Model 3 and Model Y vehicles in its U.S. inventory, and offered steeper discounts on Model X and Model S cars. This move aims to attract buyers amidst economic uncertainty, increased competition, and the anticipation of a Model 3 redesign. The company is actively using incentives and considering advertising to boost demand.

title: Nearshoring in Mexico – Where Are the Opportunities?
summary: This article examines the opportunities and risks associated with nearshoring in Mexico, particularly in response to global supply chain disruptions. It highlights Mexico's strategic location, competitive labor costs, and tax incentives as key advantages, while also discussing significant challenges such as corruption, political interference, infrastructure deficiencies, and security concerns. The case of Tesla's manufacturing plant in Nuevo León is used to illustrate the complex interplay of economic and political factors.

title: Billionaire Tom Siebel faces tumult at C3.ai as investor lawsuit, short sellers question metrics
summary: C3.ai CEO Tom Siebel is facing controversy due to an investor lawsuit alleging misrepresentation of a partnership with Baker Hughes ahead of the company's 2020 IPO, and criticism from short sellers regarding aggressive accounting and customer metrics. While C3.ai's stock has surged with AI excitement, it remains significantly below its peak, and concerns persist about unbilled receivables, executive turnover, and the company's method for counting customers. Siebel denies the allegations, defending the company's accounting practices and growth prospects.

title: Tesla Inc. stock rises Monday, outperforms market
summary: Tesla Inc. (TSLA) stock rose 1.70% on Monday to $217.61, outperforming a generally down market. The NASDAQ Composite Index fell 0.09% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average dropped 0.59%. This marked Tesla's seventh consecutive day of gains.

title: Car prices in flux, may decline from overproduction, ‘price wars’
summary: Analysts are predicting a drop in car prices in the coming months due to overproduction by automakers and declining consumer demand. A UBS report suggests a surplus of five million units by year-end will force price cuts and that competition among OEMs, especially in the EV sector, along with Tesla's discounting strategy, will further drive prices down. Despite these reductions, vehicle values remain inflated compared to pre-pandemic levels, suggesting dealerships may still experience favorable conditions.

title: IONIQ 5 set for fully driverless public taxi service
summary: Motional Inc. will launch a fully driverless public taxi service using Hyundai's IONIQ 5 robotaxis with Level 4 self-driving capabilities in Las Vegas later this year. Motional, a joint venture between Hyundai Motor Co. and Aptiv PLC, emphasized that its Level 4 robotaxis are designed to operate safely without a driver, unlike consumer vehicles with advanced driver-assistance systems. The company plans to expand its services in the US before looking to major cities globally, despite facing hurdles like safety concerns and high manufacturing costs.

title: UK-based company to repurpose electric vehicle batteries in shuttered Chippewa Falls frac sand plant
summary: A UK-based company, Collaborative Engineering Services, is set to invest up to $15 million in Chippewa Falls, Wisconsin, to repurpose electric vehicle batteries at a former frac sand processing plant. This initiative aims to give a second life to battery cells from recalled EVs for applications like home solar storage and utility vehicles. The move highlights a trend of former frac sand facilities in western Wisconsin being repurposed following the industry's bust.

title: Invesco S&P 500 Equal Weight Consumer Discretionary ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NYSEARCA:RSPD)
summary: This article provides detailed information on the Invesco S&P 500 Equal Weight Consumer Discretionary ETF (RSPD), including its current price, key statistics, overview, and top holdings. It notes that the ETF primarily invests in consumer discretionary equity, tracking an equal-weighted index of large-cap US Consumer Discretionary stocks. The stock has seen a slight decrease year-to-date and underwent a 3-1 split in July 2023.

title: Sanan, STMicroelectronics to Invest USD3.2 Billion in China EV Chipmaking Project
summary: China's Sanan Optoelectronics and Switzerland's STMicroelectronics are jointly investing $3.2 billion in a new venture to produce silicon carbide (SiC) devices for electric vehicles (EVs) in Chongqing, China. Sanan will hold a 51% stake and build a related $988 million plant for SiC substrates, while ST will contribute its manufacturing technology. The facility is expected to be operational by late 2025 and reach full capacity by 2028, producing 10,000 eight-inch SiC wafers weekly.

title: Microvast EV battery plant in Hopkinsville in doubt after federal agency halts $200M grant
summary: The U.S. Department of Energy halted a $200 million grant to Microvast Holdings Inc., putting the company's planned $504 million EV battery plant in Hopkinsville, Kentucky, in doubt. The decision followed allegations from Republican lawmakers of Microvast's improper ties to China. Kentucky officials have paused $21 million in state incentives, while Microvast's related plant in Clarksville, Tennessee, is proceeding.

title: Tesla Will Open Its Charging Network to G.M.’s Electric Vehicles (Published 2023)
summary: General Motors announced it would adopt Tesla's charging technology for its electric vehicles, following a similar agreement made by Ford. This deal is expected to establish Tesla's charging plugs as the industry standard, providing G.M. owners with access to Tesla’s extensive and reliable charging network. The move could also benefit Tesla by generating revenue from charging non-Tesla vehicles and helping it qualify for federal funding for charging stations.

title: Tradr 2X Short TSLA Daily ETF (TSLQ) Dividend Yield 2026, Date & History
summary: The Tradr 2X Short TSLA Daily ETF (TSLQ) currently offers a high annual dividend yield of 103.85%, translating to $22.38 per share. The ETF pays dividends annually, with the most recent payment of $1.9184 per share distributed on December 26, 2025, after an ex-dividend date of December 22, 2025. Investors can track TSLQ's dividend history and compare its performance against other NASDAQ companies and market averages.

title: Tesla Eyes $3 Billion Boost From Chargers at Rivals’ Expense
summary: Tesla is projected to earn as much as $3 billion by 2030 from deals with Ford and General Motors for their EVs to use its charging network. This move is expected to establish Tesla's 'North American Charging Standard' as the preferred approach in the U.S., pressuring other competitors to adopt it. The increased revenue from charging will boost Tesla's income while rivals like GM and Ford continue to struggle with EV program profitability.

title: Musk says triple the electricity needed for sustainable energy future
summary: Billionaire entrepreneur Elon Musk stated that the U.S. will need to triple its current electricity supply by 2045 to achieve a sustainable energy future, driven by increased electrification and electric vehicle adoption. He highlighted that while EV adoption is progressing rapidly, replacing all fossil-fuel cars will take about 20 years. Musk emphasized that sustainable power generation, stationary batteries, and electric transportation are the three pillars of this future, but stressed that permitting for new infrastructure remains a significant challenge.

title: Blink Charging Unveils Final Design for New Dual-Port EV DC Fast Charger at EVS36
summary: Blink Charging has announced the final design for its new 240kW DC Fast Charger, featuring dual-port NACS and CCS connectors. This innovative charger, incorporating advanced silicon carbide technology, aims to address the growing demand for EV charging infrastructure. Designed for rapid charging, it will support EV adoption internationally and is currently awaiting UL certification.

title: Three takeaways from Tesla CEO Elon Musk's keynote at EEI energy event
summary: During a keynote at an annual energy conference, Tesla CEO Elon Musk discussed the future of sustainable energy, highlighting the exponential increase in energy demand expected by 2035 and the rapid electrification of transportation. He also touched upon Tesla's role with its electric vehicles and energy storage products like Powerwall and Megapack. Additionally, Musk voiced concerns about artificial intelligence's energy consumption and called for greater regulation of the technology.

title: SolarEdge Reveals Advanced Bi-Directional DC EV Charger at Intersolar 2023
summary: SolarEdge Technologies introduced its new Bi-Directional DC EV Charger at Intersolar Europe 2023, designed for efficient, high-powered charging and home energy storage via EV batteries. This innovative charger enables solar-powered Vehicle-to-Home (V2H) and Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) functionalities, utilizing a DC-coupled architecture for direct charging from PV sources and offering capabilities like fast charging up to 24kW and dynamic energy optimization. It is expected to be available in the second half of 2024.

title: Elon Musk slams woke S&P rating after Tesla scores lower than Philip Morris
summary: Elon Musk criticized S&P Global's ESG rating system after Tesla received a significantly lower score (37 out of 100) than Marlboro maker Philip Morris International (84 out of 100). Musk labeled Environmental Social Governance as "the devil," questioning why a company producing cigarettes, which cause millions of deaths annually, would be deemed a more ethical investment than an electric car manufacturer. S&P Global defended its scoring methodology, stating that company scores are comparable within their respective sectors rather than across industries.

title: Get Out Now! 3 EV Stocks That Are Poised to Plunge.
summary: This article warns investors to sell three specific EV stocks: Lucid Group (LCID), Rivian Automotive (RIVN), and Mullen Automotive (MULN). The author details financial struggles, production issues, and cash burn for each company, suggesting they are poised for further decline and may reach penny-stock status. Tesla (TSLA) is highlighted as the industry benchmark, emphasizing the underperformance of these three companies in comparison.

title: SK파워텍, 美 실바코와 손잡고 차세대 SiC 전력반도체 개발
summary: SK파워텍이 미국의 반도체 설계 자동화(EDA) 기업 실바코와 협력하여 SiC(탄화규소) 전력반도체 개발에 나선다. SK파워텍은 실바코의 시뮬레이션 툴 '빅토리 TCAD'를 활용해 개발 시간을 단축하고 성능을 최적화할 계획이다. 이 협력은 전기차 등 다양한 분야에서 수요가 증가하는 SiC 전력반도체 시장에서의 경쟁력을 강화하는 데 기여할 것으로 보인다.

title: TESL Simplify Volt TSLA Revolution ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (VCAR)
summary: MarketBeat provides an aggregate rating and price target for the TESL Simplify Volt TSLA Revolution ETF (VCAR) based on the weighted average of individual stock ratings within its portfolio. The ETF currently holds an aggregate "Hold" rating with a price target of $14.04. This assessment is derived from 44 analyst ratings covering two companies, which represent 53.7% of the ETF's holdings.

title: Elon Musk Loses Key Litigator Amid Lawsuit Over Dogecoin Insider Trading Claims
summary: Elon Musk has lost a key in-house litigator, Adam Gabor Mehes, from his legal team amidst a $258 billion lawsuit concerning alleged Dogecoin market manipulation. This departure follows the addition of a new attorney, Allison Huebert, to Tesla's legal department. Musk continues to deny allegations of insider trading and leveraging his social media to influence DOGE's price.

title: Rivian 3rd OEM since May to sign Tesla Supercharger network agreement
summary: Rivian Automotive has reached an agreement with Tesla to grant Rivian drivers access to Tesla's Supercharger network in the U.S. and Canada, starting next spring. Rivian will also continue to expand its own charging network and plans to integrate North American Charging Standard (NACS) ports into new vehicles from 2025. This move follows similar agreements by Ford and General Motors, aiming to accelerate EV adoption by increasing charging infrastructure availability.

title: Blink Charging Announces New Dual-Port CCS And NACS Fast Charger
summary: Blink Charging has announced a new 240-kilowatt dual-port DC fast charger that supports both the CCS1 and Tesla's North American Charging Standard (NACS) connectors. This new charger, which uses silicon carbide power electronics for improved efficiency, is still in its final design stage but is expected to be available for order soon. The introduction of NACS compatibility is particularly significant for Blink Charging's network, as Tesla vehicles, which are NACS-native, constitute the majority of EVs in the US.

title: 2023 Cars.com American-Made Index: What About the Least American Cars?
summary: The 2023 Cars.com American-Made Index (AMI) once again named the Tesla Model Y as the most American-made car. This article explores vehicles that didn't make the cut, highlighting that many popular models from "American" brands are manufactured abroad due to the complex global automotive supply chain. It details how automakers vary assembly locations and provides a list of major automakers alongside the percentage of their U.S. light-duty sales that come from domestic assembly, with Tesla leading at 100%.

title: Applied Materials to invest $400 million in India for new engineering center
summary: U.S. semiconductor toolmaker Applied Materials (AMAT.O) announced a $400 million investment over four years to establish a new engineering center in India. This move follows Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi's meeting with Applied Materials CEO Gary Dickerson and is part of a broader wave of investments in India's chip industry. The new facility, planned for Bengaluru, aims to support over $2 billion in planned investments and create 500 new engineering jobs.

title: Alan McKim founded Clean Harbors. As technology chief, he’ll guide it into the future
summary: Alan McKim, founder of Clean Harbors, has transitioned from CEO to Chief Technology Officer to guide his multi-billion-dollar environmental cleanup company into the future, focusing on technology and AI integration. This move follows a significant ransomware attack in 2020 which highlighted the critical role of technology in the specialized hazardous waste industry. McKim, who built Clean Harbors from one truck into a global enterprise, will now drive technological advancements to meet evolving customer needs and waste challenges like lithium-ion batteries and solar panels.

title: Is Tesla's Stock Losing Its Charge?
summary: Despite a significant year-to-date gain of over 140%, Tesla's stock is experiencing recent profit-taking, leading some analysts to suggest moving to the sidelines. This pullback presents opportunities for traders using single-stock ETFs like TSLS for bearish plays or TSLL for bullish positions. While short-term momentum may be dissipating, Tesla's long-term prospects as a leading EV manufacturer remain strong due to the global shift towards reduced carbon emissions.

title: Toyota tackles Tesla trauma with radical EV production overhaul
summary: Toyota is overhauling its electric vehicle (EV) production strategy to compete with Tesla, aiming to "change the future with BEVs." This radical update was announced by Takero Kato, head of Toyota's EV development division, signaling a significant shift in the company's approach to the burgeoning EV market.

title: EV, Charging & Battery News: Volvo, Nikola, Rivian, StoreDot, Rheinmetall, Hyundai, Cadillac & Hyundai
summary: This article summarizes recent developments in the EV, charging, and battery sectors from several major companies. Volvo Trucks North America is conducting extreme weather testing for its VNR Electric model with the University of Minnesota, while Nikola's headquarters experienced a suspicious fire affecting multiple battery-electric trucks. Rivian announced it will adopt Tesla's NACS charging standard and integrate it into future vehicles, and StoreDot reported excellent performance feedback for its extreme fast charging battery cells from major automotive manufacturers. Rheinmetall introduced a new plug-and-play heat pump solution for EVs, Hyundai is testing its IONIQ 5N at Nürburgring and detailed its EV platform plans, and Cadillac previewed its upcoming ESCALADE IQ electric SUV.

title: Lucid, Aston Martin sign $450M EV powertrain deal
summary: Electric automaker Lucid has entered a long-term agreement with luxury brand Aston Martin to supply EV powertrain and battery systems, including its high-performance dual motor drive unit and 900-volt Wunderbox home charging unit. This approximately $450 million deal marks Lucid's first outside agreement to license its proprietary EV technology, with Aston Martin paying through a combination of stock and phased cash payments, making Lucid a shareholder. Aston Martin plans to launch its first electric sports car utilizing Lucid's technology in 2025.

title: Aston Martin turns to U.S. EV company Lucid for high-tech help
summary: Aston Martin has partnered with U.S. EV maker Lucid Group, giving Lucid a 3.7% stake in the company and cash payments in exchange for access to Lucid's high-performance EV technology, including electric powertrains and battery systems. This move is critical for Aston Martin's transition to electric vehicles, as smaller car manufacturers rely on partnerships due to the high cost of EV development. Both companies share Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund as a common major shareholder.

title: Polestar to adopt Tesla's supercharger network in the US, Canada
summary: Swedish electric vehicle maker Polestar has announced an agreement with Tesla to integrate Tesla's Supercharger network in the United States and Canada. Starting in 2025, new Polestar vehicles sold in North America will feature Tesla's North American Charging Standard (NACS) port. This move follows similar announcements from Volvo Cars, General Motors, and Rivian, indicating a growing shift towards Tesla's charging standard for EV manufacturers in North America.

title: ADS-TEC Energy further develops its battery-based fast charging systems for the US market; supports CCS1 and NACS standards in North America
summary: ADS-TEC Energy announced its fast-charging systems for the US market will support both CCS1 and Tesla's NACS standards, adapting to recent announcements by Ford, GM, and Rivian to adopt NACS. Despite the ongoing debate in the EV charging industry regarding a single standard, ADS-TEC Energy emphasizes its flexible, battery-based solutions can accommodate changing market conditions and continue to expand the charging infrastructure where it's most needed. The company, which has a significant presence in the US, aims to provide ultra-fast charging even on low-powered grids.

title: XPeng Announces G6 Ultra Smart Coupe SUV
summary: XPeng Inc. has launched its G6 Ultra Smart Coupe SUV in China, built on the SEPA2.0 architecture and priced from 209,900 RMB to 276,900 RMB, with deliveries starting in July 2023. This vehicle features advanced ADAS (XNGP), an 800V powertrain (XPower), integrated aluminum die-casting, and CIB battery-body integration. The G6 boasts a 755 km range, fast-charging capabilities, and a sophisticated design, aiming to solidify XPeng's leadership in the smart EV market.

title: Chinese EV maker Xpeng's shares jump after pricing SUV below Tesla Model Y, lifts peers
summary: Chinese EV maker Xpeng saw its US-listed shares jump 7.6% after pricing its new G6 SUV 20% below Tesla's Model Y in China. This strategic pricing aims to boost Xpeng's sales following disappointing delivery numbers in May. Analysts anticipate monthly sales of 10,000 units for the G6, with further demand expected from Beijing's 520 billion yuan tax break for new energy vehicles.

title: TThe High Price of Low Volatility | U.S. Equity market commentary 2Q23
summary: This article by Bill Nygren, Portfolio Manager at Oakmark, discusses how different entities define investment risk and argues against the common perception of index funds as low-risk, highlighting increasing concentration risk in indices like the NASDAQ 100 and S&P 500. He contrasts this with Oakmark's approach, which focuses on long-term value investing and accepts higher short-term volatility for potentially better long-term returns. Nygren contends that index fund investors and those seeking low volatility stocks are overlooking significant valuation risks.

title: Apple makes history as first $3 trillion company amid tech stock surge
summary: Apple Inc. achieved a historic milestone, becoming the first company to reach a $3 trillion market value, driven by investor optimism about its expansion into new markets and a projected easing of interest rate hikes. This accomplishment is set against a backdrop of a broader tech stock surge, with other companies like Tesla and Nvidia also seeing significant gains due to advancements in AI and strategic partnerships. The article highlights Apple's strong financial performance and its reputation as a safe investment, alongside the success of other tech giants.

title: UAE Cabinet approves testing of self-driving vehicles on its roads
summary: The UAE Cabinet has granted the first license for testing self-driving vehicles on its roads, approving WeRide, a Level 4 self-driving solutions provider, to conduct tests with various autonomous vehicle types. This decision, made during a meeting chaired by Sheikh Mohammed bin Rashid Al Maktoum, also included the approval of a national policy for electric vehicles to promote sustainability and reduce emissions. The move aims to enhance the well-being and quality of life for residents by embracing advanced mobility solutions and aligning with evolving global trends.

title: Korea-China battery minerals tie-up mutually beneficial: CNGR chief
summary: CNGR, the world's leading battery precursor maker, believes that strengthening the battery materials partnership between China and South Korea is mutually beneficial, especially given the strict US regulations on electric vehicles and their components. CNGR's chairman, Weiming Deng, highlighted that Korea excels in cathode materials while China leads in precursors, making closer cooperation a win-win. This strategy is exemplified by CNGR's recent 1.5 trillion won joint venture deal with POSCO Holdings in Korea to produce EV battery materials, aiming to start commercial production by 2026 and navigate the requirements of the US Inflation Reduction Act.

title: Hedge Fund and Insider Trading News: Crispin Odey, Cathie Wood, Said Haidar, Man Group, Black Diamond Therapeutics, Inc. (BDTX), Tri-Continental Corp (TY), and More
summary: This article compiles various hedge fund and insider trading news. It covers Said Haidar's hedge fund surging after losses, Man Group's acquisition of Varagon Capital for direct lending, SES Imagotag's stock defying a Gotham City Research report, foreign hedge funds showing interest in Argentinian stocks, and Cathie Wood's ARK ETFs selling Tesla shares.

title: Markets Brief: There’s Good News for Investors in Rates Staying Higher for Longer
summary: Despite the Federal Reserve's anticipated interest rate hikes, rising bond yields are creating attractive opportunities for investors, particularly in fixed-income assets. The strong June jobs report reinforces the likelihood of continued rate increases, but strategists believe this "higher-for-longer" environment allows investors to lock in significant yields, with even real returns exceeding inflation expectations. This shift signals a favorable period for revisiting fixed-income portfolios before the Fed concludes its hiking cycle.

title: FIAX ETF Stock Price History
summary: This article provides comprehensive historical stock price data for the Nicholas Fixed Income Alternative ETF (FIAX), including daily closing, opening, high, low, volume, and percentage change. It also offers key statistics over selected date ranges and answers frequently asked questions about the ETF's performance, such as its 52-week range and daily trading fluctuations. The data is presented in a table format covering February 23, 2026, to March 20, 2026, highlighting recent price movements.

title: Top Performing Leveraged/Inverse ETFs: 07/09/2023
summary: This article lists the top 10 performing leveraged and inverse ETFs for the week ending July 9, 2023. It highlights ETFs like MSOX, EVAV, KOLD, and others, explaining the factors contributing to their strong performance, such as cannabis legalization, Tesla's Q2 results, falling natural gas prices, and changes in bond yields and market volatility. The article also briefly describes each ETF's investment focus and the market conditions that influenced its returns.

title: Tesla settles class-action Solar Roof lawsuit for $6 million
summary: Tesla has agreed to a $6 million settlement in a class-action lawsuit concerning sudden price hikes for its Solar Roof system in 2021. These increases led to over 6,300 customers canceling their contracts, despite many having already signed agreements for installation. The settlement addresses the financial impact on customers who faced significant unexpected costs.

title: How Tesla's price cuts almost put Scott Painter out of business
summary: Tesla's significant price cuts nearly led to financial ruin for Scott Painter, a business owner with an $85 million fleet of vehicles. The unexpected depreciation reduced the value of his fleet by millions in a single day, highlighting the severe impact of Tesla's pricing strategy on secondary markets and related businesses.

title: Cathie Wood's ARK Takes Profit as Coinbase Trades at Highest Level This Year
summary: Cathie Wood's Ark Invest has sold $12 million worth of Coinbase (COIN) shares, taking profits as the cryptocurrency exchange's stock reached a yearly high. This move comes after Ark Invest had been an aggressive buyer of Coinbase shares during a period of sector turmoil, and as Coinbase's stock has surged almost 150% year-to-date despite regulatory uncertainty. The sale follows news of a potential surveillance-sharing deal for Bitcoin ETFs, which could ease the path to the first spot ETF.

title: Texas Capital Texas Equity Index ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NYSEARCA:TXS) $TXS
summary: The Texas Capital Texas Equity Index ETF (TXS) is an exchange-traded fund launched on July 12, 2023, that aims to track a tier-weighted equity index composed of companies headquartered in Texas. The ETF has a current market capitalization of $34.32 million and assets under management of $34.51 million, with a net expense ratio of 0.49% and a dividend yield of 0.74%. Its top holdings include McKesson, Tenet Healthcare, and Tesla, and it holds 216 stocks with a "Moderate Buy" aggregate rating.

title: Tesla looking to make about half million EVs annually in India, Times of India reports
summary: Tesla is reportedly discussing a proposal with the Indian government to establish a factory capable of producing approximately half a million electric vehicles annually, with plans to use India as an export base for the Indo-Pacific region. This comes after previous stalled entry plans due to India's high import taxes on EVs, with Tesla now reportedly reconsidering local manufacturing and engaging in talks about government incentives. The projected starting price for these vehicles would be around 2 million rupees, making them more expensive than existing cheapest Indian EVs but a significant step towards local production.

title: Samsung takes on Micron with new flash auto memory UFS 3.1
summary: Samsung Electronics has begun mass production of its new Universal Flash Storage (UFS) 3.1 automotive chip, designed for in-vehicle infotainment systems with significantly reduced power consumption. The company aims to surpass Micron Technology as the market leader by 2025 by supplying these chips to global automakers and parts manufacturers. This move strengthens Samsung's presence in the rapidly growing automotive semiconductor market, driven by increasing demand for advanced chips in electric and self-driving vehicles.

title: SpaceX valuation tops Boeing, Raytheon after insider share sale boosts it to near $150 billion
summary: SpaceX's valuation has surged to nearly $150 billion following a secondary share sale to insiders, surpassing aerospace giants Boeing and Raytheon. This makes SpaceX the most valuable aerospace and defense company in the U.S. The company's Starlink satellite internet business is a primary driver of this valuation, with significant investments also going into the Starship program despite recent launch setbacks and regulatory challenges.

title: Automotive semiconductor shortage mostly resolved: S&P Global
summary: S&P Global Mobility reports that the automotive semiconductor shortage has largely resolved, with light vehicle losses due to the shortage falling significantly from 9.5 million in 2021 to an estimated 524,000. Automakers have adapted production schedules and are ramping up output. However, as vehicles become more complex, demand for advanced semiconductors is expected to rise, potentially impacting supply and increasing the value of semiconductors per car.

title: Why the Nasdaq 100 may not capture the full growth potential of AI
summary: This article argues that while the Nasdaq-100 has seen significant gains from AI-driven tech stocks like Nvidia, it may not offer investors the most comprehensive or effective exposure to the long-term growth potential of artificial intelligence. The authors suggest that dedicated AI thematic strategies can provide broader diversification into pure-play AI companies, capture the growth of future mega-caps earlier, and improve risk-adjusted returns compared to a core allocation in the Nasdaq-100. They emphasize that while the Nasdaq-100 taps into AI through a few large companies, a targeted AI strategy offers more comprehensive exposure across the evolving AI landscape.

title: Avient gears new R-polymers for auto sector
summary: Avient Corp. has introduced new recycled-content thermoplastic elastomers (TPEs) under its reSound REC product line, specifically designed for automotive interior applications. These TPEs, containing 35 percent or 45 percent postconsumer recycled (PCR) content from food packaging, offer a sustainable alternative with comparable performance to traditional TPEs. They meet common automotive OEM standards for VOCs and fogging and can be customized for various interior components, contributing to manufacturers' sustainability goals.

title: The Fourth Turning Is Here
summary: In this article, Keith McCullough discusses Neil Howe's new book "The Fourth Turning Is Here" and the current state of the American economy and society. He highlights concerning statistics about the public's perception of America's collapse and the challenges facing the U.S. economy, including slowing growth and re-accelerating inflation, contrasting his data-driven analysis with mainstream media narratives. McCullough provides detailed market signals across various asset classes, emphasizing the importance of objective data in navigating turbulent times.

title: 2014 Tesla Model S vs 2023 Tesla Model 3 Review: Is Newer Actually Better?
summary: This article compares a 2014 Tesla Model S with a 2023 Tesla Model 3 to determine which offers a better ownership experience in 2023. The review highlights differences in powertrain technology, driving experience, charging speeds, interior comfort, and overall cost considerations. While the Model S offers luxury and a lower used price, the Model 3 provides modern efficiency, faster charging, and a warranty, ultimately making it the more practical choice for a long-term commitment.

title: Volkswagen Takes $700 Million Xpeng Stake for EV Pact to Win Back China
summary: Volkswagen AG is investing $700 million in Chinese EV maker Xpeng Inc. to jointly develop electric vehicles. This strategic move aims to combat a sales decline in China, Volkswagen's largest market, and will result in VW holding a 4.99% stake in Xpeng. Additionally, Volkswagen's Audi brand will strengthen its collaboration with SAIC Motor Corp Ltd. to enhance its EV offerings.

title: ‘A tripling of electrical output’: Tesla’s Musk urges power sector to anticipate higher demand
summary: Tesla CEO Elon Musk has warned the power sector to prepare for significantly higher electricity demand in a decarbonized future, stating that current predictions are likely too low. He emphasized the need for a "tripling of electrical output" to transition to a fully sustainable and electric energy system, encompassing transport and heating. Musk's remarks were made at a Pacific Gas & Electric summit, where both he and PG&E's CEO highlighted the urgency for grid modernization, faster project deployment, and supportive regulatory frameworks to meet this anticipated load growth.

title: Dallas firm's development could be biggest ever in Central Texas
summary: A Dallas-based developer, Xebec Holdings LLC, has unveiled plans for a massive 3,300-acre industrial and logistics campus on the former Alcoa aluminum plant site in Milam County, near Austin. This development, which could reach up to 50 million square feet of industrial space, is projected to be the largest in Central Texas, supported by existing infrastructure and proximity to major economic hubs like Samsung's chip factory and Tesla headquarters. The project is expected to create significant job growth and economic prosperity for the region, with the first phases operational by mid-2025.

title: Tesla created secret team to suppress thousands of driving range complaints
summary: Tesla reportedly established a "Diversion Team" in Las Vegas to handle and often cancel service appointments related to customer complaints about overstated driving range. This initiative was a response to an influx of complaints stemming from Tesla's alleged practice of rigging its in-dash range-estimating software to show "rosy" projections, particularly driven by CEO Elon Musk's directive. Regulators and independent testers have consistently found Tesla vehicles failing to meet advertised range estimates, leading to fines in South Korea for false advertising and audits by the U.S. EPA.

title: 2023 Tesla Model 3, Model Y investigated for loss of steering control
summary: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) has launched an investigation into steering control issues affecting 2023 Tesla Model 3 and Model Y vehicles, based on 12 complaints. These complaints describe either an inability to steer or a loss of power steering, with one reported crash but no injuries. This is the fourth NHTSA investigation for the 2023 Model Y and the second for the 2023 Model 3, which could potentially lead to a recall.

title: Lucid cuts prices by up to 11% on Air models, and stock slumps toward a 6-week low
summary: Lucid Group Inc. announced price cuts of up to 11.5% on its Air Pure, Touring, and Grand Touring electric vehicle models, leading to a slump in its stock prices and those of rival EV makers. The announcement precedes Lucid's earnings report and coincides with a recall of 59 Air EVs due to a faulty rearview camera image.

title: DTE offers $2,000 in subsidies, special charging rates for EVs
summary: DTE Energy is offering significant subsidies for electric vehicle adoption in Michigan, including rebates of up to $1,500 for EV purchases or leases and $500 for home charger installations. These incentives are part of a broader push to expand EV use, though the company faces criticism regarding its infrastructure's reliability during extreme weather events. The article highlights the conflict between EV adoption goals and the current state of energy infrastructure, as expressed by Michigan residents.

title: How to Buy LG Energy Solution Stock [2025]
summary: LG Energy Solution is a South Korean battery company listed only on the Korea Exchange. International investors can gain exposure by buying shares of ETFs with exposure to the company or indirectly through LG Display stock, which trades on the NYSE. The article provides a step-by-step guide on how to invest in ETFs with LG Energy Solution exposure through a regulated broker, outlining factors to consider when choosing a broker and assessing lithium battery stock investments.

title: Blink Will Benefit From Industry Shift to Tesla Charging Standard, CEO Says
summary: Blink Charging (BLNK) CEO Brendan Jones stated that the auto industry's shift to Tesla Inc.'s charging standard will benefit his company, despite Blink operating a competing network. Automakers like Ford and General Motors are adopting Tesla's North American Charging Standard to give their EV buyers access to Tesla's extensive public charging stations. Jones affirmed that Blink is well-positioned to offer stations equipped with Tesla-style plugs, leveraging the company's 186% surge in quarterly revenue.

title: 10 Things You May Not Know About Tesla, Inc.
summary: This article lists ten interesting facts about Tesla, Inc. It covers details such as Elon Musk not being the original founder, but rather a key early investor, the origin of the company's name, their battery technology, the extensive Supercharger network, and the safety features and performance aspects of their vehicles.

title: ReElement Technologies Produces Ultra-High Purity, 99.99%, Battery-Grade Lithium from LFP Manufacturing Waste
summary: ReElement Technologies, a subsidiary of American Resources Corporation, announced it has achieved ultra-high purity 99.99% battery-grade lithium from LFP battery manufacturing waste using its patented multi-modal chromatography technology. This technology offers a cost-effective and environmentally friendly method for refining lithium, crucial for the domestic auto and electrification industries. The company is scaling its production capacity, with plans for a larger annual capacity of approximately 13,750,000 kilograms per year.

title: Elon Musk’s Twitter Sued For Race Discrimination During Mass Layoffs
summary: Seven former Twitter employees are suing the company, now rebranded as X, alleging race discrimination in its mass layoffs. The lawsuits claim Black employees were unfairly impacted, citing unlawful race-based discrimination and Musk's history of reinstating banned racists. This legal action follows similar allegations against Musk's other company, Tesla, for racial harassment.

title: Stellantis COO calls UAW demands a 'losing proposition'
summary: Stellantis COO Mark Stewart has called the United Auto Workers' initial contract demands a "losing proposition" that could endanger jobs and the company's future. The UAW is seeking a 46% wage increase, a 32-hour work week paid for 40, and expanded benefits, which Stellantis estimates would significantly increase labor costs, making them uncompetitive. Stewart expressed disappointment with the UAW president's "theatrics" and urged a focus on realism for the long-term health of the company and job security.

title: When Will All Fast-Charging Stations Be Open to All EVs?
summary: The EV charging landscape is rapidly evolving with Tesla's North American Charging Standard (NACS) gaining traction among major automakers. While this promises greater interoperability and access to Tesla's extensive Supercharger network, full compatibility across all fast-charging stations for all EVs will take years, not months. The changes include automakers switching to NACS, other networks adding NACS connectors, and a joint venture among six major car companies to build a new vast charging network, all spurred by federal funding.

title: 8 Best Battery Stocks & ETFs to Watch
summary: This article identifies top battery stocks and ETFs for investors, highlighting the increasing demand for batteries across various sectors from electronics to EVs. It introduces key players like Tesla, CATL, Panasonic, and emerging companies like QuantumScape and FREYR Battery, discussing their technological advancements and market positions. The article also suggests battery-focused ETFs for diversified exposure to the sector.

title: Tesla Rolls Out New Base Model S and X for $10,000 Less
summary: Tesla has introduced new, lower-priced Standard Range versions of its Model S and Model X vehicles, costing $10,000 less than previous base models. This move brings the starting prices of these models down significantly since the beginning of the year and follows recent price cuts in China, signaling a potential strategy to boost sales despite impacting profit margins. CEO Elon Musk has indicated a willingness to sacrifice profitability for sales growth amidst rising interest rates.

title: Tesla drops Model S, Model X base price with Standard Range versions
summary: Tesla has introduced new, lower-priced Standard Range versions for its Model S and Model X, making them $10,000 cheaper than previous base models. These new versions feature software-limited battery packs, resulting in reduced range and slightly slower acceleration. This pricing adjustment is suspected to be a response to rising interest rates and increased competition from rivals like Lucid.

title: Tesla didn't fix Autopilot after fatal crash, engineers say
summary: Tesla engineers' testimony suggests the company did not implement fixes for its Autopilot system following a fatal crash. This information comes amidst ongoing scrutiny and legal challenges surrounding Tesla's self-driving technology.

title: Worldwide Auto Warranty Expenses:
summary: The article details the worldwide automotive warranty expenses for 2022, analyzing claims paid, accruals made, and warranty reserves held by 38 major manufacturers. It highlights key trends such as the lowest claims total in five years, rising accruals to a five-year high, and fluctuating reserve balances. The report also examines regional differences and the impact of EV manufacturers and recalls on warranty metrics, providing an in-depth look at the financial health of the global auto industry from a warranty perspective.

title: Hesai Reports Record Lidar Sales, Revenue
summary: Hesai Technology (NASDAQ: HSAI) reported record-breaking unaudited financial results for Q2 2023, with quarterly revenue reaching RMB440.3 million ($60.7 million), a 108.5% YoY increase. The company shipped 52,106 lidar units, a 946.5% YoY increase, significantly driven by ADAS lidar growth. Hesai also solidified its position as the No. 1 global automotive lidar supplier, secured new design wins with major OEMs, and deepened its collaboration with NVIDIA.

title: Toyota, Honda, Nissan’s China sales all plummet YoY in July
summary: In July 2023, Toyota, Honda, and Nissan all experienced significant year-on-year sales declines in China, following a broader trend of Japanese auto brands' plummeting sedan and SUV sales in the market. This downturn is attributed to the expiration of tax incentives and, crucially, their lagging adoption of new energy vehicles (NEVs), while Chinese NEV giant BYD saw substantial growth. Nissan is now considering exporting China-made cars to support its operations amidst these challenges.

title: Tesla says two ex-employees behind May data breach
summary: Tesla has revealed that two former employees were responsible for a May data breach that compromised the personal information of over 75,000 individuals, including staff. The exposed data was leaked to German media outlet Handelsblatt, prompting Tesla to file lawsuits and seize the devices of the identified employees. This incident follows a previous report in April about Tesla employees sharing customer information internally.

title: Technology continues to find fertile ground northeast of Austin
summary: The area northeast of Austin, including Pflugerville, Hutto, and Taylor, is experiencing significant commercial and industrial development, largely driven by large technology companies like Amazon, Samsung, and Tesla. Skybox Datacenters and Prologis are developing PowerCenter Austin, a massive 600-megawatt data center campus in Hutto. This development, alongside others, is expected to bring substantial economic benefits and further infrastructure growth to the region.

title: NVD - GraniteShares 2x Short NVDA Daily ETF Stock Price and Quote
summary: This article provides detailed stock price and quote information for the GraniteShares 2x Short NVDA Daily ETF (NVD), an exchange-traded fund designed to deliver inverse daily returns of NVIDIA Corporation's stock. It includes performance metrics, financial data, and relevant news headlines. The ETF aims to replicate -1.5 times the daily percentage change of NVIDIA's common stock through swap agreements.

title: GraniteShares 2x Short TSLA Daily ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:TSDD)
summary: The GraniteShares 2x Short TSLA Daily ETF (TSDD) provides 2x inverse leveraged exposure to the daily price movement of Tesla stock. As of March 3, 2026, TSDD was trading at $9.65, representing a 24.8% increase since the beginning of 2026. The ETF has an expense ratio of 1.15% and launched on August 21, 2023.

title: GraniteShares' Single Stock ETFs Will Follow Nvidia, Tesla, AMD
summary: GraniteShares has launched four new single-stock ETFs to capitalize on the trading momentum of Nvidia, Tesla, and Advanced Micro Devices. These new ETFs offer unique leveraged and short exposures, with the GraniteShares 1.5x Short NVDA Daily ETF (NVD) launching just before Nvidia's earnings report. The asset manager plans to roll out almost three dozen more such filings, emphasizing being first to market with these trading-focused products.

title: The 3 Most Undervalued Stocks Under $5 to Buy Now: August 2023
summary: This article identifies three undervalued stocks under $5 for long-term investment in August 2023, focusing on sectors like commodities and clean energy. The recommended stocks are Kinross Gold (KGC), Uranium Energy (UEC), and Wallbox (WBX), highlighting their growth potential within their respective industries.

title: United Auto Workers Members Approve Possible Strikes at GM, Ford and Stellantis
summary: United Auto Workers members have overwhelmingly approved potential strikes at General Motors, Ford, and Stellantis, as the union seeks to regain concessions made over 15 years ago and secure protections during the industry's transition to electric vehicles. While the vote empowers union leadership to call a strike if negotiations fail, all three automakers have expressed commitment to reaching a deal without a work stoppage. President Biden has also voiced concern about a potential strike, emphasizing the need for fair wages and job security for UAW members.

title: Is Xpeng (XPEV) a value in Electric Vehicles?
summary: This article questions whether Xpeng (XPEV), a Chinese electric vehicle maker, is a good value despite its lower stock price compared to Tesla. The author argues against its value due to significant quarterly operating losses, declining revenues and gross profit, and a shrinking growth rate. Despite these financial losses and asset shrinkage, American investors are still interested, likely due to the growth of the Chinese EV market.

title: Tesla Powerwall owners will boost Texas power grid, make money
summary: Texas is launching a "virtual power plant" pilot program that will reward Tesla Powerwall owners for using their in-home battery packs to support the grid. The program, managed by ERCOT, allows Powerwall owners in designated regions to sell surplus power to the grid. This initiative aims to integrate stored solar energy seamlessly and follows similar pilot programs in other states like California.

title: Ski-doo maker BRP launches its most expensive off-road vehicle ever
summary: BRP Inc., the maker of Sea-Doo and Ski-Doo, has introduced its most expensive off-road vehicle to date, the Can-Am Maverick R, priced between $43,999 and $54,799 in Canada. This 240-horsepower side-by-side vehicle is a strategic move to capture a larger share of the high-end motorsports market. BRP aims to leverage the Maverick R's advanced features and the growing demand for premium off-road products to boost sales and profits, despite investor concerns about economic slowdowns.

title: Mercedes-Benz to Open First Charging Hubs in October
summary: Mercedes-Benz is launching its first EV Charging Hubs in October in Atlanta, Mannheim (Germany), and Chengdu (China), prioritizing convenience and renewable energy. These hubs will be open to all EV brands, with special benefits for Mercedes owners, and the company plans to have over 10,000 chargers globally by the end of the decade. This initiative, alongside partnerships with other automakers and Tesla, underscores Mercedes' commitment to building a robust charging ecosystem to support its electrification strategy.

title: S&P Global Mobility: August US auto sales trends remain familiar
summary: S&P Global Mobility predicts US light vehicle sales in August will reach 1.34 million units, an 18% increase year-over-year but only a 3% rise from July. The seasonally adjusted annual rate (SAAR) is expected to be 15.2 million units. While sales show year-over-year growth, market softening indicators are present, including a decreasing daily selling rate and ongoing affordability concerns due to rising interest rates and high vehicle pricing.

title: Tesla Ordered by NHTSA to Address New Issue Over Autopilot
summary: Tesla is facing increased scrutiny from the NHTSA over its Autopilot driver-assistance system. The agency issued a "special order" concerning a configuration that allows drivers to operate vehicles for extended periods without needing to apply torque to the steering wheel, potentially subverting driver monitoring. Tesla has submitted a confidential reply to address the regulator's concerns.

title: Explainer: What happened to shut down Toyota's production in Japan?
summary: Toyota's 14 assembly plants in Japan were forced to shut down due to a computer system malfunction that processes vehicle parts orders. The company confirmed it was not a cyber attack but occurred during a system update. This disruption highlights the vulnerability of Toyota's "just-in-time" manufacturing model and resulted in an estimated loss of $356 million in revenue from a single day's production.

title: VinFast market cap reaches $191B, comparable to Tesla’s valuation in 2020
summary: Vietnamese EV maker VinFast (NASDAQ:VFS) achieved a market capitalization of $190 billion, making it the third most valuable carmaker globally, a valuation Tesla reached in 2020. This milestone is notable given VinFast's relatively low sales of 24,000 cars in 2022 compared to Tesla's established leadership when it hit a similar valuation. The high market cap is partly attributed to a limited float, with founder Pham Nhat Vuong controlling 99% of shares, which causes significant stock volatility.

title: Tesla freshens Model 3 with premium touches, cuts Model S, X prices
summary: Tesla has updated its Model 3 with a sleeker front fascia and other aerodynamic improvements, giving it a more premium feel. Concurrently, the company has reduced the prices of its Model S and Model X vehicles. These changes aim to refresh Tesla's lineup and potentially boost sales for the premium models.

title: Tesla releases refreshed Model 3 with longer driving range in China
summary: Tesla has launched a refreshed Model 3 in China with a higher price and longer driving range, a move that precedes its U.S. release and highlights the company's focus on the Chinese market. Simultaneously, Tesla cut prices for its premium Model S and Model X vehicles and its "Full Self-Driving" software in its biggest markets. This strategy aims to protect margins on the Model 3 while addressing intense EV competition, especially in China.

title: Globalstar taps SpaceX to launch Apple's Emergency SOS satellites
summary: Globalstar has partnered with SpaceX to launch satellites for Apple's Emergency SOS iPhone feature by 2025, with Apple funding 95% of the costs, including the $64 million launch fee. This agreement is notable given an ongoing regulatory dispute where Globalstar accuses SpaceX of attempting to secure radio spectrum vital for its own satellite services. The Emergency SOS feature, exclusively reliant on Globalstar's network for iPhone 14 and newer models, allows users to connect to satellites for emergency help without cellular or Wi-Fi.

title: Albemarle, Major Lithium Producer, Boosts Liontown Bid to $4.3 Billion
summary: Albemarle Corp., the world's largest lithium producer, has increased its takeover bid for Australian lithium miner Liontown Resources Ltd. to A$6.6 billion ($4.3 billion). Liontown has granted Albemarle due diligence access following this enhanced offer, which underscores the growing value of the Australian lithium sector despite recent price declines. Analysts view this as a positive sign for the lithium sector's robust fundamentals and a potential driver for more mergers and acquisitions.

title: Tesla sues Chinese firm over tech secret infringement -Chinese state media
summary: Tesla Shanghai has filed a lawsuit against Chinese firm Bingling Intelligent Technology for technology secret infringement and unfair competition. The lawsuit, scheduled to be heard on October 10 in Shanghai, targets the Changzhou-based chip designer and auto parts maker, in which a Xiaomi investment fund unit holds a significant stake. This action underscores Tesla's efforts to protect its intellectual property in the Chinese market.

title: 2024 Volkswagen ID.4 Gets Power, Controls Update
summary: The 2024 Volkswagen ID.4 is receiving updates, primarily for models equipped with the larger 82-kWh battery. These versions will see increased horsepower and range for both rear- and all-wheel-drive configurations, as well as significant interior enhancements. Key improvements include an updated, more intuitive control interface with a larger display, backlit sliders, and revised steering wheel controls.

title: Honda to Adopt North American Charging Standard (NACS) for its EV Models in North America
summary: Honda Motor Co., Ltd. announced an agreement with Tesla, Inc. to adopt Tesla's North American Charging Standard (NACS) for its EV models in North America, starting from 2025. This move will allow Honda EV owners to use Tesla Supercharger stations, significantly enhancing charging convenience. Honda is also part of a joint venture with other automakers to build a high-powered EV charging network, aiming to achieve widespread EV adoption and carbon neutrality.

title: Lotus launches electric GT model as mass expansion plan rolls on
summary: Lotus unveiled its new fully-electric grand tourer (GT) sports car, the Emeya, in New York, marking a significant step in its transition from a niche brand to a mass manufacturer with a target of 150,000 sales annually by 2028. The Emeya, which will begin production in 2024 in China, aims to attract buyers from German brands and Tesla with its long range and luxury features. This expansion is supported by Geely and Etika Automotive, with Lotus Technology also planning to go public via a merger with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp.

title: NHTSA pushes to recall 52 million airbag inflators that ruptured and caused injury, death
summary: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) is advocating for the recall of 52 million airbag inflators manufactured by ARC Automotive Inc. and Delphi Automotive Systems due to their potential to rupture and cause serious injury or death. This proposed recall comes after one fatality and seven injuries in the U.S., as well as incidents abroad, linked to shrapnel propelled by over-pressurization during airbag deployment. ARC Automotive has disputed the safety defect, but the NHTSA's investigation suggests weld slag is the likely cause of the ruptures, affecting vehicles from numerous manufacturers including BMW, Ford, GM, Hyundai, and Tesla.

title: Tesla’s market value could surge by $500 billion because of its Dojo supercomputer, Morgan Stanley says
summary: Morgan Stanley believes Tesla's Dojo supercomputer could increase the company's market value by as much as $500 billion. The firm upgraded Tesla's stock to overweight with a $400 price target, citing Dojo's potential to enable faster adoption of robotaxis and network services, similar to Amazon Web Services' impact on Amazon. Analysts anticipate that Dojo will make software and services the primary value driver for Tesla going forward.

title: EV maker VinFast seeks 'daring' applicants in India hiring drive
summary: VinFast, the Vietnamese electric vehicle (EV) manufacturer, has started a hiring drive in India, looking for candidates described as "daring." This move signals the startup's intent to enter the Indian market, where it will compete with established players like Tata Motors and potentially Tesla, which is also eyeing the country for a factory. The company, which saw its market value halve after its Wall Street debut, is searching for sales, legal, and back-office personnel for its Gurugram office.

title: From Cheap Cash to Tax Breaks, EVs in China Get Lots of Love
summary: China heavily subsidizes its electric vehicle (EV) industry through a variety of mechanisms, including significant tax breaks, production subsidies, cheap land and loans, and R&D support. These extensive measures have made Chinese-made EVs more affordable and boosted demand, leading to a considerable increase in exports to Europe, prompting an EU investigation into unfair subsidies. While these subsidies have fostered rapid growth and technological advancement in the sector, many EV manufacturers in China still struggle with profitability due to the large number of brands in the market.

title: Tesla, Saudi Arabia in early talks for EV factory - WSJ
summary: Saudi Arabia is reportedly in early talks with Tesla to establish a manufacturing facility in the kingdom, according to the Wall Street Journal. The kingdom is attempting to diversify its economy away from oil and has offered Tesla access to metals and minerals for its EV production. This comes as Tesla aims to significantly increase its annual vehicle sales by 2030 and is scouting locations for new factories.

title: Should Investors Worry About EVs Overloading the Grid?
summary: A common concern among investors is that the existing electrical grid may not be able to support the widespread adoption of electric vehicles (EVs). However, industry expert Anthony Sassine of KraneShares explains that utility companies are proactively planning for this transition, looking ahead as much as 15 years. Solutions include expanding the grid and installing chargers in various locations to disperse charging demand and avoid peak time overloads.

title: Tesla Inc. stock falls Wednesday, still outperforms market

title: Thailand's new PM holds talks with Tesla chief Musk

title: Great Expectations: The Saturn Cars Story

title: Going beyond Tesla: ETF bets solely on electric vehicle makers
summary: A new ETF, Defiance ETFs' Solactive Pure U.S. Electric Vehicle ETF (Pure EV Index fund), offers a concentrated bet solely on electric vehicle manufacturers, as opposed to other EV-related ETFs that include tech companies or component producers. The fund invests in only the five largest market-cap EV makers—Tesla, Nio, Rivian, Li Auto, and Xpeng—provided they derive at least 50% of their revenue from EV development or manufacturing and have high trading volume. Since its launch in June, the ETF has gained over 18%, boasting $5.1 million in net assets.

title: Ford Motor Company 6.5 % Notes 2022-15.08.62 Trade Ideas — NYSE:F/PD

Overall, the content reflects diverse perspectives on Ford's market position, emphasizing both its operational improvements and market challenges.

title: Toppling Tesla: Toyota Unveils Solid-State Battery Production Breakthrough
summary: Toyota Motor Corporation recently held a technical briefing showcasing new technologies to challenge Tesla and BYD's EV market dominance. The company unveiled its assembly line for solid-state batteries, intended for mass production by 2026 or 2027, which promises to dramatically improve EV performance. Toyota also demonstrated its prototype gigacasting equipment, aiming for higher productivity than competitors by combining traditional craftsmanship with digital technology, despite accusations of imitating Tesla.

summary: Honda and Acura have announced new agreements with EVgo and Electrify America, expanding their EV customers' access to North America's largest charging networks. These agreements, combined with access to Tesla Superchargers and a new joint venture high-powered charging network, aim to provide Honda and Acura EV drivers with around 100,000 public DC fast charge points by 2030. Customers purchasing or leasing a new Honda or Acura EV will also receive charging packages including public charging credits and installation credits for home charging solutions.

title: Dave Pericak, Director EV Truck Programs, Ford Motor Company
summary: Dave Pericak, Ford's Director of EV Truck Programs, emphasizes the importance of applying a customer-centric and rule-breaking performance mindset to EV development. He discusses the challenges of mainstream EV adoption, focusing on education to dispel misconceptions and the need to reduce costs while retaining vehicle character. Pericak also highlights the significance of accessing the Tesla Supercharger network and Ford's strategy to optimize battery efficiency rather than simply increasing size.

title: Chinese demand outlook pressures lithium prices to 2-year lows

This decline is attributed to concerns over Chinese demand for lithium, a key EV battery component, as battery makers still hold significant inventory despite the typical fourth-quarter demand increase. The slump has also negatively impacted lithium producers, with related ETFs reaching multi-year lows.

title: Tesla reintroduces rear-wheel drive Model Y at lower price
summary: Tesla has reintroduced a rear-wheel-drive version of its Model Y, offering it at a lower price point. This move aims to make the electric SUV more accessible to consumers. The article was published on October 3, 2023.

title: China’s latest rare earths quota could sustain weak prices
summary: China's increased rare earth quota for 2023, raising the output limit by about 14% over 2022, is expected to maintain low rare earth ore and oxide prices. This move, driven by China's desire for "rational prices" to support its domestic industries like EV manufacturing, ensures a well-supplied market despite weak downstream demand. The sustained lower prices, however, pose challenges for Western rare earth producers who require higher prices for investment and profitability.

title: New Lithium Stock Trades Today. It's Another Way to Play the EV Trend.
summary: Lithium Americas, a lithium mining startup, has completed its transformation into two separate companies. It has spun off Lithium Americas Argentina to its shareholders. This move presents a new opportunity for investors to engage with the electric vehicle trend.

summary: Chinese EV startup WM Motor has filed for bankruptcy, marking the end of a once-promising company amidst intensified price competition in China's automotive market. The materials will be shipped to Tesla’s assembly factory in Austin, marking a significant product shift for the Helsinki-based company.

title: Fermata Energy's FE-20 Bidirectional EV Charger and V2G Software Platform Achieves UL 9741 and UL 1741 SA Certifications, Signaling Shift in EV Charging Landscape
summary: Fermata Energy's new FE-20 bidirectional EV charger and V2G software platform have received UL 9741 and UL 1741-SA certifications, highlighting its commitment to safety and performance in vehicle-to-everything (V2X) technology. Developed with Heliox, the FE-20 allows commercial EV owners to generate revenue by selling stored energy back to the grid, marking a significant advancement for the electrification and mobility sectors. This certification reinforces Fermata Energy's position as a leader in V2G technology and its potential to revolutionize energy management.

summary: Tesla has reportedly selected Finnish company Outokumpu Oyj, Europe's largest stainless steel producer, to supply the stainless steel for its Cybertruck. title: Man faces four years behind bars after tricking Tesla into handing him five cars he never paid for
summary: Michael Gonzalez of Shelburne, Vermont, has been sentenced to four years in prison after pleading guilty to multiple interstate felonies for scamming Tesla. Gonzalez exploited a loophole in Tesla's system to obtain five luxury vehicles worth approximately $560,000 using minimal deposits and fictitious bank account details. His scheme unraveled when his attempt to commit arson on a Model X he couldn't legally resell led to an insurance investigation, exposing his fraud.

title: Tesla earnings: investors watching margins, deliveries, self-driving
summary: Investors are closely watching Tesla's upcoming earnings report, focusing on potential decreases in margins due to continued price cuts aimed at boosting demand. Key areas of interest include the company's ability to meet its annual delivery target, the impact of falling margins, updates on the Cybertruck launch, the progress of its full-self-driving technology, and details regarding the new factory in Mexico. The pressure on margins is intensified by an aging vehicle lineup, high interest rates, and competition from BYD.

Musk also tempered expectations for the Cybertruck's financial contribution, stating it would take 12-18 months to achieve significant positive cash flow after production begins.

This cautious approach suggests a strategic focus on existing projects and an awareness of broader economic conditions.
