title: Tesla gets $100 mln US ultra-fast charger order from BP EV charging unit
summary: BP's electric vehicle charger unit, BP Pulse, has placed a $100 million order for Tesla's ultra-fast chargers to be rolled out across the United States, marking the first time Tesla's charging hardware will be deployed on an independent network. This initiative is part of BP Pulse's plan to invest up to $1 billion in U.S. charging stations by 2030 and will see the chargers, compatible with both NACS and CCS connectors, installed at BP sites and third-party locations starting in 2024. The move further solidifies Tesla's NACS as a potential industry standard, providing a new revenue stream for the EV market leader.

Despite the scrapped joint venture, both companies reaffirm their commitment to affordability in the EV market and will continue to collaborate on other projects, including fuel cell technology and autonomous vehicles.

summary: Chinese autonomous driving technology company Pony.ai has secured a US$100 million investment from Saudi Arabia's smart city developer Neom. This funding, channeled through the Neom Investment Fund, will support Pony.ai's research and development and operations. Additionally, the two entities will form a joint venture to establish autonomous driving services, advanced vehicles, and smart vehicle infrastructure within Neom and other key Middle East and North Africa markets.

title: bp Orders $100 Million Worth of EV Chargers From Tesla

The Tesla chargers will be compatible with both NACS and CCS connectors, catering to a wider range of EV models, and will integrate with bp pulse's Omega software for fleet management.

This move is a significant step towards BP's goal of establishing 100,000 charging points globally by 2030 and marks Tesla's first sale of hardware for an independent charging network.

VinFast is evaluating sites in Tamil Nadu and Gujarat, prioritizing states with port access to facilitate East-West connectivity and reduce logistical costs.

While EV sales are still growing, the pace has decelerated significantly, leading companies to delay production expansions and cut prices, impacting their profitability. This shift indicates a more cautious approach to the EV transition as automakers grapple with making electric vehicles affordable for a broader market.

This appointment comes as Lucid reduces its full-year production guidance due to declining sales of its Lucid Air sedan and faces growing competition in the luxury EV market. Winterhoff's role will focus on improving operational efficiency, accelerating global expansion, and enhancing Lucid's go-to-market strategy amidst financial losses.

title: Deere must face US farmers' 'right-to-repair' lawsuits, judge rules
summary: A U.S. judge has ruled that Deere & Co must face consolidated lawsuits from farmers accusing the agricultural machinery maker of unlawfully conspiring to restrict maintenance and repair services. The judge rejected Deere's arguments for dismissal, stating that the plaintiffs met the legal thresholds to pursue their antitrust claims. This case is part of a broader "right-to-repair" movement challenging restrictive repair practices across various industries.

title: Did Tesla Retaliate Against Employees for Opposing Harassment?
summary: A federal lawsuit, EEOC v Tesla, Inc., alleges that Tesla tolerated widespread racial harassment against its black employees at its Fremont, California facilities between 2015 and 2023. The lawsuit further claims that Tesla retaliated against employees who opposed this harassment, with evidence suggesting various forms of workplace retaliation. The EEOC is seeking compensatory and punitive damages, back pay, and injunctive relief to reform Tesla's employment practices.

title: Geely and NIO agreement could see battery swapping tech on Volvo, Polestar and Lotus cars
summary: Geely Holding, the parent company of Volvo, Polestar, and Lotus, has signed an agreement with NIO to standardize battery technology and share battery-swapping functionality. This collaboration aims to expand NIO's existing battery-swapping network and could lead to future vehicles from Geely's brands integrating this technology, addressing range anxiety and improving EV charging consistency.

title: General Motors Is Pulling Away From Cruise. What Happens Now?
summary: General Motors is significantly reducing its investments in its robotaxi subsidiary, Cruise, following a series of incidents in San Francisco and federal probes that led to Cruise pulling its entire fleet. This move forces Cruise to rethink its expansion strategy, focusing on leaner operations, safety, and rebuilding trust, while GM shifts its focus back to traditional gas-powered vehicles. Auto industry analysts suggest Cruise will need to go back to basics, scale sustainably, and potentially partner with other companies to survive, despite some experts believing it still possesses valuable assets.

title: Nio (NIO) Stock Price Prediction in 2030: Bull, Base & Bear Forecasts
summary: This article provides bull, base, and bear case stock price predictions for Nio (NIO) in 2030, set at $72, $45, and $22 respectively. It details Nio's financial history since its 2018 IPO, highlighting significant losses, high R&D spending, and limited global presence as current challenges. The forecasts are based on projected revenue growth, potential profitability, and the company's ability to manage costs and expand into new markets.

title: Tesla’s Dojo Supercomputer Head Exits in Blow to Efforts
summary: Ganesh Venkataramanan, the head of Tesla's Dojo supercomputer project, has left the company after five years, posing a significant setback to the automaker's self-driving technology endeavors. Peter Bannon, a long-time Tesla director and former Apple executive, has taken over leadership of the project. This departure comes as Tesla plans to invest over $1 billion in Dojo by the end of 2024, a project analysts believe could add $500 billion to Tesla's valuation.

title: Atlas Lithium Fully Funded to First Production in 2024
summary: Atlas Lithium Corporation has secured full funding of US$49.5 million from lithium industry leaders Yahua and Chengxin, allowing it to proceed with its early revenue strategy. The company aims to begin initial production of spodumene concentrate by Q4 2024, utilizing modular Dense Media Separation (DMS) technology to expedite the process. This funding and strategic partnerships with major lithium hydroxide producers mark a significant step towards Atlas Lithium becoming a Tier 1 producer in Brazil's Lithium Valley.

title: Less stations, more power sharing: How ChargePoint intends to tackle the "big EV" business
summary: ChargePoint's VP of Sales in Europe, Andre ten Bloemendal, discusses the company's strategy for the heavy-duty EV charging market, emphasizing software solutions, telematics, and AI. ChargePoint aims to optimize charging for commercial fleets by moving beyond simply providing physical stations to intelligent power sharing and route planning. The company, which considers itself a software provider, recent acquisitions and product launches like Power Link 2.0 demonstrate its focus on pan-European, hardware-agnostic solutions for CPOs to address diverse commercial EV charging needs.

title: Lucid (LCID) Stock Price Prediction in 2030: Bull, Base & Bear Forecasts
summary: This article provides bull, base, and bear case predictions for Lucid (LCID) stock price in 2030, highlighting the company's reliance on Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund (PIF). It details Lucid's financial performance since its 2021 SPAC merger, including significant losses per vehicle and production challenges. The forecast suggests price targets of $26 (bull), $20 (base), and $8 (bear), emphasizing the need for cost control, market expansion, and continued PIF support for Lucid's survival and growth.

title: Tesla says California agency implicitly approved of its 'Autopilot' brand
summary: Tesla is defending its use of "Autopilot" and "self-driving" branding for its driver assistance features against a California regulatory action. The company argues that the California Department of Motor Vehicles (DMV) implicitly approved these terms by not taking action in previous investigations in 2014 and 2017. Tesla also highlighted that the DMV decided against prohibiting "self-driving" language in 2016 regulations.

title: iShares Self-Driving EV and Tech ETF (NY: IDRV
summary: This article provides a stock quote and related news for the iShares Self-Driving EV and Tech ETF (IDRV). It features current trading information, performance metrics, and a compilation of recent news headlines relevant to electric vehicles (EVs) and related technologies, including updates on companies like Rivian, Nio, Tesla, and Polestar. The article also includes a FAQ section answering common questions about the ETF.

title: Tesla to lose $7,500 consumer tax credits for some Model 3 vehicles
summary: Tesla's Model 3 Rear-Wheel Drive and Long Range vehicles will lose their federal tax credit of up to $7,500 starting December 31, 2023, due to new guidance under the U.S. Inflation Reduction Act. The new rules, effective January 1, aim to reduce reliance on China for the EV supply chain by imposing stricter battery sourcing requirements. Tesla urged customers to take delivery by December 31 to receive the full tax credit.

title: Tesla's second-generation robot is here! Flexibility has greatly increased, and you can also pinch eggs with two fingers...
summary: Tesla has unveiled its second-generation Optimus robot, showcasing significant advancements in flexibility, speed, and control. The new model features an 11-degree of freedom dexterous hand with tactile sensors on each finger, allowing for delicate tasks like pinching an egg. This iteration marks a shift towards enhanced movement and perception, with a focus on refined hardware and software upgrades, prompting industry interest in core hardware components and materials.

title: The second generation of Tesla's humanoid robot Optimus is here! Lose 20 pounds, do squats, and be able to “bungee jump”, more like a real person!
summary: Tesla has unveiled its second-generation humanoid robot, Optimus Gen 2, showcasing significant improvements in appearance, anthropomorphic activities, and physical capabilities. The new model is lighter, faster, and features enhanced dexterity, including the ability to handle delicate objects with improved tactile perception. Tesla CEO Elon Musk believes Optimus robots will eventually account for the majority of Tesla's long-term value, with an expected price under $20,000 and millions of units in production within 3-5 years.

title: FTC Adds Transparency to Car Buying With CARS Rule
summary: The Federal Trade Commission has finalized the CARS Rule, effective July 30, 2024, to enhance transparency in car buying and leasing by prohibiting misleading ads, needless add-ons, and bait-and-switch pricing. The rule outlines four key principles making misrepresentation illegal, requiring clear disclosure of offering prices, banning charges for add-ons that provide no benefit, and demanding express, informed consent from consumers for all charges. While the FTC presents it as a win for both consumers and honest dealers, the National Automobile Dealers Association has criticized it as bureaucratic overreach.

title: Company Offers $200,000 For Tesla Cybertruck
summary: Resell Calendar, a company specializing in resell news, has offered $200,000 to purchase a Tesla Cybertruck before the end of 2023. This offer is based on their prediction that early Cybertruck deliveries could resell for around this amount due to high demand and limited production. The company is confident in its assessment and is willing to extend the offer into 2024 if they don't acquire one this year.

title: Is ARK Innovation ETF a Top Buy Ahead of 2024?
summary: The ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK), managed by Cathie Wood, dramatically outperformed the S&P 500 in 2023 due to its focus on disruptive technologies. While its fees are lower than some growth funds, ARKK is more expensive and volatile than passively managed ETFs and has lagged behind them in longer-term performance. The article suggests that ARKK's 2023 momentum could continue into 2024, especially if interest rates are cut, but warns investors to monitor its performance due to its high volatility and fees, and the early-stage nature of its holdings.

title: 2024 Lotus Eletre R: Head-Spinning Power Meets Futuristic Tech
summary: The 2024 Lotus Eletre R, an all-electric hyper-SUV, is highlighted for its impressive power, acceleration, and advanced technology. Produced in Wuhan, China, by Lotus Technology, under the controlling interest of Chinese conglomerate Zhejiang Geely Holding Group, this vehicle marks a significant shift for the 75-year-old Lotus brand. The author notes its sophistication and efficiency rival those of Tesla.

title: S&P Global Mobility forecasts 88.3M auto sales in 2024
summary: S&P Global Mobility forecasts global new light vehicle sales to reach 88.3 million units in 2024, a 2.8% increase, despite challenges like high interest rates and consumer uncertainty. The report also projects global light vehicle production to slightly decline to 89.4 million units in 2024 while BEV sales are expected to reach 13.3 million units, representing 16.2% of global passenger vehicle sales.

title: Electric Cars With the Longest Range for 2023
summary: This article lists the top 18 electric vehicles for 2023 that offer a minimum of 300 miles of driving range, based on EPA estimates. It details each car's maximum range, price, and key features, starting with the Lucid Air at 516 miles. The list aims to help alleviate range anxiety for potential EV buyers by highlighting models with extended capabilities.

title: Blackboxstocks to acquire UK-based Evtec Aluminium by Q1 2024
summary: Dallas-based Blackboxstocks Inc. has agreed to acquire UK-based Evtec Aluminium Limited, with the acquisition expected to close in Q1 2024. Evtec, a supplier of essential components for the EV and luxury automotive markets, has a growing order book and plans for significant expansion, including future acquisitions and a U.S. launch. The merger will see Blackbox stockholders retain a significant interest and a contingent value right for their fintech operations, while Evtec will maintain operational control and strategic growth plans.

title: NIO to Get $2.2B Investment From Abu Dhabi Government Fund
summary: NIO Inc. has secured a $2.2 billion investment from CYVN Holdings LLC, an Abu Dhabi government fund, making CYVN its largest single shareholder with a 20.1% stake. This investment is crucial for NIO as it navigates challenges from a price war and aims to boost efficiency, having recently cut 10% of its workforce. The transaction follows a previous $738.5 million investment from CYVN in July and is expected to close by the end of December.

title: Here's The Terrible News Automakers Announced When They Hoped You Weren't Paying Attention
summary: The article highlights several major negative announcements from the automotive industry that were released just before Christmas, a time when companies hope to bury bad news. Key events include Cummins facing a $1.675 billion fine for diesel emissions fraud, GM issuing a stop-sale on the Chevy Blazer EV due to software issues, the shutdown of Hyperloop One, and Tesla recalling over 120,000 cars for faulty door latches in crashes. These incidents cover a range of problems from environmental violations to product quality and safety concerns.

title: Lithium and EV Market Update: Slower-Than-Expected Sales Growth Just a Bump in the Road
summary: The EV market experienced slower-than-expected sales growth in 2023, primarily due to market dynamics in China and elevated consumer costs, leading to a softening of lithium prices. Despite these challenges, EV adoption remains strong, with key pillars supporting its growth still intact. The article maintains a positive outlook for the specialty chemical and the EV revolution, expecting continued growth in the coming years.

title: Top 6 Portfolio Moves for 2024
summary: This article outlines six key portfolio adjustments investors should consider for 2024, focusing on balancing risk and maximizing returns. Recommendations include rebalancing portfolios, increasing bond allocations due to improved yields, optimizing cash holdings for competitive yields without over-allocating, maintaining inflation protection, and considering exposure to small-cap stocks. These moves aim to address market shifts, inflation concerns, and current valuation disparities.

title: China’s WeRide to test robot buses in Singapore
summary: Chinese autonomous vehicle company WeRide has received test licenses for robot buses in Singapore, furthering its global expansion plans for driverless transport. Singapore is a prime location for such testing due to its compact size and advanced infrastructure, and aims to use automated public transport to address an aging population and potential bus driver shortages. However, Chinese AV firms like WeRide face scrutiny in the United States over data privacy and national security concerns, limiting their market access there.

title: Uber to Deploy Teslas to Join Japan’s Taxi Market, Nikkei Says
summary: Uber Technologies Inc. plans to enter Japan's taxi market by deploying Tesla vehicles, according to the Nikkei newspaper. The company has already rolled out 30 Tesla Model Ys and aims to reach 100 electric cars in 2024. This move capitalizes on Japan's recent decision to introduce ridesharing, driven by a shortage of taxi drivers and increasing inbound tourism.

title: Tesla to roll out revamped Model Y from Shanghai plant - Bloomberg News
summary: Tesla is reportedly preparing to launch a revamped version of its Model Y from its Shanghai plant. This development was initially reported by Reuters in March, indicating that production for the updated Model Y is expected to commence in 2024. The news comes from anonymous sources familiar with the matter, as per Bloomberg News.

The article details key features, improvements, and specifications for each model, including the Chevrolet Bolt EV, Nissan Leaf, Mini Cooper SE, Hyundai Kona Electric, Mazda MX-30, Volkswagen ID.4, Tesla Model 3, Kia Niro EV, Hyundai Ioniq 5, and Toyota bZ4X.

title: Apple's stock underperformed top tech peers in 2023 due to longest revenue slide in 22 years
summary: Apple's stock underperformed its mega-cap tech peers in 2023, experiencing its longest revenue slide in 22 years with four consecutive quarters of declining sales. This decline was attributed to a combination of a weak economic environment for smartphones and PCs, and company-specific issues such as the lack of new iPad models and an Apple Watch sales ban. Despite a 49% stock rally for the year, Apple lagged behind competitors like Nvidia, Meta, Tesla, Amazon, Alphabet, and Microsoft.

title: Tesla sales top forecast in 4th quarter, but still fall below Chinese EV maker BYD
summary: Tesla exceeded its fourth-quarter delivery forecasts with 484,507 vehicles sold, bringing its 2023 total to over 1.8 million, a 38% increase from 2022. Despite this growth, Chinese automaker BYD surpassed Tesla in global EV sales for the fourth quarter, delivering 526,409 electric vehicles. Tesla's full-year sales, while strong, fell short of Elon Musk's initial 2 million vehicle goal, and analysts expect price cuts made throughout the year to impact profit margins.

title: Trimble executive: GNSS still core of PNT
summary: Olivier Casabianca, Vice President of Trimble Positioning Services, emphasizes that GNSS remains the core technology for geospatial industries like construction, agriculture, and transportation, providing fundamental precise positioning. He notes Trimble's expansion into new sectors such as telecom, IoT, and automotive, offering centimeter-accurate correction services globally via satellite. While acknowledging the rise of alternative PNT solutions like LEO constellations and mixed signals (Wi-Fi, video), Casabianca asserts that an ideal solution combines various technologies, with absolute GNSS positioning as the foundation.

title: Survey Says: Affordability, U.S. Manufacturing at Top of Mind for Shoppers
summary: A Cars Commerce survey reveals that affordability and U.S. manufacturing are top concerns for car shoppers in 2024, influenced by economic uncertainty and UAW strikes. Consumers are cost-conscious, navigating rising new car prices and a changing used car market, while Gen Z begins to impact buying trends, particularly favoring in-person purchases and EVs. The article also highlights evolving attitudes towards EVs, with consideration declining slightly due to cost and charging infrastructure concerns, and the continued debate around American-made vehicles.

title: Tesla's China-made EV sales grow 68.7% y/y in December
summary: Tesla sold 94,139 China-made electric vehicles in December, marking a 68.7% increase year-on-year, according to the China Passenger Car Association. This figure also represents a 14.2% rise from November deliveries. Chinese rival BYD also achieved strong results, delivering 341,043 passenger vehicles in December and a record 944,779 new energy vehicles in the fourth quarter.

title: Nio Reveals ET9 EV Flagship, Promising Ultra-Fast 600-KW Charging
summary: Nio has unveiled its new ET9 EV flagship, which features a 900-volt electrical architecture supporting rapid 600 kW charging and steer-by-wire technology. The large "landjet" design includes a 120.0-kWh battery, dual motors producing 697 hp, and advanced rear-seat amenities. Preorders are open in China, with deliveries expected in early 2025 and prices starting around $112,000.

title: Tesla loses EV sales crown to BYD in Q4, despite annual sales record
summary: Chinese automaker BYD surpassed Tesla in the fourth quarter of 2023 to become the world's largest electric vehicle seller, although Tesla maintained its lead for the full year with a record 1.8 million EV sales. This shift highlights increasing competition for Tesla in major markets, compounded by price reductions and declining profits. Meanwhile, traditional automakers like GM and Ford, along with EV startup Rivian, are scaling back their EV production goals due to lower-than-expected demand.

title: Exclusive: Tesla expands to suburb northeast of Austin as footprint grows
summary: Tesla Inc. has reportedly expanded its presence in the Austin area with a new facility in Hutto, though the specific purpose of the site remains unclear. This expansion is part of Tesla's growing footprint in Central Texas, further integrating the EV maker into the region's economy. The article hints at Tesla's continued development and its impact on the local job market.

summary: The ARK Autonomous Technology & Robotics ETF (ARKQ) is given a "hold" rating due to concerns about its high price-to-earnings ratio, concentrated allocation in growth stocks, and struggling long-term momentum. The article highlights potential risks if value stocks outperform growth stocks and points out the fund's high expense ratio and low trading volume. A technical analysis suggests potential trouble on the chart despite a recent bounce.

The ETF does not directly invest in TSLA stocks and pays dividends weekly, with its current assets under management at $810.22 million.

title: Can Lithium Prices Recover from 2023’s Slump?
summary: Lithium prices experienced a significant slump in 2023 due to a slowdown in EV sales, but analysts and asset managers like Sprott and Global X remain optimistic about a long-term recovery, citing increased exploration budgets and project delays. The article also explores potential challenges such as an oversupply of lithium and the emergence of alternative battery technologies like sodium-ion, while offering investment options in lithium producers and EV manufacturers.

title: 12 Lessons the Market Taught Investors in 2023
summary: The article outlines 12 crucial investment lessons from 2023, emphasizing that market forecasts are often inaccurate and valuations cannot be used for market timing. The piece also delves into the future prospects and potential challenges for the 60/40 portfolio, including inflation and moderate equity returns, while emphasizing its long-term resilience.

title: The Automotive Industry’s Response to AI and E-Vehicles: Traveling Forward
summary: The automotive industry is rapidly changing, with Artificial Intelligence (AI) playing a pivotal role in the evolution of electric vehicles (EVs). AI enhances everything from design and manufacturing efficiency to battery management, safety features, and the overall user experience. This integration also unlocks new revenue streams for manufacturers through personalized services and advanced driver-assistance systems.

These companies are poised to benefit from China's regulatory push, improving charging infrastructure, and increasing consumer adoption of New Energy Vehicles (NEVs).

title: Hertz will sell a third of its EV fleet to buy gasoline cars instead
summary: Hertz announced it will sell 20,000 electric vehicles (EVs) from its U.S. fleet, about a third of its global EV fleet, and reinvest the proceeds into gasoline-powered cars. The decision stems from higher repair costs for EVs, lower than anticipated demand impacting resale values, and the need to balance supply with customer preferences. This move is another blow to EV adoption targets and highlights ongoing challenges in the EV market.

title: Hertz to Sell 20,000 EVs in Shift Back to Gas-Powered Cars
summary: Hertz plans to sell 20,000 electric vehicles from its U.S. fleet throughout 2024, citing weak demand and high repair costs for EVs. The company will reinvest the proceeds into gas-powered cars to meet customer demand, marking a significant reversal from its 2021 strategy to purchase 100,000 Tesla vehicles. This move is expected to improve Hertz's financial results and free cash flow in 2024 and 2025.

title: Hertz early terminates 20,000 EVs from US fleet
summary: Hertz is selling off 20,000 electric vehicles from its US fleet, about one-third of its global EV fleet, due to lower-than-expected customer demand and high operating costs, particularly for collision repairs. This strategic move will cost the company an estimated $245 million in depreciation expenses, but Hertz expects to offset this by 2025 through improved revenue and reduced operating costs. Despite this, other major rental companies like Enterprise and SIXT reaffirm their commitment to expanding their EV offerings.

title: Puerto Rico is using residents’ home batteries to back up its grid
summary: Puerto Rico has launched a yearlong pilot program using residents' home batteries to provide backup power for its grid and prevent blackouts, potentially leading to North America's largest residential-storage virtual power plant. The program, involving Luma Energy and thousands of households, aims to modernize the grid, transition to clean energy, and reduce reliance on fossil fuel peaker plants. Participants receive financial compensation and can control their battery’s reserve amount, addressing both energy independence and grid stability.

title: Target seeing some disruption of India supplies due to Red Sea crisis
summary: U.S. retailer Target is experiencing minor disruptions to shipments from India and Pakistan due to the Red Sea crisis, which has caused delays and extended transit times for vessels. The company is working with shippers to redirect merchandise around the Suez Canal, with minimal expected impact on costs or delivery to customers. While some retailers are seeking alternative freight options, Target is not currently considering air freight and expects no impact on its spring merchandise, as most of its products from China are shipped directly across the Pacific.

title: Musk wants 25% voting control at Tesla before fulfilling AI goal
summary: Elon Musk has stated he requires at least 25% voting control at Tesla, nearly double his current stake, before he feels comfortable scaling the company to be a leader in AI and robotics. He indicated if this voting power is not granted, he would prefer to develop AI products outside of Tesla, a stance that governance experts suggest could infringe upon his CEO duties. The billionaire sold billions of dollars worth of Tesla shares in 2022, partly to fund his acquisition of Twitter.

title: Albemarle to cut staff, pause expansions amid falling lithium prices
summary: Albemarle, the world's largest lithium producer, announced plans to cut jobs and defer spending on expansion projects, including a U.S. refinery, due to falling lithium prices. This move is part of a broader cost-cutting effort expected to save $95 million annually. The company's shares dropped following the announcement, and analysts cut price targets amidst a global oversupply of lithium.

title: What Does 2024 Hold for the Magnificent Seven?
summary: The "Magnificent Seven" stocks, including Alphabet, Amazon, Apple, Meta, Microsoft, Nvidia, and Tesla, drove 73% of stock market gains in 2023 due to superior earnings growth and profit margins. While some analysts predict a pullback in 2024 due to potential overvaluation and cyclicality, others believe their strong forecasted earnings growth could lead to continued outperformance, especially while interest rates remain elevated. The article highlights contrasting fortunes early in 2024, with Nvidia surging and Tesla facing its worst January since its IPO.

title: How Do Hybrid Cars Work?
summary: Hybrid cars combine a gasoline engine with one or more electric motors to boost fuel efficiency and lower emissions, with various types offering different functionalities. The most common are parallel hybrids, which can run on electricity, gasoline, or a combination, and they use regenerative braking to recharge their high-voltage battery. Other types include plug-in hybrids (PHEVs) that can be charged from an outlet and have a larger electric-only range, series hybrids where the gas engine acts solely as a generator, and mild hybrids that offer limited electric assistance.

title: Charlotte-based Albemarle cuts back on ambitious plans, cuts jobs as lithium slumps
summary: Albemarle Corp. is slashing costs, cutting jobs, and deferring investments in new projects due to a slowdown in the electric vehicle market and slumping lithium prices. The company still plans to prioritize reopening its Kings Mountain mine, which could supply lithium for 1.2 million EVs annually. Albemarle aims to cut up to $500 million from capital spending and $95 million from annual operating costs.

title: EPA sends vehicle emissions reduction plan to White House for review
summary: The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) has submitted its proposal to significantly cut vehicle emissions through 2032 to the White House for review, a move expected to boost electric vehicle sales. While environmental groups and Tesla support tougher standards, major automakers warn the plan is "neither reasonable nor achievable" and could lead to substantial fines and premature phasing out of gas-powered cars. The EPA plans to finalize these new emissions rules by March, part of a broader set of regulations that could reshape the American automotive landscape.

title: S&P 500 Rises as US Stocks Build on Record High: Markets Wrap
summary: US stocks, including the S&P 500, continued to build on record highs, with traders evaluating robust economic signals and upcoming corporate earnings reports. Despite warnings of an overheated market, equities are gaining momentum due to expectations of Fed rate cuts and the sustained growth driven by artificial intelligence. Key earnings reports from companies like Netflix, Tesla, and Intel are anticipated this week.

Investors are encouraged to consider these opportunities before the market fully takes off.

title: Ark Invest’s aggressive accumulation of its Bitcoin ETF propels it into top 5 of ARKW portfolio
summary: Ark Invest has significantly increased its holdings of the Ark 21Shares Spot Bitcoin ETF (ARKB), making it a top-five asset in its Ark Next Generation Internet ETF (ARKW) portfolio. Simultaneously, the firm liquidated a substantial amount of its ProShares Bitcoin Strategy (BITO) shares, aligning with market expectations for a shift towards spot Bitcoin ETFs. This strategic reallocation has positioned ARKB as the third-largest spot Bitcoin ETF among the "Newborn nine," with assets under management exceeding $500 million.

title: Electric car maker Polestar to cut around 450 jobs globally
summary: Electric car manufacturer Polestar announced plans to cut approximately 450 jobs globally, representing about 15% of its workforce, due to "challenging market conditions." The company, like many other EV makers, has faced slow growth in electric vehicle demand, price cuts, and supply chain issues. This decision is part of a revised business plan aimed at achieving cash flow break-even by 2025 and reducing reliance on external funding.

title: AMD earnings are around the corner: This number could be the ‘swing factor’
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is set to report its fourth-quarter earnings, with significant attention on its MI300 AI accelerator and data-center GPU forecasts. The company is looking to gain a larger share of the AI chip market, currently dominated by Nvidia. This outlook was reinforced by Elon Musk's recent statement about Tesla's plans to purchase AMD chips.

title: G.M. Profits Hurt by Electric Car Business and Strike (Published 2024)
summary: General Motors reported that its fourth-quarter profit in 2023 was negatively impacted by a 40-day strike and a significant accounting charge related to its electric vehicle business. Despite a revenue increase, the company faced challenges from slower-than-expected EV growth, high battery cell costs, and manufacturing problems with its new Ultium technology. G.M. expects its electric cars and trucks to become profitable in the second half of the current year as battery costs decline.

title: GM’s ‘all-in’ electric future now includes gasoline
summary: General Motors (GM), which previously committed to an all-electric future by 2035, is reintroducing plug-in hybrid vehicles to its North American lineup. This decision reflects the challenges traditional automakers face in transitioning to EVs amid slowing sales and strict emissions regulations. While GM maintains its long-term EV goal, the move to hybrids offers a compromise, providing some environmental benefits while charging infrastructure develops and addressing customer demand for range flexibility.

title: Top 10: Electric Scooters
summary: This article lists the top 10 electric scooters available, highlighting their features, company origins, and environmental benefits. It emphasizes electric scooters as a convenient and sustainable alternative for short urban commutes, given that over 80% of trips are less than 10 kilometers. Companies like Lime, Bolt, Segway, and Unagi are featured, showcasing their contributions to micro-mobility and efforts to enhance user experience and safety.

title: Tesla sued by 25 California counties over hazardous waste handling
summary: Twenty-five California counties have filed a lawsuit against Tesla, alleging long-standing mishandling of hazardous waste at its facilities across the state. Despite its mission for sustainable energy, Tesla has faced repeated criticism for lax environmental practices, including prior EPA settlements and allegations of water contamination in Germany. The lawsuit claims Tesla intentionally disposed of hazardous waste improperly, failed to label materials accurately, and did not adequately train employees.

title: Volvo Cars to stop funding Polestar, may hand stake to Geely
summary: Volvo Cars announced it will cease funding Polestar Automotive Holding and may transfer its 48% stake to its top shareholder, China's Geely Holding. This decision, which led to a significant jump in Volvo's stock, comes as Polestar struggles and analysts criticized Volvo's involvement as a drain on resources. Geely has committed to providing full operational and financial support to Polestar going forward.

title: 2024 Lexus GX 550 Review: Go Anywhere, Do Anything
summary: The 2024 Lexus GX 550 is a significant overhaul from its predecessor, featuring a new platform, engine, and interior, transforming it into a luxurious yet highly capable off-road SUV. It introduces an expedition-ready Overtrail trim, a more powerful V-6 twin-turbo engine, and an enhanced interior with modern tech and comfort. While more expensive, its blend of luxury, refinement, and off-road prowess positions it strongly against competitors like the Land Rover Defender.

title: Tesla recalls nearly all its U.S. vehicles for incorrect font size on warning lights while NHTSA probes power steering loss
summary: Tesla is recalling nearly all of its U.S. vehicles due to an incorrect font size on warning lights, affecting millions of cars. This recall comes as the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) is also investigating reports of power steering loss in Tesla vehicles. The issue with the warning lights concerns visibility and compliance with safety standards, while the power steering investigation highlights ongoing safety scrutiny.

title: Hertz will stop buying EVs from Polestar, too
summary: Hertz is backtracking on its commitment to purchasing electric vehicles, now pausing plans to acquire 65,000 EVs from Polestar. This follows an earlier decision to sell 20,000 Teslas, as the rental company has faced higher than expected depreciation, price cuts, and repair costs, leading to an estimated loss of over $245 million on its EV agreements. Despite booming overall EV sales, Hertz's operational model and financial losses have led it to reconsider its aggressive EV fleet expansion goals.

title: TSLL ETF: Leveraging Tesla's Volatility (NASDAQ:TSLL)
summary: The Direxion Daily TSLA Bull 1.5X Shares ETF (TSLL) is a leveraged fund designed to provide 1.5 times the daily performance of Tesla, Inc. shares, allowing investors to capitalize on Tesla's highly volatile stock price for short-term gains. While TSLL offers opportunities for traders due to its leverage, it carries high risks including volatility decay and lack of diversification, making it unsuitable for long-term holding. The author, Michael A. Gayed, CFA, notes the surprising demand for single-stock ETFs and its relevance to his "risk-on" or "risk-off" market signals.

title: Europe’s Biggest Software Company Stops Offering Teslas to Employees
summary: SAP SE, Europe's largest software company, has decided to stop offering Tesla electric vehicles to its employees as company cars. The decision stems from Tesla's fluctuating prices, which complicate financial planning and increase risks for SAP's fleet management. This move is a setback for Elon Musk's efforts to expand Tesla's presence in the corporate car market.

title: Toyota investing $1.3 billion in Kentucky to build all-electric, three-row SUV
summary: Toyota Motor is investing $1.3 billion in its Kentucky plant to produce a new all-electric, three-row SUV for the U.S. market, with production expected to begin between late 2025 and early 2026. This investment is part of Toyota's broader plan to inject $35 billion into battery-electric vehicles through 2030, even as consumer adoption of EVs has been slower than anticipated, prompting some automakers to scale back their EV investments. Toyota emphasizes that while EVs are a solution for carbon neutrality, they are not the sole approach, as the company continues to invest in hybrids, plug-in hybrids, and hydrogen fuel cells.

title: ¿Cuáles son los CEDEAR más elegidos por los argentinos?
summary: Certificados de Depósito Argentinos (CEDEAR) are a popular investment option in Argentina for diversifying portfolios and hedging against peso devaluation by investing in foreign companies. In January, Coca-Cola (KO) was the most chosen CEDEAR, followed by Apple (AAPL) and Tesla (TSLA). Recent changes by Banco Comafi regarding conversion ratios aim to make CEDEARs more accessible to a wider range of investors by lowering entry prices.

title: Hertz shares regain traction after wider-than-expected Q4 loss
summary: Hertz Global's shares rallied after initially dropping following a wider-than-expected Q4 loss of $348 million and an adjusted loss-per-share of $1.36. The car rental company attributed the losses to headwinds related to its electric vehicle fleet, including depreciation expenses, and announced plans to sell 20,000 EVs and acquire more gas-powered cars to regain "operational cadence."

title: Best Electric Vehicle of 2024
summary: The Hyundai Ioniq 5 has been named the Best Electric Vehicle of 2024 by Cars.com for the second consecutive year, praised for its balance of attributes, roomy cabin, and practical design. Finalists included the Lucid Air and Ford F-150 Lightning, recognized for their performance, range, and unique features. Several other EVs from BMW, Genesis, Hummer, Hyundai, Mercedes-EQ, and Porsche were also nominated for the award.

title: One of America’s last aluminum smelters just closed. Let’s rescue Kentucky’s from that fate.
summary: The article discusses the decline of the primary aluminum industry in the U.S. and specifically in Kentucky, threatening the clean energy supply chain. It highlights the closure and idling of smelters, attributing the issue primarily to the high cost of electricity from fossil fuels and the need for a shift to clean energy. The author calls for leveraging federal incentives, like those from the Inflation Reduction Act, to modernize smelters and transition to renewable energy to save the remaining plants and secure clean energy jobs in Kentucky.

title: Colorado regulator’s vision for Xcel virtual power plant: Tariff-driven, 50 MW and online by this fall
summary: A Colorado regulator is pushing Xcel Energy to develop a 50-MW virtual power plant (VPP) by year-end, incorporating a new "prosumer tariff" to incentivize distributed energy resources. While Xcel supports the concept, they have expressed concerns about the ambitious timeline, suggesting 18 months for Distributed Energy Management System (DERMS) implementation. Stakeholders believe a third-party DERMS could accelerate the VPP launch, with recommendations for a fully-scaled program expected by November 2026.

title: Protolabs surpasses $500 million in annual revenue
summary: Protolabs announced record annual revenue exceeding $500 million for the first time in its 25-year history, driven by growth in digital manufacturing services for electric vehicle and aeronautics industries. The company reported strong financial results for the full year 2023, including significant increases in Protolabs Network revenue and net income compared to the previous year. This growth is attributed to their flexible manufacturing offerings and a shift towards higher-volume production beyond traditional prototyping.

title: Panasonic Energy Partners with NOVONIX for Sustainable Synthetic Graphite Supply in EV Battery Production
summary: Panasonic Energy has signed a binding off-take agreement with NOVONIX Limited for the supply of sustainable synthetic graphite, a key component for EV battery anodes. This partnership aims to strengthen Panasonic Energy's North American supply chain and significantly reduce the carbon footprint of its lithium-ion battery production by leveraging NOVONIX's innovative, energy-efficient graphite manufacturing technology. The agreement is a crucial step in Panasonic Energy's goal to cut the carbon footprint of its EV battery supply chain by 50% by 2031.

title: (TSLA) Tesla Rakes In $9 Billion From Carmakers Failing to Sell Enough EVs
summary: Tesla has accumulated nearly $9 billion in revenue from selling regulatory credits since 2009, including $1.79 billion last year. This lucrative business comes from other carmakers failing to meet emissions standards, a revenue stream that Tesla itself once expected to diminish.

title: Tim Aten Knows: Tesla building service center in Naples area
summary: Tesla Inc. has acquired an industrial property in East Naples for $7 million to construct a nearly 45,000-square-foot service and sales center, projected to open in the second quarter of 2025. This will be Tesla's first self-developed service center, featuring over 20 service bays and a focus on online sales, with only a curated selection of vehicles on display. The facility will also sell and install solar roof and home power systems, addressing a high demand for Tesla services in the Naples area.

title: Tesla temporarily slashes prices for some Model Y cars in the US
summary: Tesla has temporarily reduced prices for some Model Y vehicles in the U.S. until February 29, 2024, following a similar move in Germany. The price cuts affect the Model Y rear-wheel drive and Long Range variants, representing discounts of 2.3% and 2% respectively. This strategic pricing adjustment comes amid cooling demand for EVs, increasing competition from more affordable electric vehicles, and a warning from Tesla of "notably lower" sales growth this year, further impacting the company's margins.

title: GraniteShares 2x Long NVDA Daily ETF (NVDL) Short Interest & Short Float | Updated Mar 2026
summary: This article details the short interest and short float for GraniteShares 2x Long NVDA Daily ETF (NVDL) as of December 15, 2025. NVDL had 14.34 million shares sold short, representing 29.90% of the public float, marking a 32.88% increase from the prior report. The short interest ratio is 1.2 days to cover, indicating a relatively quick covering period based on average trading volume.

title: US auto retailers' falling new vehicle margins compress quarterly profits
summary: US auto retailers are experiencing squeezed profits due to falling new vehicle margins, driven by increased price cuts and incentives to attract buyers. Higher vehicle production has eased supply, further impacting dealer margins, while challenges in the EV market, including lower prices and higher maintenance costs, also contribute to the downturn. Despite these pressures, retailers are finding some profit relief in their after-market service units.

title: 2024 Could Be a Buyer’s Market — if You’re Buying New: Report
summary: A new report from Cars Commerce indicates that 2024 could be a buyer's market for new cars due to normalizing production and increased inventory, particularly for vehicles under $30,000. Conversely, the used-car market faces a looming shortage of late-model vehicles, suggesting a continued challenge for buyers in that segment. Electric vehicle shoppers are in an even stronger position due to increased EV inventory and declining demand leading to lower prices and more incentives.

title: YieldMax Magnificent 7 Fund of Option Income ETFs
summary: This article provides a detailed profile of the YieldMax Magnificent 7 Fund of Option Income ETFs (YMAG), including its price, category, expenses, inception date, and top holdings. It also presents analysis on its performance, fund flows, AUM influence, and ESG scores, comparing it against ETF Database category averages and FactSet segment averages. The fund's primary holdings are option income strategy ETFs for individual "Magnificent 7" stocks.

title: Here's How Much Money You'd Have if You Invested $7,000 in the "Magnificent Seven" in 2017
summary: This article examines the investment returns if one had invested $7,000 across the "Magnificent Seven" stocks (Alphabet, Amazon, Apple, Meta Platforms, Microsoft, Nvidia, Tesla) in 2017. It highlights the significant growth these companies experienced, with Nvidia and Tesla showing the highest returns, and compares this performance to an investment in the S&P 500 over the same period. The author concludes that these established growth stocks can still offer good long-term returns despite their current size.

title: XCHG looks to juice up its business with New York IPO
summary: XCHG, a European fast-charging station maker founded by former Tesla engineers, is pursuing a Nasdaq IPO to raise capital, targeting a modest valuation initially but hoping for an upside surprise. The company differentiate itself with fast-charging stations capable of feeding unused power back into the grid, a key feature of its Net Zero Series. Despite recent net losses due to increased operating expenses, XCHG aims to leverage its strong position in the European market and invest IPO proceeds into a new U.S. factory and global expansion.

title: Global Commercial Energy Storage Market Analysis and Outlook to 2028: A $4.91 Billion Opportunity
summary: The global commercial energy storage market, valued at $3.25 billion in 2022, is projected to reach $4.91 billion by 2028 with a 6.25% CAGR. This growth is driven by advancements in battery technology, particularly lithium-ion, and the increasing integration of renewable energy sources, with Europe leading the market due to supportive policies. Key players like Tesla and LG Chem are innovating to enhance storage capabilities and promote sustainable energy.

title: Eli Lilly, Novo Nordisk get growth stock status on weight-loss drug boost
summary: Eli Lilly and Novo Nordisk have achieved growth stock status due to the explosive demand for their weight-loss drugs, GLP-1 agonists, pushing their shares to record highs and valuing them similarly to high-growth tech companies like Tesla and Nvidia. Both companies exhibit significant valuation premiums over their healthcare peers, with analysts expecting substantial revenue growth in the coming years. While their success is largely attributed to their weight-loss drugs, Eli Lilly also benefits from diversification through acquisitions and cancer drug sales.

title: What's New With Electric Vehicles for 2024?
summary: The 2024 model year sees a continued influx of new electric vehicles, despite concerns over price and higher interest rates. While some affordable EVs like the Chevrolet Bolt have been discontinued, new models like the Fiat 500e are launching at under $35,000. The article details various new EV introductions for 2024, including models from Acura, Audi, BMW, Chevrolet, Fiat, GMC, Honda, Kia, Lotus, Mercedes-Maybach, Polestar, Porsche, Rolls-Royce, Tesla, and VinFast, highlighting their specifications, ranges, and pricing.

title: Chinese EV maker Xpeng unveils capex blitz as it faces ‘new phase of bloodbath’
summary: Chinese EV maker Xpeng plans to invest a record 3.5 billion yuan (US$486 million) in capital expenditure during 2024 to develop intelligent cars and will hire 4,000 new workers. The company's CEO, He Xiaopeng, announced that Xpeng will launch 30 new models over the next three years, anticipating a "new phase of bloodbath" in the fiercely competitive EV market. Despite rapid growth in EV sales last year, a slowdown is expected, intensifying the struggle among more than 200 Chinese EV manufacturers.

title: The Companies We’re Watching During Proxy-Voting Season 2024
summary: The 2024 proxy-voting season is marked by increased shareholder activism, particularly focusing on environmental, social, and governance (ESG) issues like climate impacts, workers' rights, and AI oversight. Key companies like Tesla, Apple, and Applied Materials are under scrutiny for their board governance, compensation, and ethical AI guidelines, amid growing regulatory momentum for climate disclosures. The season also highlights new opportunities for individual investors and increased scrutiny of "anti-ESG" resolutions.

title: Lotus Tech completes merger with LCAA, to trade as LOT on Nasdaq
summary: Lotus Technology Inc., a luxury electric vehicle manufacturer, has completed its merger with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (LCAA). The combined entity, now named Lotus Technology Inc., will begin trading its American depositary shares on Nasdaq under the ticker symbol "LOT" on February 23, 2024. This U.S. listing is a significant step in Lotus Tech's global expansion strategy for sustainable luxury mobility.

title: Here's How Much Money You'd Have if You Invested $10,000 Into the Ark Innovation ETF's Top 5 Stocks in 2019
summary: This article examines the hypothetical returns of investing $10,000 into the top five holdings of the Ark Innovation ETF (ARKK) as of January 2019, compared to investing the same amount directly into the ETF. It reveals that direct investment in the individual top stocks, particularly Nvidia and Tesla, would have yielded significantly higher returns despite some underperforming assets like Invitae. The author suggests that individual stock selection from the ETF's holdings can lead to better outcomes than investing in the entire fund, though the ETF offers diversification.

title: 2024 Honda Prologue Review: A Wrapper-Fresh EV
summary: The 2024 Honda Prologue is Honda's first mass-market EV, developed in partnership with GM and based on GM's Ultium platform, specifically sharing architecture with the Chevrolet Blazer EV. While it presents as a Honda and offers competitive styling and features like wireless Apple CarPlay and Android Auto (unlike its GM counterparts), its powertrain and interior structure are largely GM-sourced. The Prologue aims to be a solid, although pricey, electric SUV that bridges the gap until Honda releases its own ground-up EVs around 2026.

title: YieldMax Adds MSTR ETF to Option Income Lineup
summary: YieldMax has launched the YieldMax MSTR Option Income Strategy ETF (MSTY), adding MicroStrategy Inc. to its suite of synthetic covered call ETFs. MSTY aims to generate monthly income through options premiums and U.S. Treasuries, though it does not provide full exposure to the underlying stock's upside price performance. This new offering expands YieldMax's product line to 21 ETFs, with a 0.99% expense ratio.

title: Episode #522: Wes Gray & Robert Elwood on How to Convert a Separately Managed Account (SMA) to an ETF
summary: This podcast episode features Wes Gray and Robert Elwood discussing their experience in converting Separately Managed Accounts (SMAs) to Exchange Traded Funds (ETFs) via Section 351, including a recent conversion of $770 million. They delve into the process, advantages, and challenges of such conversions, highlighting tax efficiencies and the broader implications for financial advisors and family offices. The discussion also touches upon the future of asset management and the rising trend of SMA to ETF conversions.

title: Lotus Tech Is Going Public Today At A $5.5 Billion Valuation
summary: Lotus Technology is going public on the Nasdaq under the ticker ‘LOT’ today, February 23, 2024, after finalizing its merger with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp, valuing the company at approximately $5.5 billion. Despite EV market uncertainty, Lotus has raised over $880 million and plans to accelerate its growth in the luxury BEV market. The company, known for its all-electric Eletre SUV and Emeya sedan, aims for a 100% electric product portfolio by 2027, including a new sports car successor to the Emira.

title: Tesla could break ground on big EV factory near Monterrey, Mexico, in March
summary: Nuevo León's governor stated that Tesla Inc. could begin construction on its Gigafactory Mexico as early as March, following discussions with company leadership. The governor noted that the factory in his state would be larger than initially planned and mentioned ongoing permitting issues related to electricity, water, and environmental impact. This development comes after CEO Elon Musk had tempered expectations regarding the timeline, citing concerns about the global economy and high interest rates.

title: Cathie Wood Predicts Innovation Could Be Worth $220 Trillion by 2030
summary: Cathie Wood, CEO of ARK Invest, discussed how the convergence of five technological platforms—multiomic sequencing, robotics, energy storage, AI, and blockchain—could lead to innovation worth $220 trillion by 2030. She highlighted robotaxis as a prime example of this convergence, predicting a $10 trillion revenue burst and a significant impact on Tesla's valuation. Wood also reiterated her bullish stance on Bitcoin, especially after the approval of spot Bitcoin ETFs, and sees it as a new asset class.

title: Lotus Cars Are Legit. What We Thought After Driving an Eletre.
summary: Lotus Technology has become a publicly traded electric vehicle manufacturer and a significant player in the luxury EV market. The article discusses this development, highlighting the author's driving experience with the Lotus Eletre S, an electric luxury car with a powerful engine.

title: Vietnam's VinFast seeks India EV import duty cut as plant construction starts
summary: Vietnamese electric vehicle manufacturer VinFast has requested that India temporarily reduce import duties on its cars for about two years. This reduction would allow Indian customers to become familiar with VinFast's products while the company's local manufacturing plant in Tamil Nadu is under construction. VinFast plans to start production by mid-2025, initially for domestic sales and then for exports, with an intended investment of up to $2 billion, including $500 million in the first five years.

title: Lotus Technology completes a satisfactory IPO
summary: Lotus Technology, a luxury electric vehicle manufacturer, successfully completed its IPO on Nasdaq by merging with SPAC L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp. The new entity, valued at around $7 billion, began trading on February 23, 2024, under the symbol "LOT". Shares rose 2.15% to $13.80, giving it a market capitalization of $9.3 billion.

title: Now, Elon Musk-led Tesla’s rival VinFast wants India to reduce import duties on its electric cars
summary: Vietnamese electric vehicle maker VinFast has asked India to temporarily lower import duties on its cars for two years. The company, which is building a manufacturing plant in Tamil Nadu, proposes reducing the 100% import duty on fully-built electric vehicles to 70-80% for a limited number of cars. This move aims to familiarize customers with VinFast's products while the company awaits the government's decision amid opposition from domestic automakers.

title: Apple Kills Its Electric Car Project (Published 2024)
summary: Apple has canceled its decade-long project to develop an electric, self-driving car, a move announced internally to employees. This decision shifts many of the car project's staff to Apple's artificial intelligence division, marking a rare public setback for the company. The car, codenamed Titan, was intended to rival Tesla's E.V.s and bolster Apple's innovation legacy, but faced development challenges and financial investment of billions of dollars.

title: Inside Active Growth ETF TGRW’s Red Hot Returns
summary: The T. Rowe Price Growth Stock ETF (TGRW) has delivered impressive returns, outperforming its category averages with a 51% return over one year. This active growth ETF, which charges 52 basis points, strategically seeks out firms with strong cash flows and earnings growth, allowing it to adapt to market changes. TGRW's tactical underweighting of companies like Tesla (TSLA) compared to passive growth ETFs has contributed to its superior performance.

title: Novonix eyes North American market for anode and cathode materials
summary: Novonix is an international lithium-ion battery technology company that develops and produces anode and cathode materials in North America, addressing high demand. The company, founded by Dr. Chris Burns, integrates three divisions and traces its origins to Professor Jeff Dahn's pioneering LIB research at Dalhousie University. Novonix focuses on sustainable, high-performance battery materials, advanced testing equipment like Ultra-High Precision Coulometry (UHPC), and is expanding its production capabilities in the US, supported by significant investments and partnerships to localize the EV supply chain.

title: Destination Progressive… or One of These Other Carriers
summary: Progressive is focusing on its "Destination Era strategy" to meet broader customer needs, even if it means selling competitors' policies through its Progressive Advantage Agency (PAA). This strategy generates stable commission revenue and strengthens customer retention and brand loyalty, driving more customers to ultimately bundle Progressive-underwritten products. Despite initially facing an uncertain market in 2023, Progressive ended the year meeting its profitability target and plans to increase advertising spend and roll back underwriting restrictions in 2024, feeling more confident about growth.

title: The Rise of Humanoids, Explained
summary: This article discusses the rapid advancements and growing market potential of humanoid robots, driven by technological convergence of robotics and AI, and decreasing hardware costs. It highlights how humanoids are poised for mainstream adoption in industrial and household settings, potentially creating a multi-trillion dollar market by 2035, and identifies investment opportunities across the supply chain. The piece also details the challenges and improvements needed for widespread adoption, comparing the trajectory of humanoids to that of early automobiles and collaborative robots.

title: Invest Like A Congressional Insider With NANC And KRUZ?
summary: This article examines ETFs like NANC and KRUZ which aim to track congressional members' investments, based on the premise that politicians might act on insider information. The author, Richard Shaw, concludes that these ETFs' methodologies do not deliver the implied benefit and are not good investment choices, despite some members of Congress having successful stock investments. The analysis stems from a client's inquiry into the viability of such investment strategies.

title: Surging Lithium Market Growth Expected Through 2030 Due to Increasing Adoption of Cleaner Energy Sources
summary: The global lithium market is projected to grow significantly from $22.2 billion in 2023 to $89.9 billion in 2030, driven by the increasing adoption of cleaner energy sources, particularly electric vehicles. This growth is fueled by advancements in rechargeable battery technology and stricter environmental regulations pushing for reduced carbon emissions. Several lithium mining companies, including Lithium South Development Corporation, Lithium Americas (Argentina) Corp., Sigma Lithium Corporation, Piedmont Lithium Inc., and Standard Lithium Ltd., are positioned to capitalize on this expanding market.

title: Here’s the One Chinese EV Stock Worth Buying and Holding
summary: Li Auto (NASDAQ: LI) is the only profitable Chinese EV manufacturer among its peers like Nio and Xpeng, primarily due to its focus on premium hybrid electric SUVs that appeal to families and mitigate range anxiety. The company reported significant net income growth of 2,068% year-over-year in Q4 2023, driven by a 184.6% increase in vehicle deliveries. While it offers premium features and innovative technology, Li Auto issued downside guidance for Q1 2024.

title: Tesla shares fall after Morgan Stanley cuts price target on weakening EV demand
summary: Tesla's shares declined after Morgan Stanley lowered its price target due to weakening electric-vehicle demand, particularly in China, and an aging product lineup. Analyst Adam Jonas cited an oversaturated Chinese EV market and persistent price competition, while other factors like supply chain disruptions and the lack of a mass-market vehicle also weigh on the stock.

title: The Optimists Were Right About FANG Stocks
summary: The article discusses how the FANG stocks (Facebook, Amazon, Netflix, Google) have significantly outperformed other investments since their popularization in 2013, contrary to skeptics' predictions. It highlights that the strong stock market performance was backed by dominant business fundamentals, not just speculative enthusiasm. The author questions whether similar skepticism regarding the current "Magnificent Seven" stocks will also prove incorrect.

title: Charted: Ozempic-maker Novo Nordisk surges on new obesity drug trial results
summary: Novo Nordisk, the maker of Ozempic and Wegovy, saw its shares reach a record high after announcing positive early results for its new weight-loss drug candidate, amycretin. The company's stock has surged over 88% in the past year, making it the 12th largest company globally by market value. Amycretin showed patients losing 13.1% of their weight in preliminary trials and is expected to enter Phase 2 trials later this year.

title: Training Techs for EV Duty
summary: Lincoln Tech has opened a new Tesla Training Center in Maryland to address the growing need for EV-qualified technicians, with the program offered at no cost to accepted students. The article highlights the safety concerns and specialized training required for EV maintenance and the industry-wide effort by vocational schools and companies like Manheim to train technicians and basic familiarization for a broader range of dealership employees.

title: The 3 Most Undervalued Lithium Stocks to Buy in March 2024
summary: This article identifies three undervalued lithium stocks for March 2024: Atlas Lithium (ATLX), Sigma Lithium (SGML), and American Lithium (AMLI). Despite a recent dip in lithium prices, the sector is poised for significant long-term growth driven by demand from EV and tech industries. The chosen companies are highlighted for their production expansion plans, strategic positions, and strong analyst price targets, suggesting substantial potential gains for investors.

title: Stardust Power: Securing a domestic, sustainable supply of battery-grade lithium for a clean future
summary: Stardust Power is establishing a large-scale lithium refinery to secure a domestic and sustainable supply of battery-grade lithium for the US, aiming to meet up to 10% of domestic demand. The company emphasizes sustainable production methods, including using brine feedstock and designing fully electric production trains, to support the clean energy transition and reduce reliance on foreign sources. Stardust Power plans to merge with GPAC, complete a feasibility study, and achieve an annual production of 50,000 tonnes of battery-grade lithium from two trains.

title: Tesla settles with Black worker who won two trials over racist discrimination
summary: Tesla has settled a racial discrimination lawsuit with Owen Diaz, a Black former elevator operator at its Fremont factory, who had twice won damages in federal court. Diaz's attorneys confirmed an "amicable resolution" but kept the terms confidential. The settlement comes amid ongoing legal challenges for Tesla regarding alleged widespread racist discrimination, and criticism of CEO Elon Musk's handling of hate speech on X.

title: Tesla to pay $42 million for employee crash that injured motorcyclist
summary: An Indianapolis jury found Tesla and one of its employees partially liable for a 2017 crash involving a company-owned Ford truck and a motorcyclist. The motorcyclist sustained severe injuries, including a partial amputation and traumatic brain injury. Tesla and the employee, Kyle Kaszuba, must pay over $42 million in damages after the initial $60 million award was reduced due to the victim being found 30% liable.

title: Tesla to raise US prices for Model Y vehicles by $1,000 on April 1
summary: Tesla announced it would increase prices for all Model Y cars in the U.S. by $1,000 starting April 1. This follows a previous price hike of $1,000 for the Model Y rear-wheel drive and long-range vehicles on March 1. The company has adjusted prices frequently, temporarily cutting them in February after reductions in Europe and China.

title: Major electric vehicle lithium project paused. Other SC battery recycling investments continue.
summary: Albemarle Corp. has paused its $1.3 billion lithium processing plant project in Chester County, South Carolina, due to low lithium prices and slowing electric vehicle demand. This decision impacts job creation but contrasts with continued investment in battery recycling projects by companies like Cirba Solutions and Redwood Materials. The pause highlights the volatility in the EV supply chain while South Carolina continues its efforts in the battery sector.

title: Pumping the brakes
summary: Major electric vehicle lithium projects in South Carolina are facing delays due to a significant drop in lithium prices and slower than anticipated EV demand. Albemarle Corp. has paused its $1.3 billion lithium processing plant in Chester County, and Cirba Solutions has yet to break ground on its $300 million battery recycling facility. Meanwhile, Redwood Materials is continuing with the construction of its $3.5 billion battery recycling facility in Berkeley County, providing a contrasting example of ongoing investment in the state's battery industry.

title: New EV policy to help launch many premium-quality SUVs: VinFast Auto
summary: VinFast Auto announced that India's new EV policy, which offers import duty concessions for companies investing a minimum of USD 500 million in manufacturing, will enable them to launch a diverse range of premium, eco-friendly SUVs at accessible prices. VinFast India CEO Pham Sanh Chau stated that the company has pledged USD 500 million for its manufacturing facility in Tamil Nadu. The policy is aimed at boosting EV manufacturing in India and attracting global players like Tesla.

title: All Eyes on AI: Automotive Tech on Full Display at GTC 2024
summary: The GTC 2024 conference is showcasing the latest advancements in AI and automotive technology, with a focus on NVIDIA DRIVE Thor. Major EV manufacturers like BYD, Hyper, and XPENG are integrating DRIVE Thor into their next-generation vehicles, alongside trucking and robotaxi companies. The conference also highlights new tech such as Omniverse Cloud APIs for AV development and generative AI for in-vehicle experiences.

title: AMZZ Stock Price | GraniteShares 2x Long AMZN Daily ETF
summary: This article provides detailed information on the GraniteShares 2x Long AMZN Daily ETF (AMZZ), including its current stock price, trading exchange (NASDAQ), and key financial statistics like expense ratio and assets. It also offers insights into its performance, holdings, and technical analysis, helping investors assess its potential. The ETF aims to provide 2x leveraged daily returns designed to correspond to the performance of Amazon.com stock.

title: The 5 Biggest Buyers of Nio (NIO) Stock
summary: Shares of Nio (NIO) are down following news that Tesla would cut its Model 3 and Y production in China. Despite this, hedge funds increased their exposure to NIO stock by 30% in Q4. Voloridge Investment Management, HSBC, Point72, Millennium Management, and Jane Street were among the biggest buyers.

title: BlackRock's Fink deserves a standing ovation, Galaxy Digital CEO says
summary: The CEO of Galaxy Digital stated that BlackRock's Larry Fink deserves a standing ovation for his work, specifically mentioning it in the context of a bitcoin event. This article appears within a section discussing Exchange Traded Funds and also includes other featured stories related to institutional investing.

title: Dow announces intent to invest in new world-scale carbonate solvents facility in the U.S.
summary: Dow announced its intent to invest in a new world-scale carbonate solvents facility on the U.S. Gulf Coast to meet increasing demand in the electric vehicle and energy storage markets. This facility will capture over 90% of CO2 from its manufacturing process to produce critical components for lithium-ion batteries. The project received support and was selected for award negotiations by the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) Office of Clean Energy Demonstrations.

title: Tesla Staff Ordered to Demo Full Self-Driving Mode to Buyers
summary: Tesla CEO Elon Musk has mandated that all staff in North America conducting vehicle handovers must install and demonstrate the Full Self-Driving (FSD) driver-assistance technology to buyers. This "hard requirement" includes a "short test ride" to familiarize customers with FSD's capabilities, despite Musk acknowledging it will slow down deliveries. The directive aims to highlight FSD's effectiveness and justify its $12,000 cost amidst ongoing scrutiny regarding its marketing and functionality.

title: Century Aluminum to build green aluminium smelter in the US with DOE grant
summary: Century Aluminum has been selected to receive up to $500 million in Bipartisan Infrastructure Law and Inflation Reduction Act funding from the US Department of Energy to construct a new green aluminum smelter in the US. This project aims to be the first new US primary aluminum smelter in 45 years, potentially doubling the nation's current primary aluminum production and strengthening domestic supply chains for critical materials. The initiative is part of the Industrial Demonstrations Program (IDP) to decarbonize energy-intensive industries and foster a sustainable future for aluminum production.

title: Copper’s Red-Hot Rally, Fueled by EVs, Renewables and a Smelter Squeeze
summary: Copper futures have reached an 11-month high, driven by supply risks, a strong global economic outlook, and potential output cuts from Chinese smelters facing a crisis over processing fees. Demand for copper is significantly boosted by the renewable energy sector and electric vehicles, with the U.S. government recognizing its critical role. Further tailwinds for copper are expected from reshoring efforts in developed economies and improving Purchasing Manager’s Index figures.

title: Tesla to offer US customers a free 1-month trial of Full Self Driving
summary: Tesla will offer a free one-month trial of its Full Self Driving (FSD) feature to all eligible U.S. customers to boost subscriptions and revenue. This move aims to showcase FSD's capabilities, as many do not realize how well it works, and could help Tesla address its dwindling operating margins. Despite offering advanced features, FSD is still classified as a Level-2 autonomous system, requiring constant driver supervision, and has been subject to regulatory scrutiny and recalls due to safety concerns.

title: EV Maker VinFast Plugs Into Thailand: 15 Dealers Sign On To Drive EV Revolution In The Land Of Smiles
summary: VinFast Auto Ltd. has signed agreements with 15 dealers in Thailand to establish 22 showrooms, primarily in the Greater Bangkok Area, to expand its electric vehicle distribution network. The company aims to leverage Thailand's high EV adoption rates and charging infrastructure to introduce its VF e34, VF 5, VF 6, and VF 7 models, along with electric scooters. This move is part of VinFast's broader global expansion strategy, targeting over 50 countries in 2024, including the construction of manufacturing plants in the U.S., India, and Indonesia.

title: This year’s ranking of EV carmakers from most to least ‘clean’: Report
summary: A new report by a coalition of labor and environmental groups ranked 18 top automakers on the cleanliness of their supply chains, finding that Ford and Mercedes-Benz lead, with Tesla making a significant jump to third place. The report emphasizes that an EV's "cleanliness" goes beyond tailpipe emissions, accounting for human rights, sustainable mining, and decarbonization efforts in materials like steel and aluminum. East Asian firms generally lagged due to a lack of policy disclosures, and the industry as a whole still has significant improvements to make, particularly regarding Indigenous rights.

title: These Growth Funds Continued to Shine in Q1 2024
summary: Growth equity funds, especially those with exposure to AI-related stocks, continued their strong performance in Q1 2024, significantly outperforming value funds. While large-cap growth led the way, small- and mid-cap growth funds also fared well. International growth funds also saw gains, although they lagged behind their US counterparts.

title: Aqua Metals is building a more sustainable battery recycling ecosystem
summary: Aqua Metals is pioneering a new electro-hydrometallurgy process for Li-ion battery recycling, which aims to be cleaner, more efficient, and more cost-effective than current pyrometallurgy and hydrometallurgy methods. The company plans to scale its technology with a new plant in Nevada, targeting 10,000 tons of black mass processing annually by 2025. This process recovers critical battery minerals, including lithium, and focuses on creating a closed-loop, sustainable supply chain, contrasting with the environmental challenges and lower recovery rates of other methods.

title: Self-driving semi-trucks are the future of US transport — and they’ve already hit the road
summary: Self-driving semi-trucks are beginning to be deployed on US roads, with companies like Aurora Innovations and Kodiak Robotics trialing autonomous rigs, particularly in Texas. This technology aims to revolutionize the trucking industry by reducing travel times and costs associated with human drivers. However, concerns remain regarding safety, with previous accidents involving self-driving vehicles, and potential job displacement for human truckers.

title: Edison Scholar Says Ditch the Gas Guzzler
summary: Millie Lombera, a senior at MSTMA at Roosevelt High and an Edison Scholar, plans to pursue computer science to optimize electric vehicle energy usage and integrate them into the energy grid. She was awarded a $50,000 scholarship from Edison International for her commitment to a clean energy future and her academic achievements despite financial hardships. Lombera aims to contribute to reducing fossil fuel dependence and carbon emissions.

title: Mechatronics - fusion of mechanical and electronic
summary: Magna is a leading supplier of mechatronics systems to the global automotive industry, integrating mechanical capabilities with electronic control systems to enhance vehicle interaction. Their solutions include advanced latching systems like SmartLatch™ and Comfort+™, power closures such as automatic doors and liftgates, and sophisticated door modules for manufacturing efficiency. Magna also designs mechatronic control modules, pedestrian protection systems, and customizable hinges and handles, all aimed at improving safety, convenience, and styling in modern vehicles.

title: Tesla settles lawsuit over Autopilot crash that killed Apple engineer
summary: Tesla has settled a wrongful death lawsuit brought by the family of Apple engineer Walter Huang, who died in a 2018 Model X crash while Autopilot was engaged. The settlement allows Tesla to avoid a public trial, which was set to begin Monday, and prevents the airing of evidence that could have set a precedent in future product liability suits. The National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB) previously found Tesla's driver assistance systems were partly to blame, along with possible driver distraction and problematic road construction.

title: Billionaire Bargain Bin: 3 Stocks the Forbes 400 Loves (That You’ve Never Heard Of)
summary: This article identifies three lesser-known stocks favored by members of the Forbes 400 list: Royalty Pharma (RPRX), Samsara (IOT), and New Mountain Finance Corp. (NMFC). Each company's CEO or founder is a billionaire, yet the companies themselves are not widely recognized by the general public.
