The author details the business model, financial performance, and market potential for each stock, emphasizing their value propositions despite their relative obscurity.

title: Prevention through Design (PtD) Award
summary: The Prevention through Design (PtD) Award, presented by NIOSH and partners, recognizes individuals, teams, and organizations for eliminating or reducing hazards through design efforts or contributing to the body of knowledge enabling PtD solutions. Nominations are due the first weekday of March and highlight successful implementations of PtD principles, with recent winners including Bruce Main and Jacobs Solutions in 2024. The article details the award criteria, nomination process, and provides examples of past awardees and honorable mentions, showcasing diverse PtD applications.
source: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention | CDC (.gov)
source_domain: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention | CDC (.gov)

title: The Metals Company names new board vice chairman
summary: The Metals Company (TMC) has appointed Steve Jurvetson, a renowned venture capitalist and former Tesla and current SpaceX board member, as vice chairman to its board of directors and special advisor to the CEO. Jurvetson, known for his early-stage tech investments, will guide TMC through its next growth phase, focusing on deep-sea polymetallic nodules for the energy transition. TMC CEO Gerard Barron highlighted Jurvetson's visionary approach and experience in scaling impactful companies.

title: Electric Vehicles: Slow, then Fast
summary: A recent Citi Research report, led by Itay Michaeli, predicts a period of slow growth for electric vehicle (EV) volume this year, followed by significant acceleration in the longer term. The report forecasts a modest 12% global battery electric vehicle (BEV) volume growth for 2024, but anticipates annual penetration gains to return to the 3- to 4-point range starting next year, potentially reaching 33% global EV adoption by 2030. The analysis suggests that while current EV sentiment may be overly negative, factors like the rise of PHEVs and strategic investments by automakers could drive future market expansion.

title: PAL IPO News - On the road again: Auto transport roll-up Proficient Auto Logistics files for a $100 million IPO
summary: Proficient Auto Logistics, an auto transportation and logistics roll-up, has filed with the SEC for an initial public offering aiming to raise $100 million. The company, formed from five operating entities, boasts one of North America's largest auto transport fleets with 1,130 vehicles and trailers, serving major auto companies like General Motors, BMW, Tesla, and Rivian. With $415 million in revenue for 2023, Proficient Auto Logistics plans to list on Nasdaq under the symbol PAL, with Stifel, Raymond James, and William Blair as joint bookrunners.

title: Cathie Wood, ARK CEO, on Tesla and Nvidia Stock, Favorite AI Plays
summary: Cathie Wood, CEO of ARK Invest, remains a strong believer in Tesla's long-term potential, citing its leadership in autonomous driving and robotics, and recently purchased shares after a significant dip. She also stated that the ARK 21Shares Bitcoin ETF is merely the first step in ARK's broader plan for digital asset offerings. The article also touches on her views regarding Nvidia and other AI plays.

title: Auto giant Magna to pilot Sanctuary AI’s humanoid robots for factory use
summary: Magna International will begin testing humanoid robots from Sanctuary AI in its factories within the next few months, marking Magna's second investment in the Vancouver-based firm. The "Phoenix" robots, designed to mimic human form and intelligence, will operate in "pilot mode" initially, guided by human operators, with the goal of eventually achieving full autonomy for repetitive manufacturing tasks. This initiative is part of a broader trend among auto-parts manufacturers like Mercedes-Benz, BMW, and Tesla to integrate autonomous robots to address labor costs and potential shortages.

title: 3 EV Charging Stocks to Turn $100,000 Into $1 Million: April 2024
summary: This article identifies three EV charging stocks—Blink Charging (BLNK), Wallbox (WBX), and Enphase Energy (ENPH)—with potential for significant growth by 2030, despite recent market corrections. The author believes these companies are poised for multibagger returns, citing their financial guidance, product launches, expansion strategies, and strong cash positions. The article highlights the early growth stage of the EV charging market and anticipates substantial value creation.

title: Tesla reportedly to lay off a tenth of its workforce after dismal first-quarter sales
summary: Tesla is reportedly planning to lay off about 10% of its workforce, impacting around 14,000 employees, following a dismal first quarter of sales. CEO Elon Musk communicated the cost-cutting measure in a memo to staff. This news comes as two key executives announced their departure and Tesla's stock has lost about a third of its value this year due to slowing EV sales and increased competition.

title: Piedmont Lithium takes a big step forward in plans to build $1B mine near Charlotte
summary: Piedmont Lithium has received a key mining permit from North Carolina for its proposed $1.2 billion Carolina Lithium mine and processing plant in Gaston County. This approval marks a significant step forward for the project, which aims to become a critical part of the American electric vehicle supply chain. Construction is anticipated to begin next year, contingent on further county rezoning, financing, and remaining permits.

title: Runway Growth Capital Provides a $40 Million Growth Investment to CarNow
summary: Runway Growth Capital has committed $40 million in growth investment to CarNow, an automotive technology platform. This funding will allow CarNow to accelerate product development, enhance its digital retailing solutions, and expand its market reach. The investment aims to further revolutionize the car-buying and selling experience for both dealerships and consumers.

title: Powering the Future: Top 10 EV Charging Companies Leading the Charge
summary: This article lists the top 10 EV charging companies, detailing their market capitalization, share price, market share, and country of origin. It highlights their contributions to the EV charging infrastructure and their efforts to make electric vehicle charging more accessible and efficient globally. The companies featured range from established giants to innovative startups, all playing a crucial role in the transition to sustainable transportation.

title: LCID Stock: Lucid Unveils Details of Its 2024 Air Grand Touring
summary: Lucid Group (LCID) has revealed details about its 2024 Lucid Air Grand Touring, featuring faster charging and an enhanced range that is less impacted by cold temperatures. This announcement has positively affected LCID stock, which has seen upward momentum after a period of decline. The new model is expected to be a crucial growth driver for Lucid in the competitive EV market.

title: Wholesale used vehicle prices slip in early April as sales pace slows
summary: Wholesale used vehicle prices experienced a 1.9% decline in the first two weeks of April, following a surge in demand during March's tax season. While year-over-year prices are down by 14%, and all used vehicle segments saw price drops, luxury vehicles depreciated the least, and EVs the most. The slowdown in sales conversion rates suggests a cooling demand, potentially due to fulfilled tax refund purchases and a shortage of affordable used car options.

title: T. Rowe Price says 2018 Tesla pay aligned with investor interests
summary: Mutual fund firm T. Rowe Price expressed support for Elon Musk's 2018 Tesla pay package, stating it demonstrated strong alignment with investor interests and led to significant value creation. This comes after a Delaware judge voided the $56 billion package, leading Tesla to schedule a special shareholder vote on June 13 to re-approve it. T. Rowe Price, a top 10 Tesla investor, indicated that the original award's requirements were met and urged a new shareholder vote, despite not yet confirming how its funds will vote.

title: Checking in on the Tesla Diner at 7001 W. Santa Monica Boulevard in Hollywood
summary: Tesla is moving forward with a novel project at 7001 W. Santa Monica Boulevard in Hollywood, combining an EV charging station with a drive-in theater and a diner. The two-story diner, designed by Stantec, will offer rooftop dining and counter-style seating, complementing the 30-vehicle charging equipment on site. This Hollywood location is reportedly a prototype for more deluxe Tesla charging stations to be rolled out across the country.

title: Tesla cuts US prices of Models Y, X, S by $2,000
summary: Tesla has reduced the prices of its Model Y, Model X, and Model S vehicles in the U.S. by $2,000 each, following a miss on first-quarter delivery expectations. The price cuts affect all variants of these models, with the Model Y base now at $42,990 and the Model S base at $72,990, among others. Additionally, Tesla announced it would end its referral program benefits after April 30.

title: The pressure’s on for big tech firms, says BofA
summary: Bank of America strategists indicate high pressure on big tech firms, particularly the Magnificent Seven, to deliver on AI promises as their earnings growth is set to decelerate. While the group is still expected to post significant growth, the contribution of AI to profits and AI capital expenditure outlook will be key focuses for investors. This comes amidst a recent market downturn for tech stocks, with predictions of a rotation to value-oriented stocks later in the year.

title: Themes Humanoid Robotics ETF Holdings
summary: The Themes Humanoid Robotics ETF (BOTT), established on April 22, 2024, focuses its investments on theme equity within the humanoid robotics sector. It currently manages $32.6 million in assets across 32 holdings. The ETF is passively managed, tracking an index that selects companies based on positive total return, revenue from humanoid robotics, and market capitalization.

title: China's Li Auto cuts car prices in market share battle
summary: Chinese automaker Li Auto announced price cuts of around 5% on four of its five models, offering refunds to owners who purchased these models earlier this year. This move intensifies the ongoing price war in China's automotive market, following similar actions by Tesla and BYD. Li Auto also recently launched a new extended-range electric vehicle, the Li L6, which sold over 10,000 units within 72 hours.

title: Why Investors Should Choose the Nasdaq-100 Over the Ark Innovation ETF
summary: This article argues that investors should favor the Invesco QQQ Trust, which tracks the Nasdaq-100, over the actively managed Ark Innovation ETF (ARKK). Although Ark Invest has had some notable successes, its overall performance has largely underperformed the Nasdaq-100, especially considering its higher expense ratio. The author suggests that despite Ark's bold, sometimes correct, predictions, its misses and underperformance make the passively managed QQQ a more suitable choice for tech-sector exposure.

title: Heavy-Duty Electric Trucks With Solar Panels Are Here For Your Food
summary: Performance Food Group is decarbonizing its food distribution fleet by using electric trucks equipped with solar-enabled electric refrigeration trailers to replace diesel-powered units. This initiative, supported by public funding and private partners like Advance Energy Machines and FreeWire Technologies, aims to significantly reduce CO2 emissions and reliance on fossil fuels for refrigerated transport. The project also includes a large rooftop solar array and energy storage system at their Gilroy facility, with plans to expand to other locations.

title: Grid Storage Batteries Could Charge Up Lithium Demand
summary: While electric vehicles are expected to be the primary driver of lithium demand, grid-scale storage batteries are emerging as the fastest-growing source of lithium demand. This growth is fueled by declining costs in alternative energy and the increasing need for electrical energy storage. Investors can capitalize on this trend through dedicated lithium miners ETFs like LITP.

title: The Advantage of Low Stock Market Expectations
summary: This article discusses the advantage of lower stock market expectations, using examples like FedEx and Lululemon to illustrate how companies with lower initial valuations can see stock price increases even with modest results, while those with high valuations may be punished for not meeting elevated expectations. The author suggests that diversification opportunities may lie outside the "Magnificent Seven," particularly in small-cap stocks, which are currently unloved, underperforming, and undervalued. The article also provides a historical perspective on market resilience and the importance of diversification following significant downturns.

title: Tesla Is Not the Next Ford. It’s the Next Con Ed.
summary: Tesla's core automotive business is facing significant challenges, including a recall of Cybertrucks, declining sales, and mass layoffs, with first-quarter net income down 55%. Despite these issues, the company is pivoting its focus to AI and self-driving cars, and more critically, its energy business—including EV chargers and large-scale battery storage—is experiencing rapid growth. This shift suggests Tesla's future may lie in becoming a utility much like Con Edison, providing essential energy infrastructure, rather than primarily a car manufacturer like Ford.

title: Expedia to make second round of layoffs in Austin this year
summary: Expedia is conducting a second round of layoffs in Austin, impacting employees at its Domain office tower. The company, which owns VRBO, is facing headwinds and has a history of staff reductions. This follows a previous round of cuts earlier in 2024.

title: Honda Plans to Establish Comprehensive Electric Vehicle Value Chain in Ontario, Canada
summary: Honda Motor Co., Ltd. announced plans to invest approximately CAD$15 billion (USD$11 billion) to establish a comprehensive electric vehicle (EV) value chain in Ontario, Canada. This investment aims to strengthen Honda's EV supply system in North America, with production expected to begin in 2028, creating at least 1,000 new jobs in addition to maintaining current employment levels. The initiative includes an EV plant, an EV battery plant, and joint venture partnerships for cathode active material and separator production, contributing to Honda's goal of 100% EV and FCEV sales by 2040.

title: Tesla's plan for affordable cars takes page from Detroit rivals
summary: Tesla is shifting its strategy for affordable cars, opting to base new models on existing product lines rather than developing entirely new vehicles, a move that mirrors practices of traditional automakers like Ford and General Motors. This decision comes after reports that Tesla shelved its long-awaited $25,000 "Model 2" and aims to produce "more affordable" models using current platforms by early next year. While some analysts praise the strategy for enabling faster launches, it also raises concerns about Tesla's ability to achieve significant cost reductions and meet ambitious production timelines, potentially changing its perception from a tech stock to a traditional automaker.

title: Stellantis And Tesla: Combine These Stocks For The Ultimate Automotive Portfolio (STLA)
summary: This article suggests combining Stellantis (STLA) and Tesla (TSLA) stocks to create a balanced automotive portfolio. Tesla leads in EVs and innovation, while Stellantis offers strong global reach, a diverse brand portfolio, and an attractive dividend. Both companies are presented as undervalued with strong financials and widespread global presence, complementing each other's strengths.

title: PAL IPO News - Take it for a spin: Auto transport roll-up Proficient Auto Logistics sets terms for $215 million IPO
summary: Proficient Auto Logistics, a roll-up of auto transportation and logistics providers, has announced terms for its initial public offering (IPO), seeking to raise $215 million by offering 14.3 million shares at a price range of $14 to $16. Insiders plan to purchase $3 million worth of shares. The company, formed from five operating entities, projects a $362 million market value at the midpoint and plans to list on the Nasdaq under the symbol PAL, with pricing expected in the week of May 6, 2024.

title: S&P Global Mobility: April auto sales to sustain spring volume push
summary: S&P Global Mobility projects 1.34 million new light vehicle sales for April 2024, translating to a 16.0 million SAAR, despite fewer selling days than previous periods. This sustained volume reflects ongoing consumer demand amidst mixed signals of increased inventory and incentives against high interest rates and vehicle prices. The report also anticipates future growth in battery-electric vehicle sales, supported by upcoming model launches and addressing production improvements.

title: Tesla Semi spotted being used by another customer
summary: The Tesla Semi program is expanding beyond PepsiCo and internal use, with one of the newly confirmed customers being Sysco, a major US food distributor. This development comes after a Tesla Semi was sighted actively used by Sysco in California, fulfilling a reservation they made upon the truck's unveiling in 2017. Tesla aims for volume production of the Semi by late next year, with wider customer deliveries anticipated in 2026.

title: Automotive Collision Avoidance System Market is Likely to Experience a Tremendous Growth in Near Future As ...
summary: The Automotive Collision Avoidance System Market is projected to grow at a CAGR of 7.9% through 2030, driven by advancements in sensor technologies, increasing adoption of autonomous vehicles, and a focus on improving safety. Key market players include Continental, Denso Corporation, and Robert Bosch GmbH. The market is segmented by application, type, component, and vehicle type, with significant opportunities identified in sensor advancements and integration with ADAS.

title: Tesla’s top HR executive has left the company, sources say
summary: Allie Arebalo, Tesla's top human resources executive and one of the most senior women at the company, has reportedly left the electric vehicle manufacturer. Her departure follows a recent trend of senior leaders resigning and comes amid broader job cuts, with Tesla aiming for a staff reduction of about 20%. It is unclear if Arebalo was let go as part of these cuts or if she resigned amidst Elon Musk's focus on cost reduction and declining vehicle sales.

title: Tesla's Volatility Offers Traders Plenty of Opportunities
summary: Tesla's recent market fluctuations, marked by a Q1 revenue drop and subsequent rally due to self-driving approval in China, create trading opportunities in leveraged ETFs like TSLL and TSLS. Despite challenges like EV oversupply, future catalysts such as new models in 2025 and increasing EV adoption could drive long-term growth. The article also highlights the Direxion Daily Electric and Autonomous Vehicles Bull 2X Shares (EVAV) for broader exposure to the expanding EV and autonomous vehicle market.

title: Tesla's Volatility Offers Traders Plenty of Opportunities
summary: Tesla's recent market fluctuations, though unsettling for some investors, present opportunities for traders who capitalize on volatility, particularly through leveraged ETFs like Direxion Daily TSLA Bull 2X Shares (TSLL) and Direxion Daily TSLA Bear 1X Shares (TSLS). Despite recent drops in revenue and concerns about demand, Tesla's self-driving approval in China and plans for new models in 2025 offer potential bullish long-term prospects. The broader trend also supports an increase in EV adoption, creating opportunities for funds like the Direxion Daily Electric and Autonomous Vehicles Bull 2X Shares (EVAV).

title: Lululemon billionaire pledges shares to Goldman for margin loan
summary: Lululemon founder Chip Wilson pledged 1.8 million Lululemon shares, valued at about $660 million, to Goldman Sachs Group Inc. as collateral for a $200 million margin loan facility. This move allows Wilson to leverage his public holdings for liquidity and expand investments outside Lululemon. Pledging shares offers tax advantages over selling, but carries risks such as potential margin calls during market volatility.

title: YieldMax's New ETF Can Capitalize on Tesla Volatility
summary: YieldMax has launched a new actively managed ETF, CRSH, designed to generate income and capitalize on the volatility of Tesla's stock by providing inverse indirect exposure. The fund uses a synthetic covered put strategy and aims to replicate approximately -100% of Tesla's share price movements without directly investing in or shorting the company. It can profit when Tesla's share price decreases and mitigates losses with out-of-the-money call options when the stock rises sharply.

title: EV Transportation Startup Aims To Fill Industry Gap
summary: ERide, a Wilmington-based electric vehicle transportation startup, offers a luxury scheduled ride service using Lucid Motors EVs, aiming to bridge the gap between rideshares and limousines. Founded by Andrew Emborsky and operated with Tim Elze, the company provides rides for $25 in Wilmington, with a membership option for priority and discounts. ERide plans to expand its driving availability and has partnered with local establishments like the Commodore Club to offer transportation solutions.

title: Tesla cancels cheapest Model Y, adds Long Range version
summary: Tesla has reintroduced the Model Y Long Range, featuring an EPA range of up to 320 miles starting at $46,630. CEO Elon Musk also revealed that recent base Model Y buyers could unlock an additional 40-60 miles of range for $1,500-$2,000, pending regulatory approval. Despite competitive pressure from other EVs with similar or greater range, the Model Y still qualifies for the full $7,500 EV tax credit, making its effective cost $39,130 for many.

title: Tesla expands Semi testing program, partners with Costco, Sysco, and US Foods
summary: Tesla is expanding its Semi testing program, partnering with major companies like Costco, Sysco, and US Foods. These new collaborations aim to refine the electric Class 8 truck's engineering before mass production begins, adding to existing partnerships with PepsiCo, Martin Brower, and Walmart. Tesla is gearing up for mass production of the Semi, with initial customer deliveries planned for 2026.

title: Carvana trends report: Used EVs narrow price gap with ICE cars, triggering sales growth potential
summary: Carvana's Q1 2024 EV trends report shows encouraging growth in used EV sales, with the average price dropping from $37,000 in Q1 2023 to $31,000 a year later. The price gap between used EVs and ICE cars has narrowed, and federal tax credits for used EVs are contributing to increased affordability and sales. Used EVs accounted for 4.3% of Carvana's total sales in Q1 2024, an all-time high, indicating significant potential for future growth.

title: Top 10: Energy Storage Companies
summary: This article lists the top 10 energy storage companies revolutionizing the industry, highlighting their contributions to sustainable energy solutions. Featuring companies like Tesla, GE Vernova, and Enphase Energy, it discusses their innovative technologies and market impact across residential, commercial, and grid-scale applications. The piece emphasizes the growing demand for clean and reliable energy and the critical role these companies play in meeting future energy needs.

title: Electric trucking costs could be more than twice diesel, report says
summary: A report by Ryder indicates that the total cost to transport goods using electric trucks could be more than double that of diesel trucks, especially for heavy-duty vehicles, and up to 67% higher for a mixed fleet. This increased cost is largely due to higher equipment expenses, general and administrative costs, and labor, even with some savings in fuel and maintenance. Despite regulations pushing for EV adoption, Ryder's CEO notes that the business case for many customers isn't viable yet due to technology limitations and insufficient charging infrastructure.

title: Idan Ofer again loses on EVs
summary: Idan Ofer's Kenon Holdings, through its subsidiary OPC, is writing off a significant investment in EV charging station company Gnrgy, marking Ofer's second loss in the EV sector after the collapse of Better Place a decade ago. Despite the booming EV market in Israel, increased competition, lack of government support, and burdensome regulation have made the charging infrastructure business challenging for Gnrgy. OPC, primarily a power plant operator, views this as a minor setback given its large market cap and profitable core business.

title: Zeekr IPO: Stock price for China-based Tesla rival will be closely watched in NYSE debut today
summary: Zeekr, a Chinese EV maker and subsidiary of Geely, is making its IPO debut on the New York Stock Exchange today under the ticker ZK. The company is offering 21 million American Depositary Shares (ADSs) at $21 each, aiming to raise $441 million, potentially up to $507.2 million. While Zeekr does not currently sell vehicles in the U.S., its CEO notes narrowing sales gaps with Tesla in China and upcoming expansion in Europe, signaling increased competition for Elon Musk's company.

title: Ford CFO tapped as vice chair; Lucid Motors exec set to succeed
summary: Ford Motor Company is shuffling its financial leadership, appointing current CFO John Lawler as vice chair and bringing in former Lucid Motors CFO Sherry House as vice president of finance, with House set to become CFO early next year. This leadership change comes as Ford, like other automakers, navigates challenges in the electric vehicle market, including shareholder concerns and profitability issues within its EV division. The article details House's compensation package and Lawler's expanded responsibilities and compensation in his new role as vice chair as the company focuses on future strategy and partnerships.

title: NLRB judge says courts must decide Starbucks' challenge to agency's structure
summary: A National Labor Relations Board administrative judge has ruled that Starbucks committed labor law violations at a New Orleans store but refrained from addressing the company's constitutional challenge to the NLRB's structure. The judge stated that constitutional claims are matters for federal courts to decide, not the administrative agency. Starbucks can appeal the decision to the five-member board and then to a federal appeals court.

title: Pegatron, NXP launch EV application testing lab
summary: Pegatron Corp and NXP Semiconductor NV have launched a joint laboratory in Taipei for the verification of electric vehicle (EV) applications, particularly smart cockpits. This collaboration is NXP's third in Taiwan and highlights both companies' bullish outlook on the growing automotive market, with Pegatron aiming to boost its revenue from auto products to 10 percent of its total next year. The new lab is equipped with machines valued at over NT$10 million and is already processing new product designs.

title: Down 20% This Year, Will Under Armour’s Stock Recover Following Q4 Results?
summary: Under Armour (NYSE: UA) is set to report its fiscal fourth-quarter results, and the stock is expected to trade higher post-earnings despite a 20% year-to-date decline. The company has faced slowing growth in North America and skepticism following the return of founder Kevin Plank as CEO. However, strong international performance and potential future share reductions could positively impact the stock, with a Trefis valuation indicating an 20% upside from current prices.

title: Tesla must face vehicle owners' lawsuit over self-driving claims
summary: A U.S. judge has ruled that Tesla must face a class-action lawsuit alleging the company misled vehicle owners about its self-driving capabilities. The lawsuit, led by a retired lawyer, claims Tesla falsely advertised Autopilot and Full Self-Driving technology as functional or "just around the corner" since 2016, inducing customers to pay more for their vehicles. The judge allowed negligence and fraud-based claims to proceed, emphasizing that if Tesla implied its hardware was sufficient for high or full automation, the falsity was sufficiently alleged.

title: TSLT Financial Statements & Charts - Etf Opportunities Trust - T-rex 2x Long Tesla Daily Target Etf MacroTrends
summary: This page provides financial statements and charts for the ETF Opportunities Trust - T-REX 2X Long Tesla Daily Target ETF (TSLT). It includes real-time stock data, market indices, and links to various financial analysis tools. The information is presented by MacroTrends and CMLViz.com.

title: Tesla must face claims it misled buyers about Autopilot and self-driving
summary: A court has ruled that Tesla must face claims alleging it misled customers regarding the capabilities of its Autopilot and self-driving features. The decision suggests that accusations of deceptive marketing related to autonomous driving technology will proceed. This development could have significant implications for Tesla and its representations of its advanced driver-assistance systems.

title: GM Stock Forecast: Is General Motors Headed to $100 by 2025?
summary: General Motors (GM) stock has gained almost 27% year-to-date, outperforming rivals, and analysts are forecasting a mean target price of $52.60, with a high of $96. Despite depressed valuations and risks like EV losses and international market challenges, the company's strong Q1 earnings and raised full-year guidance suggest a potential for re-rating, possibly reaching $100 by 2025 if it achieves its EV margin targets and maintains ICE business performance.

title: Aqua Metals raises more capital, reports loss
summary: Aqua Metals, a battery materials recycling company, has secured new funding, including a nonbinding term sheet for up to $33 million and an additional $7 million from stock sales. This capital is expected to fund the first phase of its Sierra AquaRefining Campus. The company reported a net loss of $5.75 million in the first quarter of this year, an increase from $4.6 million in the same period last year, largely due to increased plant operations and research and development costs as they expand their operations.

title: Regulatory Risks, Investment Sentiment and the India/Bharat Conundrum
summary: The article discusses the economic and regulatory landscape in India following the anticipated re-election of the BJP government led by Prime Minister Narendra Modi. It highlights India's attractiveness to global investors due to policy continuity, economic growth, and a focus on infrastructure, tax reform, and digital regulations. The authors emphasize the "India-Bharat Conundrum," reflecting the diverse market opportunities between urban and semi-urban/rural areas, and underscore the need for investors to understand the competitive and dynamic market to succeed.

title: This Might Just Be YieldMax’s Craziest ETF Yet (104.56% Dividend Yield)
summary: YieldMax has introduced a new ETF, ULTY, which offers an impressive 104.56% dividend yield. The company is known for its synthetic covered call strategy on individual stocks, generating high double-digit to triple-digit dividend yields. This strategy has led to significant interest from income investors and continues to drive the launch of new products.

title: Tesla Semi Fleet Grows As PepsiCo Takes Delivery Of Another 50 EV Trucks
summary: Tesla is delivering an additional 50 Semi trucks to PepsiCo, expanding PepsiCo's fleet to 86 Semis. While the Semi is currently in pilot production, Tesla plans to build a high-volume factory at Giga Nevada, with deliveries to external customers expected to start in 2026. The upcoming factory aims for an eventual target capacity of 50,000 units per year.

title: Guiding the Future: Garrett’s Controls Technology Pushes E-Motor Performance to New Heights
summary: Garrett Motion's advanced controls technology is enabling its e-motors to achieve rotational speeds exceeding 200,000 RPM, crucial for efficiency and performance in fuel cell, hybrid, and full electric powertrains. This innovation, rooted in in-house investment across e-motor design, power electronics, and control systems, allows for sensorless operations and pushes the boundaries of microcontrollers. The company's unique approach to testing and simulation at ultra-high speeds ensures that it continues to meet and exceed customer requirements in the evolving automotive industry.

title: Is Allegro Stock a Fabulous Play on Fabless EV Chips?
summary: The article analyzes Allegro MicroSystems (ALGM) stock as a potential investment in the EV chip market, highlighting its fabless manufacturing model and leadership in magnetic sensor ICs. It compares Allegro to competitors like Wolfspeed and Infineon, discussing its financial performance, market positioning, and recent challenges regarding Q1-2025 guidance. The author concludes that while Allegro is a contender as a pick-and-shovel play on EVs, its growth and market share face stiff competition and macroeconomic headwinds.

title: Risks automakers face as they reimagine their business
summary: This article discusses the risks automakers face as they transition to electric vehicles (EVs), highlighting financial and legal risks from shareholders, increased cyber risks due to digital reliance, and product liability from advanced driver-assistance systems. AIG's Dennis Froneberg emphasizes the importance of strategic planning, collaboration, and risk engineering to manage these challenges, including those related to potential spinoffs of non-core units.

title: Ryder: Can we Bridge the Cost Parity Gap Between Electric and Diesel Truck Operations?
summary: Robert Sanchez, Ryder’s CEO and chairman, highlighted that the business case for electric trucks is currently more expensive for fleets than anticipated, presenting a major barrier to widespread adoption. He emphasized the need for technological breakthroughs in battery technology, reduced vehicle costs, and expanded charging infrastructure to achieve cost parity with diesel trucks. Ryder's analysis indicates significant cost increases for converting to EVs, which could lead to higher prices for consumers.

title: Tesla data center delay highlights industrywide challenges
summary: Tesla is experiencing delays with its Dojo data center project in Austin, Texas, crucial for its full self-driving ambitions, leading to the termination of the project's infrastructure director. Despite an increase in contractors entering the data center market, the complexity of evolving technology and the scarcity of contractors with diverse expertise in areas like solar, water reuse, and various power systems, pose significant challenges to completing these sophisticated facilities on time. The demand for environmentally sustainable solutions further complicates contractor selection.

title: Big Lithium Processing Plant Planned in Oklahoma
summary: Stardust Power plans to build a large lithium processing plant in Muskogee, Oklahoma, aiming to process 50,000 tons of lithium annually to supply 1.1 million electric vehicles. This $1.2 billion project is driven by federal incentives promoting domestic sourcing of critical minerals and seeks to address the refining bottleneck in the North American lithium supply chain. The company is pursuing funding through a SPAC deal, government grants, and state incentives, with construction slated to begin in 2025.

title: Who Makes Lucid Cars?
summary: Lucid is a Newark, California-based luxury electric vehicle company that received a significant investment from Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund, making it the majority stockholder. The company, led by CEO and CTO Peter Rawlinson (formerly of Tesla), produces its vehicles in North America's first purpose-built EV factory in Casa Grande, Arizona, and another facility in Saudi Arabia. Lucid currently offers four variants of its Air model and plans to launch the three-row electric SUV, the Gravity, in 2025.

title: If You Can Only Buy One Battery Stock in May, It Better Be One of These 3 Names
summary: As demand for electric vehicles drives significant growth in the battery market, analysts predict a global battery opportunity of $508.8 billion by 2033. This article highlights three promising battery-related investments: Solid Power (SLDP) for its potential with A-2 sample cells, the Amplify Lithium & Battery Technology ETF (BATT) for diversified exposure, and Albemarle (ALB) as a key lithium producer benefiting from increasing demand. Investors are encouraged to consider these options to capitalize on the booming battery industry.

title: Volkswagen targets 2027 for launch of affordable EV
summary: Volkswagen plans to launch its new line of affordable electric vehicles (EVs) in 2027, with prices starting around $21,800. This move follows the termination of its partnership with Renault and aims to introduce models like the ID 1 and an ID 2all SUV based on the ID 2all concept. Volkswagen will enter a competitive market where other manufacturers like Volvo, GM, Kia, and Hyundai already offer various budget-friendly EV options.

title: Tesla Settles Fatal Crash Suit as Another Jury Trial Loomed
summary: Tesla Inc. has settled a lawsuit related to a fiery 2016 crash that killed a Model S passenger, marking the second time in two months the company has avoided a jury trial in California over a fatal wreck. This settlement follows a confidential accord in April for a high-profile case involving Autopilot, where an Apple Inc. engineer died. The recent case involved claims that the Model S suddenly accelerated, leading to a crash and fire that allegedly entrapped the passenger.

title: EV switch to hit auto suppliers hard, Stellantis CEO says
summary: Stellantis CEO Carlos Tavares stated that the shift to electric vehicles will significantly burden auto suppliers due to the need for cost cutting to make EVs affordable. He highlighted that EV production costs are 40%-50% higher than petrol models, forcing a global shift in the supplier base towards "best cost countries." Tavares also mentioned Stellantis's strategy of using multi-energy platforms and launching more affordable EV models to address consumer reluctance to purchase expensive electric cars without government incentives.

title: Why Investing Guru Jeremy Grantham’s New ETF Shuns Nvidia and Tesla Stock
summary: Legendary investor Jeremy Grantham's new ETF, GMO’s U.S. Quality ETF, is outperforming the stock market despite intentionally excluding high-momentum stocks like Nvidia and Tesla. The ETF instead focuses on defensive stocks such as Coca-Cola, alongside five of the "Magnificent Seven" tech stocks. This strategy highlights a preference for quality and value over pure momentum plays.

title: Why Investing Guru Jeremy Grantham’s New ETF Shuns Nvidia and Tesla Stock
summary: Legendary value investor Jeremy Grantham's new GMO’s U.S. Quality ETF is outperforming the stock market despite shunning high-momentum stocks like Nvidia and Tesla. The ETF opts for more defensive stocks such as Coca-Cola, while still holding five of the "Magnificent Seven" tech stocks. This strategy highlights a focus on quality and value over pure momentum plays.

title: STMicro to build €5B plant in Italy with EU Chips Act aid
summary: STMicro, a supplier to Tesla and BMW, plans to construct a massive 5 billion euro microchip plant in Catania, Italy. This significant investment is being supported by the EU Chips Act, aiming to boost semiconductor production within Europe. The new facility will focus on manufacturing electric vehicle microchips.

title: Tesla supplier STMicroelectronics to build €5B EV chip fab in Italy
summary: STMicroelectronics, a key Tesla supplier and the world's largest producer of silicon carbide microchips, plans to construct a €5 billion EV chip factory in Catania, Italy. The facility will specialize in 200mm wafers for electric vehicles and high-voltage power devices, with the EU contributing €2 billion under the Chips Act to bolster Europe's semiconductor supply chain. This move aims to increase the EU's share of global semiconductor production and ensure a reliable source of energy-efficient chips.

title: EU approves Italian aid for $5.4 bln STMicro chip plant, more to come
summary: The European Commission has approved Italian state aid of approximately 2 billion euros for STMicroelectronics to build a 5 billion euro ($5.4 billion) microchip plant in Catania, Sicily. This move is part of Europe's strategy to reduce its reliance on Asian chip imports and boost domestic semiconductor production, with more investments expected under the EU Chips Act. The new plant will produce energy-efficient silicon carbide chips primarily for the electric vehicle market, creating 3,000 jobs.

title: Apple Supplier STMicroelectronics to Build $5.4 Billion Chip Plant in Italy
summary: STMicroelectronics, a key supplier for Apple, Tesla, and Samsung, is investing approximately $5.42 billion to build a new chip-manufacturing plant in Catania, Sicily, with state support. This facility will produce silicon-carbide semiconductor products, essential for electric vehicles and data centers, as the company prepares for an anticipated recovery in the semiconductor market. The investment aims to expand production capacity for these energy-efficient chips.

title: Well-to-Product Integrated Lithium Solutions
summary: SLB offers "well-to-product" integrated lithium solutions that incorporate direct lithium extraction (DLE) to meet the increasing demand for battery-grade lithium. These solutions aim to be more sustainable and efficient than traditional methods, reducing water consumption by up to 95% and accelerating production from months to hours. The company has a demonstration plant in Clayton Valley, Nevada, where it has proven its DLE technology at scale.

title: Foresight to collaborate with KONEC on autonomous vehicle concept
summary: Foresight Autonomous Holdings Ltd. has signed a co-development agreement with KONEC Co., a Korean Tier 1 automotive supplier, to integrate Foresight’s ScaleCam 3D perception technology into a concept autonomous vehicle. This collaboration, sponsored by the Foundation of Korea Automotive Parts Industry Promotion (KAP), aims to accelerate the development and deployment of autonomous vehicles, enhancing safety and capabilities. Foresight’s ScaleCam system, based on stereoscopic technology, is designed to provide accurate obstacle detection and greater distance accuracy at long ranges.

title: GM CEO says commitment to all-electric fleet remains firm despite industry-wide sales slowdown
summary: General Motors CEO Mary Barra stated that the company's commitment to an all-electric future remains firm, though the transition will unfold "over decades." Despite an industry-wide slowdown in EV sales, Barra emphasized that GM aims to exclusively sell electric vehicles by 2035 and is working towards making EVs more affordable, with new models expected to start around $35,000. She also confirmed that GM EVs will access Tesla charging stations, addressing infrastructure concerns.

title: General Motors Shareholders Support First-Ever Deep-Sea Mining Proposal
summary: General Motors shareholders voted 12% in favor of a proposal to disclose policies on deep-sea mined minerals, marking the first time such a proposal has been put forward. Environmental advocates, including As You Sow, highlight the severe ecological risks of deep-sea mining and suggest that sustainable battery technologies and recycling can reduce the need for these minerals. Many of GM's peers have already committed to a moratorium on deep-sea mining, emphasizing the increasing industry movement towards responsible sourcing.

title: Private and public rules governing lithium mining in Argentina
summary: This report analyzes the convergence and divergence between private transnational standards and public local regulations governing lithium mining in Argentina, focusing on environmental, social, and governance (ESG) regulations. It compares Catamarca’s public regulations with the Initiative for Responsible Mining Assurance (IRMA) Standard, highlighting mixed results in accountability targets and sources, and proposing public policy recommendations for Free, Prior, and Informed Consultation (FPIC), public participation, and Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) to address existing gaps and conflicts.

title: Mercedes and Stellantis Press Pause on EV Battery Factories
summary: Stellantis and Mercedes-Benz are halting or pausing work on two electric vehicle battery plants in Europe due to slowing demand for expensive EVs. Their joint venture, Automotive Cells Company SE (ACC), is reassessing its investment plans and considering a pivot to manufacturing lower-cost lithium-iron-phosphate cells to better align with market trends. This move reflects a broader industry pattern of automakers adjusting EV and battery strategies amid a slowdown in sales growth.

title: Sysco takes delivery of 10 Freightliner electric semi trucks, plans to buy 800
summary: Sysco has added ten Freightliner eCascadia electric semi trucks to its Houston fleet, bringing its total EV fleet to 120 trucks. The company plans a major expansion, with a letter of intent to deploy nearly 800 battery electric Class 8 tractors by 2026 and an ultimate goal to electrify 35% of its U.S. broad line fleet, totaling around 2,500 electric trucks by 2030. Sysco previously reserved 50 Tesla Semi trucks and already has at least one in its fleet.

title: Sysco takes delivery of 10 Freightliner electric semi trucks - Autonews News
summary: Sysco has expanded its electric vehicle fleet with the addition of ten Freightliner eCascadia electric semi trucks in Houston, Texas, bringing its total EV fleet to 120 trucks. The food delivery company plans to deploy nearly 800 battery electric Class 8 tractors by 2026 and aims to electrify 35% of its US broadline fleet, approximately 2,500 electric trucks, by 2030. Sysco previously reserved 50 Tesla Semis and has taken delivery of at least one.

title: VivoPower’s Tembo Launches Full Electric Pick Up Utility Vehicle with Capital Light Global Supply Chain
summary: VivoPower's subsidiary, Tembo e-LV, has launched a full electric OEM pickup utility vehicle, leveraging a new capital-light global supply chain established across Asia. This strategic shift allows Tembo to bypass capital-intensive assembly processes, accelerate revenue generation, and offer both OEM vehicles and conversion kits to B2B customers. The move is also expected to significantly reduce direct costs for the EUV conversion and Jeepney programs, expanding Tembo's total addressable market.

title: LG Energy Solution Teams up with Analog Devices, Inc to Gain Competitive Edge in Battery Management Total Solution
summary: LG Energy Solution (LGES) has signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with Analog Devices, Inc. (ADI) to enhance its battery management total solution (BMTS). This two-year collaboration aims to integrate ADI's high-performance battery management integrated circuit (BMIC) and co-develop algorithms for precise internal EV battery cell temperature measurement. This partnership is expected to lead to advanced fast-charging technologies, improved battery performance, and longevity by applying real-time impedance measurement.

title: Helping Enable 'Europe's EV Supply Chain' - Tony Sage, CEO & Chairman of Critical Metals Corp
summary: Critical Metals Corp, led by CEO Tony Sage, is working to address Europe's need for local lithium supply to support the burgeoning EV market. Their primary focus is the Wolfsberg Lithium Project in Austria, which has secured an off-take agreement with BMW including an advance payment of US$15.0 million. The company anticipates a significant shortfall in lithium supply starting in 2026 or 2027, emphasizing the urgency of projects like Wolfsberg to stabilize the market for producers, automakers, and consumers.

title: Cathie Wood's Ark missed Nvidia tech rally but eyes other AI wins
summary: Cathie Wood's Ark Investment Management largely missed Nvidia's significant tech rally after selling most of its shares at around $400, despite initially buying in 2014. Wood maintains that Ark is well-positioned for future AI growth through investments in other companies, including a continued bet on Tesla and stakes in AI startups like xAI and OpenAI, viewing autonomous driving as a major AI project. She also predicts surprisingly large interest rate cuts in 2024 due to rolling recessions in the US.

title: Weekly Recalls: Audi, Honda, Land Rover, Lincoln, Mercedes-Benz, Rivian, Toyota/Lexus,Tesla
summary: This article details various vehicle recalls issued by Audi, Honda, Land Rover, Lincoln, Mercedes-Benz, Rivian, Toyota/Lexus, and Tesla. The recalls address issues such as incorrect headlight control modules, rearview camera malfunctions, sudden loss of drive power, engine failures due to manufacturing debris, improperly aimed headlights, and faulty seat belt warning systems. For each recall, the article provides the NHTSA campaign number, affected components, potential number of units, a summary of the issue, and the proposed remedy.

title: Tesla Hires Former Geico Exec to Bring Down Costs for Owners of Its Electric Cars
summary: Tesla has hired Allen Laben, a 20-year insurance veteran and former Geico director, as its head of strategic insurance partnerships. His role is to work with insurance carriers and collision shops to reduce the cost of insuring Tesla vehicles, thereby lowering the total cost of ownership. Laben aims to make Teslas "easy and economical" to insure to accelerate the world's transition to sustainable energy, addressing high premium concerns.

title: What Does Nvidia’s Stock Split Mean for Investors?
summary: Nvidia's 10-for-1 stock split has been completed, making shares more accessible to individual investors, although it does not change the company's overall value or Morningstar's rating. The split occurred amidst Nvidia's significant AI-driven boom, with strong first-quarter earnings and a positive outlook for data center revenue. Analyst Brian Colello maintains a fair value estimate of $105 post-split and sees no immediate signs of AI demand slowing.

title: VCR: Fueled By Amazon, But Weighed Down By Tesla
summary: The Vanguard Consumer Discretionary ETF (VCR) is rated a "hold" due to key risk factors, despite Amazon being a dominant holding. The ETF, with $6.06 billion in AUM and 303 holdings, focuses on retail, auto manufacturers, and restaurants, but its returns have slightly lagged the S&P 500, partly due to Tesla's performance and potential consumer spending declines.

title: POSCO Group strengthens core rechargeable battery material value chain with concurrent groundbreakings for nickel and precursor production plants
summary: POSCO Group and China’s CNGR broke ground on nickel and precursor production plants in Pohang, South Korea, to bolster the domestic rechargeable battery industry. The joint ventures, POSCO CNGR Nickel Solution and CNP New Material Technology, will produce high-purity nickel and precursors, crucial for manufacturing cathode materials for electric vehicles. This initiative, with an investment of KRW 1.5 trillion, aims to enhance the material value chain and reduce import dependency, with mass production slated for 2026.

title: EU to Impose Additional Tariffs on EV Imports From China
summary: The European Union plans to impose additional tariffs on electric vehicle imports from China, potentially raising levies to as high as 48%. These charges, set to begin around July 4th, are a result of an investigation into Chinese subsidies and are expected to escalate trade tensions while increasing the cost of EVs for consumers. Carmakers like BYD, Geely, and SAIC have been formally notified of the upcoming tariffs.

title: ‘Record demonstration’: Dark horse in quantum race passes new milestone
summary: Diraq, an Australian quantum startup, has achieved a significant milestone with 99.9% control accuracy for a quantum bit, a precision level needed for error-corrected quantum computing. The company is partnering with GlobalFoundries Inc and IMEC to manufacture its compact, silicon-based quantum computer chip. Diraq aims to produce affordable and compact quantum computers, positioning its technology as a competitive alternative to larger, more energy-intensive systems developed by rivals like PsiQuantum.

title: Opinion: A Window Of Opportunity Is Opening For General Motors
summary: General Motors, despite previous struggles with EV production, appears to be turning a corner with its Ultium platform. The company's models, like the Equinox EV, are becoming price-competitive even in developing markets like Mexico, suggesting GM can sell them profitably. This positions GM to potentially gain market share against rivals in the mid to latter part of the decade, especially if it expands its affordable EV offerings.

title: SK Hynix patents-owned Mimir IP files complaint against Micron
summary: Mimir IP, a patent management company that acquired 1,500 chip-related patents from SK Hynix, has filed a complaint against Micron Technology Inc. for patent infringement. The lawsuit, also targeting Tesla, Dell, HP, and Lenovo, was filed in the US District Court for the Eastern District of Texas and the US International Trade Commission. This case highlights a growing trend of patent infringement disputes involving non-practicing entities (NPEs) in the competitive chip industry, with potential damages reaching $480 million.
source: The Korea Economic Daily Global Edition
source_domain: The Korea Economic Daily Global Edition

title: 2024 Cars.com American-Made Index: Which Cars Are the Most American?
summary: The 2024 Cars.com American-Made Index ranks vehicles based on their impact on the U.S. economy, with the Tesla Model Y claiming the No. 1 spot for the third consecutive year. This year's index sees a diversification in the top 10, with Honda, Volkswagen, Toyota, Lexus, and Jeep models breaking up Tesla's previous dominance. The article also details the methodology behind the AMI, which considers assembly location, parts content, engine and transmission origins, and U.S. manufacturing workforce.

title: Tesla defends its lead in American-Made Index; Honda and VW models close in
summary: Tesla has maintained its top position in the American-Made Index, as reported by Automotive News. However, models from Honda and Volkswagen are narrowing the gap, indicating increased competition in the domestically manufactured vehicle market. This suggests a shifting landscape in automotive production within the United States.

title: 2024 Cars.com American-Made Index: What About the Least American Cars?
summary: This article discusses the "least American" cars based on Cars.com's 2024 American-Made Index criteria, highlighting that many popular models from US and foreign brands are assembled outside the United States. It emphasizes that while some brands like Tesla produce all their cars domestically, others, including Ford, GM, and Honda, have significant portions of their lineups manufactured internationally. The analysis encourages consumers to look beyond brand origin to discern a vehicle's actual manufacturing location if they prioritize supporting U.S. production.

title: Tesla must face owners' lawsuit claiming it monopolizes vehicle repairs and parts
summary: A U.S. judge has ruled that Tesla must face a class-action lawsuit from vehicle owners accusing the company of monopolizing the markets for repairs and parts. The lawsuit claims Tesla coerces owners into high-priced, long-wait repairs by limiting authorized service centers and restricting parts sales. Tesla's services and other automotive revenue constituted 9% of its total revenue in 2023.

title: New lithium battery recycling plant planned for Chester County
summary: Princeton NuEnergy announced an $11 million investment to open a new lithium battery recycling plant in Chester County, South Carolina, creating 41 jobs. The facility will recycle lithium-ion batteries from electric vehicles and electronics for reuse in new EV batteries. This new plant further establishes South Carolina as a leader in the EV industry, joining other battery recyclers and preparing for the unique fire hazards associated with lithium-ion batteries.

title: Elon Musk Confirms AI Gains Traction Fast
summary: Elon Musk, during a recent interview at the Cannes Lions conference, revealed that AI adoption is spreading rapidly, citing his university-aged son's experience with classmates extensively using AI for writing. He also humorously described his approach to spending his vast wealth. Musk reiterated his prior prediction that AI could surpass human capabilities in less than three years.

title: Motor Mouth: Lotus vaults to top of the EV tech heap
summary: The Lotus Emeya has emerged as the fastest-charging EV, capable of going from 10% to 80% charge in a mere 14 minutes, significantly outperforming competitors like Porsche and Tesla. This impressive charging speed and range recovery capabilities position Lotus at the forefront of electric vehicle battery technology. The article also highlights how other EVs, like the Xpeng G9 and Hyundai Ioniq 6, distinguish themselves in different aspects, while Tesla's Model Y shows a surprising weakness in charging performance compared to the new leaders.

title: Move Over, Lucid Air: Xpeng's Mona M03 Is The New Aero King
summary: Xpeng has unveiled the Mona M03, an affordable and compact electric sedan that it claims is the most aerodynamic production sedan ever with a Cd figure of 0.194, surpassing vehicles like the Lucid Air and Mercedes EQS. Priced at an estimated $20,700, the M03 offers impressive efficiency due to its aerodynamic design, including specialized wheels and active air intakes, resulting in an estimated 342 miles of range. While not coming to the US due to tariffs, it presents a challenge to more established EVs like the Tesla Model 3 in terms of cost and aerodynamic performance.

title: Most American-Made Vehicle? The Tesla Model Y
summary: The Tesla Model Y has been recognized as the most American-made vehicle for the third consecutive year by Cars.com's annual index. Despite Tesla's continued dominance, the list sees changes with the Honda Passport and Volkswagen ID.4 ranking second and third, highlighting significant contributions from foreign automakers with U.S.-based production. The report also notes a large increase in U.S.-based production facilities since 2020, with Alabama leading the way in vehicle assembly.

title: Tesla recalls more than 11,000 Cybertrucks for issues with trunk bed, windshield wiper
summary: Tesla is recalling over 11,000 Cybertrucks due to defects in the trunk bed and windshield wiper mechanisms. The recall addresses critical safety and functionality issues for the newly released electric pickup trucks.

title: VW Latches Onto Rivian in $5 Billion EV Pact to Regain Momentum
summary: Volkswagen is investing $5 billion in Rivian Automotive to jointly develop battery-powered vehicles and software, aiming to boost its presence in the US and catch up with Tesla. This partnership offers a financial lifeline to loss-making Rivian, which has faced production and delivery challenges and a slowdown in the broader EV market. The deal marks another strategic move by VW to regain momentum in its electric vehicle rollout, following a previous investment in China's Xpeng Inc.

title: Vehicle lessee, Asbury employee sue CDK after cyberattacks
summary: CDK has been sued by a vehicle lessee and an Asbury employee following recent cyberattacks. The lawsuit was filed after the cyber incidents disrupted operations and allegedly caused damages to individuals involved with CDK's systems.

title: Rivian receives VW investment boost
summary: Volkswagen Group plans to invest up to $5 billion in Rivian through a new equally controlled joint venture that aims to develop next-generation electrical architecture and software technology. This investment is crucial for Rivian, which incurred significant losses per vehicle delivered in Q1 2024, and is expected to help secure its capital needs for growth and accelerate VW's software-defined vehicle plans. The partnership seeks to combine the companies' strengths, reduce costs, and speed up innovation in the electric vehicle sector.

title: NeoVolta Navigates NEM 3.0 Challenges, Opportunities
summary: NeoVolta (Nasdaq: NEOV) experienced significant year-over-year growth in Q4 2023 despite a decline in annual revenue for FY2023 due to California's NEM 3.0 policy. The company has adapted to the challenges of NEM 3.0 by leveraging new opportunities, hiring new CEO Ardes Johnson from Tesla, and expanding its market presence outside California into new U.S. markets and eventually the commercial sector in 2025. NeoVolta plans to capitalize on the growing solar energy storage market, which is projected to reach $154.3 billion by 2031.

title: Nano Dimension to Acquire Desktop Metal, Creating a Leader in Additive Manufacturing
summary: Nano Dimension announced its intent to acquire Desktop Metal in an all-cash transaction for $5.50 per share, potentially adjustable down to $4.07 per share. This merger aims to create a leader in additive manufacturing with complementary product offerings, a strong financial profile, and a focus on profitability and growth through greater scale. The combined entity expects to generate significant value by accelerating the shift to designed-for-manufacturing applications and expanding customer penetration across various high-growth industries.

title: Cathie Wood’s ETFs sell Tesla’s stock for the first time in 9 months
summary: Cathie Wood's Ark Innovation ETF sold 56,425 shares of Tesla stock, worth $13.05 million, marking the first sale in nine months after the stock experienced a significant rally. The sale indicates a shift from previous months where the ETF had been acquiring Tesla shares during dips.

title: Cathie Wood S Ark Sells Tesla Shares For 15 Million
summary: Cathie Wood's Ark Invest has sold Tesla shares worth $14.54 million from its ARK Innovation ETF and ARK Next Generation Internet ETF, marking its first sale since October 2023. Despite the selloff, Tesla remains the largest holding in Ark's portfolio, valued at $891.89 million. Concurrently, Ark Invest made new investments in Palantir, Archer Aviation Inc., Intellia Therapeutics Inc., and Recurion Pharmaceuticals Inc.

title: Nano Dimension to acquire outstanding shares of Desktop Metal; Deal could be worth as little as $135m
summary: Nano Dimension is set to acquire Desktop Metal for $135-183 million, pending shareholder and regulatory approvals. The acquisition aims to create a leader in digital manufacturing with a broadened product portfolio across various materials and is expected to generate over $30 million in run-rate synergies. The deal is subject to potential downward adjustments in share price based on transaction expenses and facility loans.

title: Ford U.S. EV Sales Improved In June And Q2 2024
summary: Ford reported improved U.S. EV sales in June and Q2 2024, maintaining its position as the No. 2 brand after Tesla. While overall vehicle sales were down in June, EV sales saw significant year-over-year increases across all models, with strong growth for the F-150 Lightning and E-Transit van, despite a challenging period for the Mustang Mach-E.

title: With its stock price rising for seven consecutive days, has Tesla passed its darkest moment?
summary: Tesla's stock has risen for seven consecutive days, returning to early year highs after strong Q2 delivery volumes exceeding expectations and record energy storage deployments. While some analysts are bullish, raising target prices based on stabilizing EV demand and energy storage contributions, others maintain sell ratings due to concerns about delivery declines and ambitious sales targets. The future stock trend is being watched closely, with technical indicators and upcoming financial reports and Robotaxi unveilings potentially influencing its trajectory.

title: Legal Fee Tracker: Lawyers' $170 million payday in limbo in credit card swipe fee case
summary: A $170 million legal fee for lawyers who negotiated an antitrust settlement with Visa and Mastercard is now uncertain after a federal judge rejected the proposed deal. The judge found the settlement, intended to limit credit card swipe fees, did not go far enough to benefit merchants despite the substantial legal fees approved. This rejection means attorneys will need to seek further concessions from Visa and Mastercard, likely prolonging the litigation.

title: EV maker Lucid beats quarterly deliveries estimates helped by price cuts
summary: Lucid Group (LCID.O) exceeded second-quarter delivery estimates, delivering 2,394 vehicles, largely due to price cuts on its luxury electric sedans. The company is on track to meet its annual production forecast of 9,000 units for 2024, with increased production expected in the second half of the year. Lucid also plans to start manufacturing its Gravity SUV later this year, with an estimated capital expenditure of $1.5 billion.

title: After 9 consecutive increases, can Tesla continue to rise?
summary: Tesla's stock has seen nine consecutive days of increases, reversing its earlier decline and rising over 75% since its April low, largely due to better-than-expected quarterly deliveries and the focus on its energy storage business. Despite this recent surge, analysts like Barclays' Dan Levy remain cautious due to intense competition and a slowdown in EV demand, while Wedbush's Dan Ives is more optimistic, seeing Tesla as an undervalued AI company. The company is set to announce quarterly results on July 23rd and launch its self-driving taxi on August 8th.

title: Tesla rally pushes stock to biggest-ever ARKK weighting
summary: Tesla's significant stock rally has propelled it to its highest-ever weighting, at 15.4%, in Cathie Wood’s ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK), even after the fund trimmed its positions. This marks the strongest conviction in Tesla for the ETF over its 10-year history. Despite ARKK seeing outflows and a drop this year, Wood remains confident, having updated her forecast for Tesla's stock to reach $2,600 by 2029.

title: Tesla Is Now Biggest-Ever Weighting in ARKK After 80% Stock Gain
summary: Tesla's significant stock rally, exceeding 80% since April, has elevated its weighting in Cathie Wood's ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK) to an all-time high of 15.4%. This makes Tesla the largest component of the $6.2 billion fund, even after recent position trimming by ARK.

title: Baidu Gets a Boost on Suggestions Its Robotaxi Business Is Taking Off
summary: Baidu's American Depositary Receipts (ADRs) rose on reports that its Apollo Go robotaxi business is expected to break even in Wuhan this year, according to analyst Li Muhua. The robotaxi service is currently conducting manned tests in 11 Chinese cities and unmanned tests in several others, with operating costs anticipated to fall significantly. The positive outlook for Baidu's autonomous driving efforts is also fueled by the upcoming launch of Tesla's robotaxi in August.

title: Ryder Releases an Economic Study for Converting Diesel Fleets to Electric
summary: Ryder System, Inc. released an economic study, "Charged Logistics: The Cost of Electric Vehicle Conversion for U.S. Commercial Fleets," detailing the potential costs of converting diesel fleets to electric vehicles (EVs). The study analyzed scenarios in California and Georgia, finding significant cost increases—up to 114% for heavy-duty tractors—and estimating that these increases could add 0.5% to 1% to overall inflation if passed to consumers. Ryder's analysis highlights that current EV technology and infrastructure are not yet cost-effective for commercial fleets compared to diesel, especially for heavier vehicles, suggesting that rapid forced transitions would lead to higher transportation costs and supply chain disruptions.

title: Chinese Upstart Leapmotor Snags Lucrative Deal to Take EVs Global
summary: Chinese electric vehicle manufacturer Leapmotor, despite being less known than rivals like BYD and Tesla, has secured a significant deal with Stellantis NV. This partnership aims to expand Leapmotor's electric vehicles into global markets, a move anticipated to transform the company's international presence. The deal highlights Leapmotor's cost controls and technological expertise as key factors in its success.

title: News | Simon Property Group, BP To Add Electric Vehicle Chargers at US Retail Locations
summary: BP Pulse, the electric vehicle charging arm of BP, has partnered with Simon Property Group to install more than 900 ultra-fast EV charging stations at 75 of Simon's retail locations across the U.S. The first stations are expected to open in early 2026. This collaboration is part of BP's larger plan to invest $1 billion in U.S. EV infrastructure by 2030, expanding its presence in the public EV charging market.

title: bp to Build EV Charging Stations at Simon Properties
summary: bp pulse, bp’s global electric vehicle charging business, has partnered with Simon Property Group to install and operate EV charging stations at 75 of Simon's shopping, dining, and entertainment sites. This deal will establish over 900 ultra-fast charging bays, with the first locations expected to open in early 2026. This expansion aligns with bp's commitment to invest $1 billion in U.S. EV charging by 2030, and positions bp to potentially acquire Tesla's Supercharging network in North America following recent shake-ups.

title: Tractor Supply may have thought it solved a big problem. Now it has a few more.
summary: Tractor Supply recently retreated from its diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) and climate goals after an "anti-woke" social media protest, which has since sparked a new backlash. Experts suggest this move was poorly considered, potentially alienating a diverse customer base and creating internal instability. The company now faces challenges including reputational damage, the risk of losing customers with alternatives, and internal uncertainty among employees and investors.

title: Which New Cars Have Free Maintenance?
summary: Many new car brands offer complimentary scheduled maintenance as an incentive, varying in duration and coverage. Brands like Jaguar, BMW, and Hyundai provide extensive free service, while others, such as Ford, offer reward programs that can be used for maintenance. Buyers should review specific program details, as these benefits can save a few hundred dollars on initial ownership costs and establish a service relationship with dealers.

title: What’s Bidirectional Charging and Which EVs Offer It?
summary: Bidirectional charging is an emerging EV technology that allows electricity to flow both to and from an EV's battery, enabling capabilities like vehicle-to-grid (V2G), vehicle-to-home (V2H), and vehicle-to-load (V2L). This technology allows EVs to power homes during outages, feed power back to the grid, or run appliances, offering benefits like energy cost savings and backup power. While still in its early stages, a growing number of EVs from manufacturers like Ford, Nissan, Hyundai, GM, and Tesla are beginning to offer various forms of bidirectional charging, with experts predicting it will become a standard feature in the coming years.

title: What Does Broadcom’s Stock Split Mean for Investors?
summary: Broadcom (AVGO) is implementing a 10-for-1 stock split after market close on July 12, distributing nine additional shares for each one owned. While the split will reduce the per-share price to make the stock more accessible to smaller investors, it does not change the company's fundamental valuation or Morningstar's fair value estimate, which will adjust from $1,550 to $155 per share post-split. This move follows other high-profile stock splits by companies like Nvidia, Walmart, Alphabet, Tesla, and Amazon, aiming to improve liquidity and make shares psychologically more attractive.

title: EV maker VinFast slashes full-year sales target by 20% as U.S. factory is delayed
summary: VinFast has delayed the opening of its electric vehicle factory in North Carolina by three years to 2028 and reduced its full-year sales target by 20% to 80,000 units. The company cites a need to manage short-term spending more effectively and focus resources on immediate growth, acknowledging market volatility and challenges in the global EV market. Despite the delay and revised forecast, VinFast anticipates stronger sales growth in the second half of 2024 by expanding its global distribution network.

title: BTCZ Stock Price | T-Rex 2X Inverse Bitcoin Daily Target ETF
summary: This article provides details on the T-Rex 2X Inverse Bitcoin Daily Target ETF (BTCZ), including its current stock price of 5.28, key statistics, and performance metrics. It lists the ETF's trading on the NYSE and outlines its investment strategy, which involves leveraging derivatives to implement a short strategy in Bitcoin. Additionally, it offers historical data, dividend information, and technical analysis perspectives for potential investors.

title: Tradr ETFs Announces Leverage Increases Now in Effect for SARK, TSLQ and NVDS
summary: Tradr ETFs has announced increased leverage factors for three of their inverse ETFs: SARK, TSLQ, and NVDS, effective immediately. This change allows sophisticated investors and professional traders to gain additional short exposure to ARKK, Tesla, and NVIDIA, respectively. The company plans to continue redefining how investors use leverage to magnify returns, while also emphasizing the significant risks associated with these highly leveraged and inverse products.

title: Aehr Test Systems Reports Fiscal 2024 Fourth Quarter and Full Year Financial Results and Provides Fiscal 2025 Full Year Financial Guidance
summary: Aehr Test Systems announced its fiscal 2024 fourth-quarter and full-year financial results, reporting record full-year revenue of $66.2 million and providing fiscal 2025 guidance of at least $70 million in revenue. The company highlighted strong performance driven by silicon carbide demand for EVs, emerging opportunities in AI processor testing, and new markets like gallium nitride and silicon photonics. Additionally, Aehr acquired Incal Technology to expand its burn-in solutions for AI accelerators.

title: The AI Revolution Continues
summary: This article examines the ongoing AI revolution through the lens of WisdomTree's AI & Innovation Fund (WTAI) and U.S. Quality Growth Fund (QGRW), highlighting investor behavior and performance attribution within the sector. It specifically focuses on the sustainability of Nvidia's share price given its significant exposure in QGRW, contrasting it with WTAI's broader AI company base. Potential market catalysts for the latter half of 2024 include new AI hardware and potential interest rate cuts.

title: Shares rise as traders bet on Trump victory
summary: Seoul shares closed higher on Tuesday as traders reacted to the increased likelihood of a Donald Trump victory in the U.S. presidential election, which market participants believe could lead to corporate tax cuts and looser regulations. Technology and auto shares surged, with Samsung Electronics, SK hynix, Hyundai Motor, and Kia all seeing significant gains. Conversely, battery shares plummeted due to negative outlooks for the electric vehicle industry.

title: VCR: Consumer Discretionary Sector Dashboard For July (NYSEARCA:VCR)
summary: This monthly article series provides a dashboard with aggregate subsector metrics in the Consumer Discretionary sector, highlighting that consumer services are the most attractive subsector based on value and quality scores. It also points out that the auto and components industry is below its baseline for both scores. The article suggests Vanguard Consumer Discretionary Index Fund ETF Shares (VCR) as an alternative to XLY and identifies nine stocks that were cheaper than their peers in July 2024.

title: What to Do if Your Car’s Manufacturer Shuts Down
summary: This article provides advice for car owners whose vehicle manufacturer has gone out of business, a situation increasingly common with new automotive startups. It highlights the experiences of Fisker Ocean owners facing bankruptcy and suggests strategies such as organizing with other owners, gathering spare parts and technical knowledge, and being prepared to get creative with repairs. The author also touches on issues like insurance complications and the broader implications of connected cars and software dependence when a company folds.

title: Stocks to Buy: 7 Founder-Led Companies Set to Outperform
summary: This article highlights seven founder-led companies that are expected to outperform the market, drawing on research that suggests such firms are more innovative and adaptable. It features Tesla (TSLA), Nvidia (NVDA), Figs (FIGS), Regeneron Pharmaceuticals (REGN), Crowdstrike Holdings (CRWD), On Holding (ONON), and Hyatt Hotels (H). Each company's unique strengths, market position, and growth potential under founder leadership are discussed, providing insights into why they are considered strong investment opportunities.

title: Tesla Cybertruck off-road mode detailed
summary: Tesla has detailed the off-road modes for its Cybertruck, available via a May software update. These modes, including Overland, Baja, and Wade, adjust vehicle characteristics and offer driver aids for various terrains. The Cybertruck can also ford up to 32 inches of water for a limited time using Wade mode and features Trail Assist for low-speed off-road cruise control.

title: XPeng and Baidu lead China's rise in autonomous driving tech
summary: China's electric vehicle (EV) technology has advanced rapidly, with its autonomous driving sector now quickly catching up to Western countries. Companies like XPeng and Baidu are at the forefront, with XPeng offering a flagship autonomous driving suite comparable to Tesla's FSD, and Baidu developing its robotaxi project. China's regulatory environment is supporting the development of higher-level autonomous driving, with analysts predicting the country will dominate Level 3 autonomous vehicles.

title: Retail Investors Pile Into Small Cap Stocks at Record Pace as Fed Rate-Cut Hopes Rise
summary: Retail investors have been aggressively buying small-cap stocks, driven by increasing hopes for a Federal Reserve interest rate cut. This surge saw a record $108.8 million inflow into the iShares Russell 2000 ETF on Tuesday, with analysts suggesting this uptick in retail purchases of U.S. stocks may continue. The shift into small caps has coincided with a modest reduction in large-cap inflows, although investors remain active dip buyers in the tech sector.

title: Skild AI估值100亿，今年最快独角兽诞生了
summary: Skild AI，一家由两位前卡内基梅隆大学教授于2023年5月创立的具身智能初创公司，在成立仅一年后完成了3亿美元的A轮融资，估值达到15亿美元（约109亿人民币），成为今年增长最快的独角兽之一。该公司致力于开发通用人工智能（AGI）驱动的人形机器人产品，专注于构建可应用于各种机器人的通用大脑Skild Brain。尽管融资火热，具身智能领域也面临商业化前景不明、大厂竞争激烈以及行业泡沫等挑战，如李开复等投资人对其短期落地和人形机器人必要性持保留态度。

title: KraneShares Hedgeye Hedged Equity Index ETF
