summary: This page provides a detailed profile of the KraneShares Hedgeye Hedged Equity Index ETF (KSPY), including its current price, dividend information, expense ratio, holdings, and performance data. It also offers various charts for price, volume, and fund flows, along with ESG scores and technical analysis, aiming to provide financial advisors and investors with comprehensive data for portfolio construction and analysis.

title: Business-Friendly Policies Drive Corporate Relocations to Texas
summary: Texas is seeing a significant influx of corporate relocations from states like California, driven by its business-friendly policies, lower living costs, and absence of corporate income tax. This migration, exemplified by Elon Musk's companies and other Fortune 500 businesses, has led to substantial tax revenue gains for Texas while California experiences financial losses and rising office vacancy rates. The article also touches on political alignments in Silicon Valley, with some tech leaders now supporting Donald Trump.

title: Will Newmont’s Q2 Results Shine On Strong Gold Pricing?
summary: Newmont's Q2 results are anticipated to benefit from strong gold pricing and increased production following its Newcrest acquisition. The company expects significant synergies and improved margins, despite its stock underperforming the S&P 500 in recent years. The long-term outlook for precious metals, including gold and copper production, remains positive for Newmont.

title: Chip design software firm Cadence forecasts third-quarter results below estimates
summary: Cadence Design Systems forecast third-quarter revenue and profit below Wall Street estimates, signaling intense competition from rival Synopsys. Despite this, the company raised its fiscal 2024 revenue forecast while trimming its annual profit per share outlook. Cadence's shares fell following the announcement, although its second-quarter results slightly beat estimates.

title: DuPont Capital calls time on a 30-year external client business
summary: DuPont Capital is ending its 30-year external client business to concentrate solely on managing its parent company's pension assets. This strategic shift will allow DuPont Capital to refocus its efforts internally.

title: 3 ETFs That Are Outperforming the S&P 500
summary: This article highlights three Exchange Traded Funds (ETFs) that have successfully outperformed the S&P 500 year-to-date, making them potentially attractive additions to investor portfolios. These include the Roundhill Magnificent Seven ETF (MAGS) focusing on tech giants, the Invesco S&P 500 Top 50 ETF (XLG) concentrating on the S&P 500's largest companies, and the iShares Semiconductor ETF (SOXX) which benefits from the booming semiconductor industry, particularly AI chips. The article details their performance, major holdings, and expense ratios.

title: Elon Musk: CrowdStrike outage caused ‘seizure’ for auto supply chain
summary: Elon Musk confirmed that Tesla was impacted by the recent CrowdStrike IT outage, stating it caused a "seizure" for the automotive supply chain and led to Tesla deleting the cybersecurity software from all its systems. While some Tesla production lines were shut down, analysts suggest the overall impact on the auto industry was less severe than previous disruptions like the CDK Global cyberattack. The incident highlights ongoing concerns about cybersecurity in the automotive sector, with experts emphasizing the need for robust internal controls and better quality-control mechanisms.

title: Tesla Delays Robotaxi Event by Two Months to Oct. 10
summary: Tesla has delayed its robotaxi unveiling event by two months to October 10th, following design changes requested by CEO Elon Musk. This event is crucial for investors to assess Tesla's self-driving technology, which Musk sees as fundamental to the company's future, envisioning fully autonomous ride-hailing services.

title: Breakingviews - Donald Trump might be bad for EVs, good for Tesla
summary: Elon Musk's endorsement of Donald Trump could paradoxically benefit Tesla, even if Trump's policies hurt the broader EV industry. By potentially rolling back government support and increasing tariffs, Trump's actions would disproportionately impact Tesla's competitors, who rely more on subsidies and imported parts, and struggle to make a profit on EVs. This scenario could solidify Tesla's dominance in a shrinking market, allowing Musk to focus on other ventures like self-driving taxis and humanoid robots.

title: Rivian to face trial in Tesla trade secrets theft case, judge says
summary: A California judge has tentatively ruled that electric car maker Rivian Automotive will face trial over allegations by Tesla that it encouraged former Tesla employees to steal trade secrets. The judge denied Rivian's motion to dismiss the lawsuit, citing sufficient evidence presented by Tesla, including instances where some Rivian employees were not thoroughly investigated or disciplined regarding the alleged trade thefts. The legal dispute began in 2020, and a final hearing is scheduled for Wednesday.

title: YieldMax™ Launches Short NVDA Option Income Strategy ETF (DIPS)
summary: YieldMax™ has launched the Short NVDA Option Income Strategy ETF (DIPS), an actively managed ETF designed to generate current income from a synthetic covered put strategy on NVIDIA Corporation. DIPS seeks to provide indirect short exposure to NVDA's share price and aims to benefit from decreases in NVDA's stock while also generating income through option writing and U.S. Treasuries. The ETF's potential gains from NVDA price decreases are limited, and potential losses from increases can be up to 100%.

title: Tesla, Google Spark Sell-Off in Technology ETFs
summary: Tesla and Alphabet's Q2 earnings results triggered a significant sell-off in the technology sector, with the Nasdaq Composite dropping 3.6%. Tesla's weaker-than-expected performance and Alphabet's lower-than-consensus YouTube ad revenues impacted the "Magnificent 7" stocks and several technology-focused ETFs. Investors are now keenly awaiting the upcoming earnings reports from other major tech companies like Microsoft, Apple, Amazon, and Nvidia.

title: Ford slips as higher costs, EV unit take a toll on profit growth
summary: Ford Motor Company's shares tumbled over 13% after missing second-quarter profit estimates due to struggles with quality-related costs and intense competition in its EV business. The company reported an adjusted profit of 47 cents per share, below analysts' expectations of 68 cents, largely impacted by an $800 million increase in warranty expenses. Despite these setbacks and ongoing quality issues, Ford expects warranty costs to align with expectations in the second half of the year and continues to offer quarterly and special dividends.

title: Gasgoo Daily: Tesla seeks regulatory approval for supervised FSD in China, Europe
summary: Tesla is seeking regulatory approval for its supervised Full Self-Driving (FSD) system in Europe and China, with CEO Elon Musk expecting approval by the end of the year. This move highlights Tesla's continued expansion plans alongside other Chinese automotive companies like XPENG, Xiaomi EV, Geely, Foton Motor, Hesai Technology, Changan Automobile, and SAIC Motor making significant global and technological advancements. The article also covers other key developments in the Chinese automotive industry, including XPENG's entry into Singapore, 360 Security's partnership with MHERO Technology, and various companies expanding their global market presence and technological collaborations.

title: Study: Traditional brands' EVs surpass Tesla in satisfaction
summary: A J.D. Power study reveals that owners of electric vehicles from traditional brands are more satisfied with their cars than Tesla owners, marking a significant shift in buyer perception. This trend is attributed to improvements in non-Tesla EVs and a changing demographic of Tesla buyers who are new to the brand and less impressed than loyal fans. Porsche, Kia, and Hyundai are highlighted among the top brands for EV appeal.

title: Q&A with Om Shankar: “Konect by GVR is the next piece of the Vontier EV story”
summary: Om Shankar, VP & General Manager at Konect, discusses the launch of Konect by GVR, an EV charging platform for convenience stores and service stations. He emphasizes Konect's "watt-to-wheels" strategy, which aims to provide a comprehensive, profitable, and vertically integrated EV charging ecosystem for fuel retailers. Shankar highlights the importance of offering a robust and reliable charging experience that leverages existing retail infrastructure and addresses the profitability challenges faced by many stations in the EV transition.

title: Mitsubishi Motors to join Honda-Nissan alliance, Nikkei reports
summary: Mitsubishi Motors is reportedly set to join an alliance with Honda Motor and Nissan Motor, according to the Nikkei newspaper, creating a major tie-up in the automotive industry. This collaboration aims to standardize in-vehicle software and help the Japanese automakers cut costs and better compete in the electric vehicle market against rivals like China's BYD and Tesla. The potential move comes as Nissan faces market share losses and recently cut its annual outlook.

title: President Trump Wants America to Become the World’s “Bitcoin Superpower”
summary: Former President Donald Trump, speaking at the Bitcoin 2024 conference, endorsed Bitcoin, stating he wants America to lead in digital assets and become a "Bitcoin superpower." He outlined plans including clear regulations, protecting self-custody, increasing electricity output, and establishing a strategic Bitcoin reserve. The article also touches on the current election dynamics with Kamala Harris's surging campaign and mixed U.S. economic signals.

title: Mytra: Helping Firms like Albertsons Transform Warehouses
summary: Mytra, a company founded by former Tesla and Rivian robotics and manufacturing leaders, is revolutionizing warehouse operations with its 3D robotics and AI capabilities. They are working with major companies like Albertsons to automate material flow, reducing complexity and increasing efficiency in a sector that has seen little change in decades. Mytra recently secured $78 million in Series B funding to scale its technology and expand its team.

title: 5 Reasons to Choose Short-Term Car Loans
summary: Short-term car loans can be a financially sound choice for those who can afford higher monthly payments, as they typically lead to significant savings on total interest. Benefits include lower interest rates, faster equity building, potential insurance savings, and more disposable income for other investments once the loan is repaid. However, borrowers must ensure they can handle the increased monthly payment to avoid financial stress.

title: VinFast Delays Production After North Carolina Seizes Property for Factory Site
summary: VinFast, a Vietnamese electric vehicle manufacturer, has delayed the start of production at its North Carolina plant until 2028, four years later than initially planned. This delay comes after North Carolina taxpayers have already committed over $96 million and the state has used eminent domain to seize private properties, including homes and a church, to prepare the site for the factory and associated infrastructure like roadway improvements. The situation highlights the challenges and consequences of large-scale incentive deals and the use of eminent domain for economic development.

title: How Eli Lilly went from pharmaceutical slowpoke to $791 billion juggernaut
summary: Eli Lilly has transformed from a slow-moving pharmaceutical company into a $791 billion powerhouse, largely due to the success of its weight-loss drugs, Mounjaro and Zepbound. The company's focus on long-term R&D, including in challenging areas like Alzheimer's and diabetes, allowed it to develop innovative medicines like tirzepatide, which became a blockbuster for both diabetes and obesity. Despite facing supply chain challenges due to overwhelming demand, Lilly is aggressively investing in manufacturing and future drug development, aiming to maintain its leading position and accelerate its development processes.

title: ETF Opportunities Trust - T-Rex 2X Inverse Tesla Daily Target ETF
summary: This article provides a snapshot of the ETF Opportunities Trust - T-Rex 2X Inverse Tesla Daily Target ETF (TSLZ), detailing its current stock price and key technical indicators. It highlights the stock's performance with a real-time price update and links to various financial analysis tools available on CMLViz.com.

title: Tesla testing 320+ kw output with Superchargers and Cybertruck
summary: Tesla is reportedly testing Supercharger stations with outputs exceeding 320 kW, significantly higher than the typical 250 kW peak. This trial, confirmed by Cybertruck lead engineer Wes Morrill, is occurring at V3+ Supercharger stations. While Tesla's Supercharger team faces uncertainty, this development suggests advancements in charging capabilities, particularly for vehicles like the Cybertruck.

title: Tesla to fix 1.7 mln vehicles in China, says market regulator
summary: Tesla is initiating a remote recall of 1.7 million vehicles in China due to a software issue that could prevent the front trunk lid from being detected as unlocked, posing safety risks. The recall, implemented via an over-the-air update, affects imported Model S and X vehicles as well as China-made Model 3 and Y vehicles in Tesla's second-largest market.

title: Albemarle says help is needed to beat China
summary: Albemarle Corp. CEO Kent Masters is calling for international and governmental assistance to counter China's dominance in essential mineral markets for EV batteries, as the company faces significant challenges from slumping lithium prices. The world's largest lithium miner reported a substantial net loss and is undergoing a cost structure review, while it scales back expansion plans and cuts jobs due to market conditions. Masters argues that despite the Inflation Reduction Act, the West is not effectively building its own supply chains to compete with China.

title: Tesla Electric partners with SunRun to bolster Texas grid
summary: Tesla Electric and SunRun have partnered to enroll customers in a program that will use Tesla Powerwall battery packs to support the Texas power grid during high demand. Participants, who must be SunRun customers with solar panels and Powerwall batteries using Tesla Electric, will receive $400 per Powerwall for the 2024 calendar year, while SunRun will also earn incremental revenue. This initiative aims to bolster grid stability, particularly in the face of severe weather events, and marks SunRun's first pilot program in Texas.

title: ECD Automotive Design Reveals One-Off Custom All-Electric Jaguar Series III E-Type Restomod
summary: E.C.D. Automotive Design has unveiled a custom all-electric Jaguar Series III E-Type restomod, combining classic design with modern EV technology. This one-off "Pacific Palisades Commission" features an Eiger Gray exterior, Spinneybeck Amalfi red leather interior, and an in-house developed ECD Custom Electric propulsion system with a 42 kWh battery offering up to 150 miles of range. The vehicle integrates Tesla components for its drive systems and includes modern amenities like a JL Audio system and an EV interface, showcasing ECD's blend of timeless elegance and innovation.

title: Citron Research has been charged by the SEC with market manipulation, including shares of Freedom Holding
summary: The SEC has charged activist investor Andrew Left and Citron Capital with market manipulation for allegedly misleading investors with false stock recommendations. Left is accused of publishing recommendations and then quickly reversing his own positions to profit from the resulting stock price movements, generating an estimated $20 million in illegal gains. The article details Left's history of unethical practices and mentions his past accusations against Freedom Holding Corp., which were later disproven by an independent audit.

title: Sunrun storage attachment rate jumps to 54% as CEO cheers rising VPP potential
summary: Sunrun reported a significant jump in its energy storage attachment rate to 54% in Q2 2024, driven by a "storage-first strategy" and increased customer demand for resiliency. Despite a drop in solar installations, the company is focusing on virtual power plants (VPPs) and strategic partnerships, including with Tesla Electric, to capitalize on evolving market opportunities and enhance grid services. Sunrun sees potential for market share growth following a competitor's bankruptcy and anticipates increased investment tax credit adders to boost business.

title: China auto market hits milestone as EVs, hybrids make up half of July sales
summary: In July, new energy vehicles (NEVs), including pure electric and plug-in hybrids, comprised a record 50.7% of all vehicle sales in China, marking a significant milestone in EV adoption. This growth, a 37% jump from the previous year, highlights China's substantial investments in EV supply chains that have propelled its domestic industry. While NEV sales surged, overall domestic car sales declined for the fourth consecutive month due to weak consumer confidence and economic struggles, prompting the government to double cash subsidies for vehicle purchases.

title: ChargePoint Eliminates EV Charging Connector Confusion with Omni Port Adaptable Charging Solution
summary: ChargePoint has announced Omni Port, an EV connector solution designed to eliminate confusion and the need for adapters for EV drivers. This technology combines most common connector types into one charging port, making any EV compatible with any charging space. It will be integrated into select ChargePoint AC and DC charging stations at no additional cost and can be retrofitted to existing models later this year, ensuring a frictionless charging experience for all EV drivers.

title: WeRide May Seek Up to $400 Million in US IPO, Placement
summary: China's WeRide Inc. is reportedly seeking up to $400 million through a U.S. initial public offering and a concurrent private placement. The autonomous vehicle company plans to raise about $100 million in the IPO and $200 million to $300 million in the placement, launching as soon as next week. This IPO would be a significant listing for a Chinese company in the U.S. since Didi Global Inc.'s 2021 offering.

title: China reaches milestone with over 50% of July vehicle sales as electric or hybrid
summary: Over half of all vehicles sold in China in July were new energy vehicles (NEVs), marking a significant milestone in EV adoption and highlighting China's rapid advancement in the sector. This surge, with NEV sales up 37% year-on-year, positions China ahead of Western markets like the U.S. Domestic brands like BYD and Li Auto are setting new sales records due to heavy investments in the EV supply chain and government incentives, despite a decline in overall domestic car sales.

title: ON Semiconductor Corporation (ON): A Humanoid Robot Stock Approved by Morgan Stanley?
summary: This article examines ON Semiconductor Corporation (NASDAQ:ON) as a potential investment in the humanoid robot sector, based on a Morgan Stanley report highlighting the immense projected growth of humanoid robots. Morgan Stanley predicts the humanoid population could reach 63 million by 2050, resulting in a $3 trillion wage impact. While ON Semiconductor is listed as a key "enabler" in this market due to its sensor and power management products, the article also notes challenges such as its reliance on the automotive industry and limited benefit from CHIPS Act funding.

title: Enphase Energy’s Level 2 home EV chargers now come with NACS connectors
summary: Enphase Energy has launched North American Charging Standard (NACS) connectors for its entire line of Level 2 home IQ EV chargers, offering compatibility with Teslas and other NACS-compatible EVs. These chargers range from 32 to 64 amps, feature a fully sealed NEMA 4-rated enclosure for versatile indoor/outdoor use, and can integrate with the Enphase Energy System for solar-powered EV charging. The system also includes energy management and custom scheduling through the Enphase app, along with a 25-foot cable that automatically opens Tesla's charge port.

title: Trump and Musk discussed firing striking workers. The UAW is now seeking an NLRB investigation
summary: The United Auto Workers (UAW) union has filed unfair labor practice charges against Donald Trump and Elon Musk after a social media conversation where they discussed firing striking workers. The UAW alleges that their comments interfered with workers' rights to unionize, and the NLRB is investigating. Trump's campaign calls the charges "frivolous," while UAW President Shawn Fain states that both Trump and Musk are anti-labor.

title: 1,000% stock increase: The story behind Eli Lilly’s pharmaceutical dominance
summary: Eli Lilly's stock has surged by nearly 1,000% in a decade, reaching a record value of $840 billion, driven by the success of its weight loss and diabetes drugs, Zepbound and Mounjaro. The company, now one of the ten largest globally by market cap, has invested heavily in innovation and new manufacturing facilities to meet overwhelming demand, even surpassing competitors like Novo Nordisk in some drug sales. Eli Lilly aims to become the first healthcare company worth a trillion dollars, planning to launch over 20 new drugs in the next decade.

title: Wall Street Just Got Its Most Volatile ETF as Risky Bets Boom
summary: Defiance has launched the Defiance Daily Target 1.75X Long MSTR ETF (MSTX), a new leveraged ETF designed to offer daily returns on MicroStrategy Inc. This ETF is expected to be the most volatile in the US due to MicroStrategy's 90-day volatility of 97%, driven by its significant Bitcoin holdings. The emergence of such leveraged single-stock ETFs reflects a growing market for high-volatility products, attracting significant investor interest despite warnings from the SEC about their risks, particularly for retail investors.

title: Why Is Nio Stock Down 55% Despite Surging Deliveries?
summary: Nio stock has declined by nearly 59% year-to-date despite a 43.9% year-over-year increase in deliveries, largely due to weaker-than-expected Q1 2024 results, intense competition and price wars in the Chinese EV market, and a weak Chinese economy impacting consumer spending. While Nio faces challenges from trade barriers and underperformed the S&P 500, new government incentives and the launch of its mass-market Onvo L60 sub-brand offer potential for recovery, and its valuation appears attractive.

title: Yield Max TSLA Option Income Strategy ETF: One Of The Worst Constructed Covered Call Funds
summary: The article criticizes the Yield Max TSLA Option Income Strategy ETF (TSLY), labeling it as poorly constructed for a covered call fund. It highlights TSLY's negative total returns since inception due to Tesla's volatility and its options strategy. The author concludes that TSLY's unpredictable payouts and significant NAV decline make it an unsuitable investment for most.

title: US Gives Tiny Canadian Firm Electra $20 Million to Build Cobalt Plant
summary: Electra Battery Materials Corp. received a $20 million grant from the US government to construct a cobalt sulfate refinery in Ontario. This facility, valued at $250 million, will be North America's sole refinery for cobalt sulfate, a crucial material for lithium-ion EV batteries. Electra's CEO is also engaging in funding discussions with the Canadian government for the project.

title: Can-Am Announces The Launch Of Its Pulse And Origin Electric Motorcycles
summary: Can-Am, a brand of BRP Inc., has announced the availability of its 2025 Can-Am Pulse and Origin all-electric motorcycle lineup, aiming to reclaim its two-wheel heritage with modern electric power. These models feature a proprietary Rotax E-Power unit and a liquid-cooled system designed to optimize battery life and performance. The Can-Am Pulse offers an estimated city range of up to 160 km, while the Origin provides up to 145 km, both equipped with an 8.9 kWh battery.

title: Nine-figure podcast deals are back. Will it go any better this time?
summary: SiriusXM is investing heavily in podcasting, signing nine-figure deals similar to Spotify's past spending spree, but with a different strategy. Unlike Spotify's initial exclusive-platform approach, SiriusXM is focusing on acquiring advertising and distribution rights across multiple platforms and offering premium content through subscriptions. This new model aims for better economies of scale by reaching a broader audience, which could be a more sustainable approach than previous exclusivity attempts.

title: Tesla's steep price reductions are fueling used eletric vehicle sales
summary: Tesla's recent price cuts on new vehicles are significantly boosting sales of used electric vehicles across the US, making them more affordable for a wider range of buyers. This trend, observed at dealerships like CarMax, is further supported by government incentives such as $4,000 tax credits for used EVs. The increased supply of pre-owned EVs and Tesla's relatively unchanged models also contribute to the appeal of used electric cars.

title: Canada’s Cobalt Cache: Pentagon Invests US$20 Million on Electra Battery Materials
summary: The Pentagon has invested US$20 million in Electra Battery Materials Corporation to establish a cobalt refinery in Temiskaming Shores, Ontario. This funding, from the Ukraine Supplemental Appropriations Act of 2022, aims to boost domestic production of critical minerals and secure the supply chain for lithium-ion batteries essential for defense and EV sectors. The Ontario facility will be North America's first cobalt sulfate hydrometallurgical plant, producing a key component for over a million EVs annually at full capacity.

title: Longtime Tesla finance VP bids farewell
summary: Sreela Venkataratnam, Tesla's VP of Finance and Business Operations, has resigned after 11 years with the company, as announced in an August 21 LinkedIn post. Acknowledged as one of Tesla's top women executives, Venkataratnam noted that working for Tesla is "not for the faint of heart" but described her tenure as "extraordinary." Her departure leaves Laurie Shelby as the only woman on Tesla's executive team, and follows other high-profile exits from the company in recent months.

title: This ETF Could Be the Easiest Way to Own the Future of AI
summary: The article discusses the Ark Autonomous Technology & Robotics ETF (NYSEMKT: ARKQ) as an easy way for investors to gain exposure to the AI sector. Managed by Cathie Wood, this actively managed ETF focuses on a concentrated portfolio of AI-related companies, including Tesla and Kratos Defense and Security, rather than just the largest AI stocks. Despite its volatility, the ETF has delivered strong annualized returns of approximately 15.7% since its inception in September 2014, offering long-term investors a reasonable option for AI investment.

title: Tesla suit challenging ban on direct car sales revived by court
summary: A federal appeals court has revived Tesla Inc.'s lawsuit challenging a Louisiana law that prohibits automobile manufacturers from selling directly to consumers. The 5th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals ruled that Tesla made a plausible claim that the Louisiana Motor Vehicle Commission is biased against the company's direct sales model. This decision sends the case back to a federal district court in New Orleans for further proceedings, as Tesla continues its efforts to overcome direct sales bans in various states.

title: Milei, Elon Musk and the Lithium Triangle
summary: Javier Milei's government in Argentina is actively opening the country to foreign investment, particularly in its rich lithium reserves, a move that benefits tech tycoons like Elon Musk. This agenda involves significant legislative reforms, including those weakening environmental and land ownership protections, leading to social and environmental concerns from local communities and experts. The article highlights the tension between economic development through lithium extraction and the safeguarding of human rights, indigenous lands, and environmental standards.

title: Lotus slashes 2024 sales forecast as EU, U.S. tariffs on EVs take hold
summary: Lotus has significantly lowered its 2024 sales projection due to the impact of new tariffs imposed by the European Union and the United States on electric vehicles. This decision follows the display of a Lotus Emeya EV at the Beijing auto show. The article highlights how such tariffs are affecting the automotive industry, potentially increasing new-vehicle prices.

title: Lotus slashes delivery target over tariffs on Chinese-made EVs
summary: Lotus Technology Inc., majority-owned by China’s Geely, has significantly reduced its annual delivery target from 26,000 to 12,000 vehicles due to trade uncertainties and impending tariffs on Chinese-made EVs by the US and EU. This revised outlook, influenced by the company's base and manufacturing operations in China, comes as a setback for investors, with Lotus shares having nearly halved since its New York listing in February. The company had initially hoped its luxury EV models and LVMH partnership would help it avoid market struggles.

title: Musk, Tesla win dismissal of Dogecoin 'pyramid scheme' suit
summary: A New York federal judge has dismissed a lawsuit against Elon Musk and Tesla Inc. concerning their alleged promotion of Dogecoin as a "pyramid scheme." The judge ruled that Musk's statements about the cryptocurrency were "aspirational" and not factual, meaning "no reasonable investor" could rely on them. The investors, who claim significant losses, plan to appeal the decision.

title: Tesla to unveil its robotaxi at Warner Bros. movie studio in October
summary: Tesla plans to unveil its highly anticipated robotaxi on October 10 at Warner Bros. Discovery Inc.'s movie studio in Burbank, California. This event signifies Elon Musk's strategic shift towards autonomy and artificial intelligence, moving Tesla beyond just selling EVs to consumers. The company is developing a proprietary ride-hailing app, utilizing both purpose-built robotaxis and consumer vehicles capable of self-driving.

title: Tesla's China-made EV sales in August up 3% yr/yr
summary: Tesla's China-made electric vehicle sales increased by 3% year-on-year in August, with deliveries of its Model 3 and Model Y rising 17% from July. This growth, fueled by extended buyer incentives and government backing, suggests a strong third quarter for Tesla in China despite local sales force downsizing. Rival BYD also reported significant sales growth in the same period.

title: What's Going On With NIO Stock Today?
summary: NIO (NYSE:NIO) shares are trading lower after losing over 62% in the past year. The company is reportedly adjusting its charging model for its battery swap service in China and is set to release its Q2 financial results on September 5, 2024. NIO achieved a cumulative total of 50 million battery swap services and delivered 20,176 vehicles in August 2024.

title: Lucid Is in Trouble, Here’s Why
summary: Lucid Group Inc. (NASDAQ: LCID) is facing significant financial trouble, reporting substantial quarterly losses and burning through cash despite a recent $1.5 billion funding injection from Saudi Arabia. The luxury EV maker grapples with low production volume due to high vehicle prices over $100,000, waning EV market demand, and safety recalls. Analysts have low expectations for the stock, with no buy recommendations and heavy short interest, suggesting a challenging future for the company.

title: EV firm Polestar taps former Stellantis finance executive as CFO
summary: Polestar has appointed Jean-Francois Mady, a former Stellantis finance executive, as its new CFO, effective October 21. This appointment follows other recent executive changes at the EV maker, including a new CEO, and comes after securing $300 million in additional funding, bringing its total external funding to approximately $1.3 billion. The company aims for a double-digit gross margin by year-end and is discussing the impact of European tariffs on China-made vehicles with the European Commission.

title: Why First Solar Stock Gained 30% This Year
summary: First Solar stock has gained 30% year-to-date, outperforming its competitor Enphase Energy. This performance is attributed to solid financial results, strong demand for its solar panels, and significant benefits from U.S. government incentives under the Inflation Reduction Act. The company's focus on domestic manufacturing and rising tariffs on Chinese imports further bolster its position.

summary: This article provides detailed financial information for the GraniteShares 2x Short COIN Daily ETF (CONI), including its current stock price, trading performance across various timeframes, key metrics like net assets and expense ratio, and an overview of its investment strategy. title: Rob Arnott Is Launching an ETF Made Up of Stock-Index Rejects
summary: Rob Arnott, founder of Research Affiliates, is launching a new ETF called the Research Affiliates Deletions ETF, which will invest in companies recently removed from major stock indexes. Arnott's strategy is based on his research showing that these "reject" stocks, though deeply out of favor, tend to outperform the market by about 5% annually on a compounded basis. The fund, trading under the ticker NIXT, will hold companies for approximately five years and aims to provide exposure to value stocks frequently overlooked by mainstream index funds.

title: Liberty Global plc Class C (LBTYK) Stock Price Quote Today & Current Price Chart
summary: This article provides a real-time stock price quote and chart for Liberty Global plc Class C (LBTYK), which is trading as a CFD. The article also explains the risks and mechanisms of CFD trading for LBTYK shares.

title: Making sense of the markets this week: September 8, 2024
summary: The Bank of Canada cut interest rates for the third consecutive month, and economists anticipate further reductions, potentially reaching 3% by next summer. Meanwhile, Alimentation Couche-Tard's proposed acquisition of 7-Eleven parent company Seven & i Holdings Co. faced rejection due to shareholder best interests and anti-trust concerns, though Couche-Tard continues aggressive acquisition strategies. The article also discusses the potential end of the "Magnificent 7" stocks' outperformance trend and highlights Dollarama's strong performance compared to its U.S. dollar store counterparts.

title: Rob Arnott builds a Lazarus small cap ETF
summary: Rob Arnott's Research Affiliates, in partnership with ETF Architect, is launching the Research Affiliates Deletions ETF (NIXT) which focuses on "depressed index rejects" from the top 500 and 1,000 US companies. The ETF holds these deleted companies for five years, rebalancing annually to equal weight, based on the performance anomaly observed in companies removed from major indices. Research suggests these deleted companies often outperform original index components after their removal, with Tesla's S&P 500 inclusion and Apartment Investment and Management's deletion serving as a prime example of this dynamic.

title: Natalie Walker, partner, StepStone Group
summary: Natalie Walker, a partner at StepStone Group, has been recognized in Pensions & Investments' 2024 class of Influential Women in Investing. The article features her alongside other financial news developments, including delays in SEC work on private credit for 401(k) plans and a portfolio overhaul at the University of Illinois Foundation. It also touches on Carlyle's AUM growth and CalPERS' stance on Elon Musk's pay package.

title: The 2024 Proxy Season: Which ESG Proposals Made the Podium?
summary: Support for environmental and social resolutions in the 2024 proxy season continued to decline, while governance proposals saw a rebound. Morningstar's analysis introduces a new three-tier system (Gold, Silver, Bronze) to evaluate ESG proposals, revealing that while top-tier support has shrunk, a committed core of sustainability-conscious asset managers continues to back proposals in the lower tiers. The article also notes an increase in the number of resolutions with less than 30% adjusted support, raising questions about resolution quality and the future of shareholder democracy.

title: How to Invest in ARK Autonomous Technology & Robotics ETF (ARKQ)
summary: The ARK Autonomous Technology & Robotics ETF (ARKQ) provides diversified exposure to companies focusing on autonomous technology and robotics, targeting long-term capital growth. Key holdings include influential sectors like autonomous vehicles, with a projected 21.9% CAGR until 2030. While it offers growth potential, investors should note its 0.75% expense ratio and lack of dividend payouts, making it more suitable for long-term investors with a higher risk tolerance.

title: Algorhythm holdings insider sells over $23,000 in company stock
summary: An insider at Algorhythm Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ:RIME), Milton C. Ault III, has sold shares totaling over $23,000 in a series of transactions. These sales, conducted through Ault Lending, LLC, reflect part of regular trading activities, and follow significant strategic changes for the company, including a pivot towards AI-driven technology and potential delisting concerns from Nasdaq. Despite financial challenges and high stock volatility, InvestingPro analysts predict the company may achieve profitability this year.

title: GM, Hyundai plan global alliance on EVs, clean-energy tech
summary: General Motors Co. and Hyundai Motor Co. have signed a memorandum of understanding to explore collaboration on electric vehicles, advanced powertrains, and supply chains. This potential partnership aims to reduce costs, leverage the strengths of both automakers, and accelerate the delivery of new vehicles and clean-energy technologies to market. The companies will assess opportunities for co-development, combined sourcing of raw materials, and enhanced competitiveness in key markets.

title: ARK Loves This Drone Stock. Is It a Buy?
summary: This article analyzes Kratos Defense & Security, a drone stock that ARK Invest holds as a top position in its ARKQ ETF. While ARK is bullish, the analysis raises significant red flags, including Kratos's heavy reliance on the US government as a single customer (70% of revenue), low gross margins (under 30%), and negative free cash flow. The article contrasts Kratos with AeroVironment, suggesting the latter offers more transparency and better financial metrics for investors interested in drone technology.

title: GM collaborates with Hyundai on sourcing and production
summary: GM and Hyundai Motor have signed an agreement to collaborate on vehicle and engine production, including electric and hydrogen technologies, and will review combined sourcing of battery materials and steel. This partnership aims to reduce costs, expand vehicle offerings, and bring technologies to market faster by leveraging their respective scales and strengths. Concurrently, GM recently finalized a joint venture with Samsung SDI for a $3.5 billion battery cell manufacturing plant for EVs, emphasizing the growing need for collaboration and transparency in the evolving EV supply chain.

title: 2024 Hyundai IONIQ 5: Where Efficiency Meets Fun | News
summary: The 2024 Hyundai IONIQ 5 is highlighted as an electric vehicle that combines efficiency with fun, having won multiple awards including World Car of the Year. The article praises its performance, fast charging capabilities, retro-modern design, and advanced technology. It also emphasizes the IONIQ 5's competitive pricing and robust warranty, making it an attractive option in the EV market.

title: Gauzy: The Israeli Innovator Optimising Safety, Efficiency For OEMs
summary: Gauzy, an Israeli company, is innovating in light control technology and ADAS ecosystems to enhance safety and efficiency in the automotive and aviation industries. Their solutions address issues like motion sickness in autonomous vehicles, replace traditional mirrors with Smart-Vision CMS, and optimize cabin space and energy efficiency in EVs through dynamic light regulation. Gauzy's technology is gaining traction globally, with a significant presence in Europe and the US, and an expanding footprint in commercial and passenger vehicles.

title: Ford Motor Co’s Knickers In A Twist About Accurate Characterization Of EV Failures
summary: The article criticizes Ford CEO Jim Farley for his perceived belated realization of the threat posed by Chinese EV manufacturers, despite his long tenure in global market roles. The author, Michael Barnard, suggests that Ford is defending its failures rather than innovating, and highlights Farley's reported shock at the superiority of Chinese EVs. The piece uses Richard Rumelt's "Good Strategy Bad Strategy" to emphasize the importance of accepting reality in strategic planning, implying Ford has failed to do so.

title: STMicroelectronics unveils new generation of silicon carbide power technology tailored for next-generation EV traction inverters
summary: STMicroelectronics is introducing its fourth-generation STPOWER silicon carbide (SiC) MOSFET technology, optimized for EV traction inverters and industrial applications. This new technology, available in 750V and 1200V classes, offers improved power efficiency, density, and robustness, aiming to expand SiC advantages to mid-size and compact electric vehicles. STMicroelectronics plans to continue innovating with further SiC technology advancements, including a radical new structure, through 2027.

title: NIO Power Revolutionizes EV Mobility and Energy Storage in Europe
summary: NIO is expanding its Battery Swap technology in Europe, not only transforming EV charging but also contributing to energy storage and grid stability. The Power Swap Stations allow for quick battery changes and are being utilized in Denmark, the Netherlands, Sweden, and Germany for grid stabilization and electricity trading. NIO is also preparing to launch bi-directional Power Swap Stations to further support Europe's green energy transition and promotes a circular economy by repurposing used batteries.

title: Two major catalysts coming in October! Goldman Sachs recommends buying call options on Tesla.
summary: Goldman Sachs recommends buying call options on Tesla (TSLA.US) ahead of two significant October events: the Q3 earnings report on October 2 and the Robotaxi event on October 10. Tesla's options trading accounts for 17% of total S&P 500 options, surpassing Nvidia, and analysts anticipate meeting Q3 earnings expectations with strong growth from the China market.

title: Personal Auto Insurance Could Be Obsolete in 20 Years: Morningstar
summary: A Morningstar report suggests personal auto insurance could be largely obsolete within 20 years due to the rise of self-driving cars, shifting liability from drivers to manufacturers. While a moderate scenario predicts it might last until 2060, the most aggressive forecast sees 60% of cars as Level 4 autonomous by 2044. The report analyzes the potential impact on major insurers, noting Progressive is most at risk due to its high concentration in personal auto, while Travelers is least affected.

title: Sony Honda Mobility Adopts North American Charging Standard (NACS) for AFEELA EVs in the U.S. and Japan
summary: Sony Honda Mobility (SHM) has adopted the North American Charging Standard (NACS) for its AFEELA electric vehicles, which will launch in 2025. This agreement with Tesla Inc. will allow AFEELA EVs to access Tesla's V3 and V4 Superchargers in the U.S. and Japan, making SHM the first company in Japan to adopt NACS. Pre-orders for AFEELA will begin in the first half of 2025 in the U.S., with deliveries starting in spring 2026.

title: Elon Musk is looking into Tesla’s German workers calling in sick
summary: Elon Musk is investigating the high rate of absenteeism at Tesla's Grünheide factory in Germany, where 17% of employees called in sick in August, significantly above the industry average. This issue comes amidst production pauses due to protests and supply chain problems, as well as a slowdown in European demand for Tesla vehicles. German trade union IG Metall criticized the company's tactics, including home visits to sick employees, citing high workloads and pressure on staff.

title: Toyota Production Falls on Car Safety Scandals, US Recalls
summary: Toyota Motor Corp.'s global output and sales fell in August due to regulatory scandals in Japan and recalls abroad, exacerbating a global slump in new car sales and intense competition in China. Despite strong hybrid sales, the company's production dropped 12.6% year-over-year, while sales declined 3.7%. Other Japanese automakers like Honda and Nissan also reported production and sales decreases.

title: tesla Q3 sales figures will be released on Wednesday! The stock price has rebounded by nearly 40% from its recent low, exceeding the most optimistic expectations of major banks by more than 20%.
summary: Tesla's Q3 sales figures are anticipated to be released on Wednesday, October 2nd, with analysts expecting global deliveries of 0.462 million vehicles, a 6% year-on-year increase. Wall Street analysts maintain optimistic expectations for Tesla, raising target prices and delivery forecasts, driven by strong performance in the Chinese market and the upcoming Robotaxi event. Tesla's stock has rebounded by nearly 40% from its August 5th low, fueled by sales expectations, interest rate stimulus, and investor excitement over future AI projects like Robotaxi and Optimus.

title: Is Polestar Automotive Stock a Buy?
summary: This article evaluates Polestar Automotive (PSNY) stock, focusing on its recent financial and operational challenges. Despite an 82% sequential increase in deliveries, the company faces issues like reporting compliance problems, a complete overhaul of its leadership team, declining revenues and gross profit, and continued financial losses. The author suggests Polestar is a very risky investment suitable only for aggressive investors, highlighting that it has never been profitable and its stock is down significantly from its peak.

title: Solar, Wind, Hydro: A Guide to Alternative Energy ETFs
summary: This article provides a comprehensive guide to investing in alternative energy ETFs, highlighting their benefits such as diversification, liquidity, and cost-effectiveness. It details notable ETFs like Invesco Solar ETF (TAN) and iShares Global Clean Energy ETF (ICLN), and discusses important considerations for investors, including volatility, policy, and technological advancements. The article encourages a long-term perspective given the sector's expected growth as countries transition to sustainable energy.

title: Threat assessment: ranking risk among US companies dealing with China
summary: A market research firm, Strategy Risks, has identified the most vulnerable US companies among the top 250 publicly listed firms regarding their exposure to China. Ford Motor, Carrier, Tesla, Apple, and Coca-Cola were ranked highest due to their extensive business ties to the country. The assessment considered factors like supply chain dynamics, government connections, and industry regulations in China.

title: Tesla to End LiveOne Streaming Subsidies for Customers, Sending LiveOne Stock Down 23%
summary: Tesla will cease subsidizing LiveOne streaming for its customers starting in December, leading to a significant drop in LiveOne's stock. LiveOne will lose its preferred status in Tesla vehicles and will replace the "streaming" button with its own branded app, though grandfathered accounts will remain subsidized. This change has caused LiveOne to lower its fiscal 2025 revenue and EBITDA guidance, resulting in a substantial decline in its share price.

title: U.S. Ford Motor Company Sales Fell 11 Percent In September 2024
summary: Ford Motor Company's U.S. sales declined by 11 percent in September 2024, totaling 147,831 units. While Ford brand sales decreased, Lincoln sales saw an increase. The company's year-to-date sales for 2024 still show an increase of 2.7 percent.

title: Tesla to stop subsidizing LiveOne music subscriptions
summary: Tesla will cease subsidizing LiveOne music subscriptions for its drivers starting in December, although the partnership for in-car app integration will continue until 2026. Drivers currently benefiting from LiveOne's premium service through Premium Connectivity will need to purchase their own subscriptions for uninterrupted, high-quality music streaming. LiveOne plans to offer discounted subscriptions to Tesla drivers as this change takes effect.

title: Oracle's Larry Ellison is getting richer, rivaling Musk, Bezos, Zuckerberg
summary: Oracle co-founder and CTO Larry Ellison's net worth has surged to an estimated $208 billion, placing him firmly among the wealthiest individuals globally, behind Elon Musk and ahead of Mark Zuckerberg and Jeff Bezos, according to Forbes. His wealth growth is largely attributed to his substantial 41% stake in Oracle, whose stock has seen a 63% increase year-to-date. Oracle's strong financial performance, with reported revenues of $13.3 billion and net income of $2.9 billion in Q1, contributes significantly to this rise, with forecasts suggesting Oracle could reach a $1 trillion market capitalization by 2030.

title: Vision Marine Technologies Energizes Electric Boating with Advanced Battery Systems
summary: Vision Marine Technologies (VMT) is making significant strides in electric boating with its advanced, compact, and lightweight battery systems using Nickel Manganese Cobalt (NMC) chemistry cell technology. These batteries are powering the Four Winns H2E, offering impressive performance and range for recreational marine use. VMT is also expanding its powertrain integration to other boat manufacturers and has filed patents for its battery packs and E-Motion vehicle control unit, aiming to address critical industry challenges like retrofit availability and safety.

title: Tesla ETFs in Focus Ahead of "Robotaxi" Event
summary: Tesla is set to unveil its "Robotaxi" on Thursday, an event that has generated significant investor optimism and boosted Tesla's stock. This article highlights several ETFs with substantial Tesla allocations that investors should consider, including ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK) and Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLY). Analysts have varied expectations, with some predicting a multi-trillion dollar revenue opportunity for Tesla in the autonomous vehicle market.

title: GM says its EV losses are peaking
summary: General Motors announced that its financial losses from electric vehicles are expected to peak this year and then decrease as battery factories come online and EV sales, particularly of new models, grow. The company also plans to use advanced battery chemistry for electric pickup trucks to overcome the limits of lithium-iron phosphate batteries and recently saw a significant increase in EV sales, becoming the second-largest EV producer in the US.

title: Tesla ETFs in Focus Ahead of "Robotaxi" Event
summary: Tesla is set to unveil its "Robotaxi" at the "We, Robot" event, sparking investor interest in ETFs with significant Tesla allocations. Analysts have varied expectations, with some predicting massive revenue opportunities and others expressing skepticism about the hype. This event comes as Tesla faces increasing competition and a slowdown in EV growth.

title: Shift to EV-only future would spell job losses, Toyota chairman warns
summary: Toyota Motor's chairman, Akio Toyoda, warned that a rapid shift to an electric vehicle-only future would cause significant job losses, particularly for the 5.5 million people in Japan's automotive industry involved in engine-related technologies. He emphasized Toyota's "multi-pathway" strategy, which includes hybrids, hydrogen fuel-cell vehicles, and other powertrain technologies, in addition to EVs, as a more cautious approach to decarbonization. Toyoda estimates that EVs will constitute at most 30% of the global auto market, with diverse powertrains making up the remainder.

title: Inside the CMI Hitlist: The Five Critical Minerals Shaping Global Supply Chains
summary: This article identifies five critical minerals – Cobalt, Gallium, Graphite/Carbon, Platinum Group Metals (PGMs), and Rare Earths – that are essential for modern technology and renewable energy, consistently appearing on global critical mineral lists. The author highlights China's dominant role in the supply chains for these minerals, leading to significant geopolitical and ethical concerns, particularly regarding sourcing from regions like the Democratic Republic of Congo. The article emphasizes the urgent need for Western countries to secure alternative supply chains to reduce reliance on China and address ESG challenges.

summary: Vistra Corp. (VST) stock has surged over 200% this year, significantly outperforming the S&P 500 Utilities index. The article suggests further upside potential before Q3 2024 earnings, driven by increasing energy demand for AI data centers, strategic acquisitions expanding its nuclear and renewables capacity, and robust hedging strategies securing profitability. The company is also benefiting from a tech exodus to Texas, its home state, and federal production tax credits for nuclear power.

title: Tesla's stock price fell during the day! Investors are disappointed with the Robotaxi launch: limited surprises, insufficient details.
summary: Tesla's stock price fell significantly following a "Robotaxi Day" event that disappointed investors due to limited new technical details, insufficient information on commercialization, and the absence of the anticipated low-cost Model 2. Despite Musk showcasing new prototypes like Cybercab and Robovan, the lack of concrete timelines and detailed answers to practical implementation challenges led to market skepticism, especially given past unfulfilled promises regarding autonomous driving. Analysts remain cautious about Tesla's self-driving technology and business model without more verifiable evidence and plans to address regulatory and safety concerns.

title: Jabil to close Kentucky plant, lay off 108 workers
summary: Electronics maker Jabil is closing one of its three facilities in Florence, Kentucky, resulting in 108 layoffs, effective December 6th. The closure is due to a customer relocating operations to another Jabil site. The company plans to offer severance benefits and career outplacement assistance to impacted employees, and two other Florence facilities will remain open.

title: Freedom Holding Corp. celebrates five years on Nasdaq, marking Cyprus’ strategic role
summary: Freedom Holding Corp., parent company of Freedom24, celebrates five years on Nasdaq, highlighting its commitment to transparency and growth. Executive Director Evgenii Tiapkin discusses the challenges and rewards of the Nasdaq listing, the company's significant growth in client numbers, and Cyprus' strategic role as its headquarters. Freedom24 aims to expand its services beyond brokerage to banking with an anticipated EMI license and plans for a "super app," while emphasizing its regulatory compliance and contributions to the Cypriot economy.

title: Honda Broadens Safety Efforts to Achieve Global Goal of Zero Traffic Collision Fatalities Focusing on Driver Behavior and Traffic Safety Ecosystem
summary: Honda is expanding its "Safety For Everyone" initiative to achieve zero traffic collision fatalities involving its vehicles by 2050. This expanded focus goes beyond advanced safety technologies to include augmenting driver awareness, modifying driver behavior, and improving the traffic safety ecosystem through collaboration with government, industry, and community partners. Key initiatives include the STEM-focused Honda Safety Driven educational platform, rider/driver skills training, community grants, and the Honda Driver Coaching smartphone app.

title: Electric Vehicles May Be Struggling. G.M.’s Leader Is Still a Believer. (Published 2024)
summary: Despite a challenging year for electric vehicle sales and some manufacturers delaying EV investments, General Motors CEO Mary Barra remains committed to the company's 2035 target of phasing out gasoline cars in the U.S. G.M. anticipates its battery-powered models will become profitable by the end of the year, and sales are beginning to increase, supported by a new model priced under $30,000 after federal tax credits. Barra emphasized that the company is actively executing its plan for the transition to EVs.

title: XLY: Consumer Discretionary Dashboard For October
summary: This article analyzes the consumer discretionary sector, focusing on fundamental and momentum metrics, and identifies consumer services as the most compelling subsector due to its slight undervaluation and excellent quality score. It provides fast facts on the XLY sector benchmark and lists nine stocks that are cheaper than their peers in October.

title: Autoliv's profits meet expectations as outperforms global light vehicle market
summary: Autoliv, the world's largest maker of airbags and seatbelts, reported third-quarter profits that met expectations despite lowering its sales forecast. The company's growth outstripped global light vehicle production, and it maintained its adjusted operating margin guidance for 2024, expecting to be at the low end. CEO Mikael Bratt attributed the results to strong cost control and stabilized raw material prices.

title: General Motors Invests $625M in Lithium Americas to Boost Nevada’s Thacker Pass Lithium Project
summary: General Motors has invested $625 million in Lithium Americas for a 38% stake in the Thacker Pass lithium project in Nevada, aiming to secure a domestic supply of lithium for its EV battery production. This joint venture builds on previous investments and helps unlock a $2.3 billion U.S. DOE loan for the project, which is crucial for strengthening the U.S. EV supply chain and reducing reliance on imports. The Thacker Pass project, North America's largest known lithium deposit, is expected to produce enough lithium for one million EVs annually and contribute significantly to GM's net-zero goals.

title: Indonesia forms $1.2B EV battery venture with CATL
summary: Indonesia has partnered with China's Contemporary Amperex Technology Co Ltd (CATL) to form a $1.2 billion joint venture aimed at boosting EV battery production in the Southeast Asian nation. This initiative, which includes a project in Karawang, West Java, to increase battery output to 15 gigawatts annually, aligns with Indonesia's goal of becoming a global EV hub and establishing a comprehensive EV supply chain. The country is leveraging its nickel resources to attract foreign investors and is seeing increased activity from Chinese automakers and battery manufacturers.

title: GM, Ford face investor scrutiny as EV losses persist and gasoline vehicle pricing power wanes
summary: General Motors and Ford are facing investor scrutiny as they prepare to release their Q3 2024 results. Both automakers are struggling with substantial losses in their EV divisions while attempting to maintain profitability through gasoline-powered vehicle sales. Investors are concerned about the companies' ability to sustain pricing power in the gasoline vehicle market amidst economic headwinds and shifting consumer preferences.

title: Vicor Power Modules Enable 48V EV Power Systems
summary: Vicor has released three new automotive-grade power modules—BCM6135, DCM3735, and PRM3735—designed for 48V electric vehicle (EV) systems, offering industry-leading power density and efficiency. title: JPMorgan drops suit against Tesla over Musk tweet, warrants

The litigation stemmed from a 2014 agreement and was complicated by Elon Musk's 2018 tweet about taking Tesla private, which led JPMorgan to adjust warrant strike prices. Tesla had countersued, alleging bad-faith conduct by the bank.

summary: The S&P 500 experienced its best month of 2024 in November, rising 5.7% following a post-election stock bump. The Dow Jones Industrial Average and Russell 2000 also saw significant gains, 7.5% and 10.8% respectively. Consumer discretionary led all sectors with a 13.2% increase, driven by companies like Amazon.com Inc. and Tesla Inc., while Palantir Technologies Inc. was the top performer and Celanese Corp. the worst.

summary: Marquis Who's Who has recognized Erika Lauren Guerrero for her leadership in battery innovation as the founder and CEO of Electric Goddess. Guerrero specializes in consulting services for battery innovations across various industries and is also the co-founder of Redivivus Inc., a battery recycling technology company. Her vision for Electric Goddess includes expanding educational opportunities and understanding of safer battery technologies, with a long-term goal of advancing the battery industry and supporting space travel.

title: Chinese lidar maker Hesai adamant about global push amid EV tariffs
summary: Hesai Group, the world's largest maker of lidar sensors, is committed to global expansion despite rising EV tariffs. The company is evaluating international investment plans to make its sensors more affordable worldwide. Hesai's CFO, Andrew Fan, highlighted the advancements in lidar technology making it more reliable and cost-effective, positioning it for wide adoption in smart cars.

title: Delhi High Court to hear Tesla trademark dispute next in April 2025 as mediation continues
summary: The Delhi High Court has deferred the trademark dispute between Elon Musk’s Tesla Inc and Gurugram-based Tesla Power India Pvt Ltd to April 2025. Both parties are currently undergoing mediation to resolve the issues. Tesla Inc had accused Tesla Power India of trademark infringement and misleading consumers, leading to a court order prohibiting Tesla Power from using the Tesla trademark for its products.

title: Tesla leads November's global market value surge
summary: Tesla's market capitalization surged 38.1% in November, reaching $1.1 trillion, primarily due to expectations that CEO Elon Musk's relationship with President-elect Donald Trump will lead to relaxed regulations on self-driving cars. Other companies like Walmart and JPMorgan also saw significant market value increases, driven by strong earnings reports and investor confidence in future policies. Conversely, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company experienced a decline in market cap due to U.S. restrictions on chip sales to China.

title: The UAW’s strike on General Motors might haunt workers
summary: General Motors has laid off over 1,000 workers worldwide, including hundreds in Michigan, following the UAW strike that secured a 33% pay raise for workers. Experts suggest these layoffs are a move to counter increased labor costs and maintain competitiveness amidst challenges from rival EV manufacturers and high consumer prices. The article also highlights significant taxpayer funding given to GM by the Michigan Legislature, raising questions about job creation commitments.

title: General Motors faces water stress at 4 plants in Mexico
summary: General Motors is experiencing water stress at four of its production plants in Mexico: Ramos Arizpe, San Luis Potosí, Silao, and Toluca. This comes as the company leads light vehicle production in Mexico, with a significant increase in units produced and sold in 2024. The company is implementing conservation and recycling measures to mitigate water risk amidst increasing regulatory focus on water stress in Mexico following an atypical drought in 2023.

title: Coinbase CEO, legal chief warn law firms over SEC hires
summary: Coinbase's CEO, Brian Armstrong, and Chief Legal Officer, Paul Grewal, have issued a warning to law firms that hiring former senior U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) attorneys who were involved in actions against the crypto industry will result in Coinbase withholding business. This stance comes after the SEC sued Coinbase and Binance in June 2023 for alleged securities rules violations, which the companies deny. Armstrong specifically cited law firm Milbank's hire of Gurbir Grewal, former director of the SEC's enforcement division, as a reason Coinbase would not work with that firm.

summary: Tesla's stock has seen a significant rally in the latter half of the year, driven in part by Elon Musk's involvement in a new presidential advisory commission and the potential for self-driving technology. The article also highlights instruments for trading both upward and downward movements in Tesla's stock.

title: Rivian Stock Plunge: Time to Buy the Dip?
summary: Rivian Automotive (NASDAQ: RIVN) has experienced a significant stock plunge, being down over 30% year-to-date and 15% in the last month, despite improved performance after its third-quarter earnings report. While the company missed revenue and reported wider losses, management's expectation of a "modest positive gross profit" in Q4 and the upcoming launch of R2 and R3 models provide potential catalysts. The article suggests that Rivian's focus on trucks and SUVs differentiates it from Tesla and could lead to substantial gains if production targets and profitability are met.

title: Signet Jewelers’ Q3 Sales Slide Amid Slow Engagement Ring Sales, Tech Issues
summary: Signet Jewelers reported a 3% year-over-year sales decline in Q3 due to slow engagement ring sales and technical issues affecting its digital banners, James Allen and Blue Nile. The company's overall sales reached $1.35 billion, with same-store sales falling 1%. Signet lowered the high end of its full fiscal year guidance while raising the low end, now expecting sales between $6.74 billion and $6.81 billion.

title: Even the Pope has had an EV Epiphany
summary: Pope Francis is the first recipient of an all-electric Mercedes-Benz G-Class, which will serve as the new Popemobile. This custom-modified vehicle, featuring a height-adjustable swivel seat and a removable hard top, marks the Vatican's first green machine for the Pope. Mercedes-Benz designed the EV Popemobile with powerful electric motors, continuing its nearly 100-year tradition of supplying papal vehicles.

title: GM’s China dream fades amid shift to EVs and rise of local rivals
summary: General Motors is facing significant losses and an uncertain future in China, taking charges of over US$5 billion to restructure its operations, including factory closures. The carmaker is struggling to compete with local brands and the shift to EVs, leading to a re-evaluation of its long-term presence and ambitions in the Chinese market. The joint venture with SAIC Motor is expected to shrink its presence, potentially cutting workers and plants, and diminishing the role of brands like Buick.

title: Uber and WeRide launch robotaxi service in Abu Dhabi
summary: Uber has partnered with Chinese autonomous vehicle technology company WeRide to launch a commercial robotaxi service in Abu Dhabi, marking Uber's first international autonomous vehicle offering. The service will initially operate on a small scale between Saadiyat Island and Yas Island, and to/from Zayed International Airport, with human safety operators present in each vehicle. A fully driverless commercial launch is planned for later in 2025.

title: An F-35 fight? Support for Fort Worth-produced aircraft could derail government efficiency cut
summary: Elon Musk's proposal to cut the F-35 fighter jet program as part of government efficiency efforts faces significant opposition from Texas leaders and Lockheed Martin. The Fort Worth-produced aircraft, despite some reliability concerns, is strongly supported by state and national politicians, highlighting a potential conflict between cost-cutting and defense priorities. Lockheed Martin CFO Jay Malave anticipates scrutiny but expects the company to adapt to new administration priorities, while production delays due to software upgrades could impact deliveries.

The recognition showcases the company's commitment to a positive work environment and community involvement.

summary: General Motors is shutting down its Cruise robotaxi project, citing intense competition and high development costs. The company will instead focus on its Super Cruise partially autonomous system, expecting to save $1 billion annually. This decision follows a tumultuous period for Cruise, including an incident in October 2023 where one of its robotaxis struck and dragged a pedestrian.

title: Solar D’s robot sets new benchmark for Thailand’s solar projects
summary: Solar D Corporation Ltd launched Thailand’s first solar panel installation robot, which is 10 times faster than human workers and can install 1 megawatt in six days. The robot reduces workforce requirements, increases safety, minimizes waste, and can operate in unfavorable weather. Solar D aims to capitalize on growing demand, particularly in large-scale industrial and commercial projects, and plans to list on the Stock Exchange of Thailand in early 2026.

summary: General Motors has announced it will halt further development of its Cruise robotaxi unit, citing high capital costs and the business not being core to GM operations after investing over $10 billion. The decision comes after Cruise faced a troubled history, including safety incidents in San Francisco that led to the suspension of its operating permit and a recall. GM plans to leverage Cruise's autonomous technology expertise for its Super Cruise driver-assistance software.

title: General Motors has Called Time on Cruise Self-Driving Robotaxis
summary: General Motors has decided to stop funding its self-driving robotaxi unit, Cruise, citing high costs and a competitive market, which will likely lead to Cruise's collapse. This move aligns with GM's broader strategy to scale back EV ambitions and focus autonomous driving development on personal vehicles and its profitable gasoline-powered truck business. The decision follows a series of setbacks for Cruise, including a pedestrian incident, a criminal fine, and a reduction in its workforce, leaving Waymo and Baidu as key players in the robotaxi race.

title: GM to retreat from robotaxis and stop funding its Cruise autonomous vehicle unit

GM plans to integrate Cruise's technical team to enhance its own driver-assist technologies and expects to reduce spending by over $1 billion annually through restructuring.

title: GM to exit loss-making Cruise robotaxi business

GM plans to cut spending significantly and integrate Cruise into its driver assistance technology group.

summary: Oxford PV has unveiled new ultra-thin perovskite-based solar panels that boast 20% higher energy conversion efficiency, 30% cheaper manufacturing, and a 15% longer operational lifespan compared to current industry leaders. This breakthrough technology aims to redefine solar industry standards, offering more affordable and durable solar solutions for both large-scale and residential projects. Oxford PV expects to begin shipments in the coming quarters and plans to expand manufacturing and distribution globally.

title: XPeng Reaches 10th Consecutive Month Of Export Growth
summary: XPeng has achieved its 10th consecutive month of export growth, with 3,602 vehicles exported in November 2024. The XPeng G6 is the leading international seller, followed by the G9. The company has also expanded into Nepal and the UK, and its volume drivers, the Mona M03 and XPeng P7+, have shown strong initial performance.

title: ‘God Bless America’ fund beats political rivals with 33% gain
summary: The "God Bless America" ETF (YALL), identified with conservative investors, significantly outperformed the S&P 500 and politically-themed rival funds, gaining 32.9% this year. Its success is partly attributed to its largest holding, Tesla Inc. Despite strong performances from YALL and the Democratic-aligned NANC fund, politically-focused ETFs generally struggle to attract substantial institutional investment due to polarization and the timing of their launch.

title: The 'boring' department store whose stock is outperforming Tesla
summary: Dillard's, a regional department store, has seen its stock outperform Tesla, Apple, and Microsoft over the last five years, with a 500% increase. This success is attributed to its "drama-free consistency," focus on business fundamentals, supplier and employee loyalty, and a strategy of avoiding constant discounting. Dillard's has also been surprisingly tech-focused, adopting real-time sales and inventory tracking earlier than rivals, leading activist investor Barington Capital to suggest Macy's emulate its model.

title: It’s Official: Honda and Nissan Merging to Create Third-Largest Automaker
summary: Honda and Nissan are merging into a joint holding company by 2026, creating the world's third-largest automaker by volume, trailing only Toyota and Volkswagen. This strategic move aims to enhance their global competitiveness, particularly in the electric vehicle (EV) sector, and prevent them from falling further behind industry leaders and surging Chinese EV manufacturers. Honda will be the dominant partner, nominating the majority of directors for the new entity.

title: Royal Turf Club of Thailand Launches RTC AI Data Centre for Global Projects
summary: The Royal Turf Club of Thailand has announced the establishment of the RTC AI Data Centre in partnership with Power-All Network Co., Ltd. (a FOXCONN subsidiary) to support the "RTC x CTG Prop Trade" project, which aims to train up to 300 million individual accounts in high-skill careers. Additionally, the Royal Turf Club of Thailand, in consortium with Greenpro Capital Corp. (NASDAQ: GRNQ) and Capital Trust Group Ltd. (CTG), is driving the Thailand Entertainment Complex project, valued at US$6 billion. title: Is Tesla set to Launch Self-Driving Taxis in 2025?
summary: Elon Musk has announced plans to launch driverless ride-hailing services in California and Texas by 2025, but Tesla faces significant technical and regulatory challenges, especially in California. Texas offers a more favorable regulatory environment, yet extensive testing is still required. The company's Full Self-Driving system, despite previous setbacks and regulatory scrutiny, forms the basis for its robotaxi program, with the recent unveiling of the Cybercab causing a surge in Tesla's stock and impacting competitors.

title: Post-election riots, jail break kill 248 in Mozambique
summary: Post-election turmoil in Mozambique has led to the deaths of 248 people and a mass jailbreak of over 1,500 prisoners. The escalating violence, which began after opposition protests against alleged fraudulent election results, has also forced companies like Sasol and Tesla supplier Syrah Resources to restrict operations. An analyst suggests the ruling party will maintain power despite the ongoing negotiations and investor concerns.

title: Bitwise Files For ETF: Targets Companies With Big BTC Treasuries
summary: Bitwise has filed a proposal for a new ETF, the Bitwise Bitcoin Standard Corporations ETF, which will invest in publicly traded companies holding at least 1,000 Bitcoin in their corporate treasuries. This ETF will weight investments based on the market value of a company’s Bitcoin holdings rather than its overall market cap, aiming to capitalize on the growing trend of corporate Bitcoin adoption. The announcement comes as U.S. Bitcoin ETFs experienced significant inflows, breaking a recent outflow streak.

title: Saia, Tesla Partner to Launch Two Electric Semis
summary: Saia LTL Freight has partnered with Tesla to integrate two Tesla Semi trucks into its fleet, marking a significant milestone for Saia's 100th anniversary and its commitment to adopting new technology. During a rigorous demo period, the Tesla Semi demonstrated impressive performance in terms of range, payload capacity, grade performance, and driver comfort, achieving 1.73 kWh per mile. This collaboration highlights Saia's dedication to innovation and exploring sustainable solutions for both local and longer-haul applications.

title: Bitwise Files for ETF Tracking Companies Investing Heavily in Bitcoin
summary: Bitwise has filed a regulatory application with the SEC to launch the Bitwise Bitcoin Standard Corporations ETF, which would include companies holding at least 1,000 Bitcoin in their corporate treasuries. This ETF would also require included companies to have a market capitalization of at least $100 million and a minimum daily liquidity of $1 million, with no single component exceeding 25% weighting. The filing highlights a growing trend of Bitcoin-related investment products, prompting discussion on responsible investment choices and broader crypto adoption.

title: Bitwise proposes ETF for companies holding over 1000 Bitcoin
summary: Bitwise has proposed a new Exchange-Traded Fund (ETF), the Bitwise Bitcoin Standard Corporations ETF, which aims to invest in publicly traded companies holding at least 1,000 Bitcoin. The ETF will weight holdings based on the market value of the companies' Bitcoin reserves, rather than their overall market capitalization. This initiative reflects the growing integration of cryptocurrency into traditional financial markets as more public companies acquire Bitcoin to boost stock valuations.

title: Bitwise CEO Predicts Major Bitcoin Adoption by 2025
summary: Bitwise CEO Hunter Horsley predicts that 2025 will be a pivotal year for corporate Bitcoin adoption, driven by increasing institutional interest and improved financial products. Bitwise is launching a new ETF, the Bitwise Bitcoin Standard Corporations ETF, which tracks companies holding over 1,000 BTC in their treasuries, with MicroStrategy being a prime example. Despite past skepticism from CFOs regarding Bitcoin's price volatility, Horsley believes the foundation for wider acceptance is being laid.

title: Beijing unveils plans to boost driverless vehicle use in capital
summary: Beijing has implemented new regulations to promote autonomous driving technology, aiming to eventually introduce driverless public buses and taxis. These regulations, effective April 1, will allow autonomous vehicles passing road and safety assessments to apply for trials. This move aligns with broader efforts across China, with various companies like Baidu and Pony.ai already expanding their robotaxi fleets.

title: Lithium Market 2024 Year-End Review
summary: The lithium market in 2024 faced significant challenges due to oversupply, softer-than-expected electric vehicle demand, and geopolitical factors, leading to prolonged low prices. Despite a dip, M&A activity surged as major producers acquired smaller projects amidst a volatile landscape. The article also highlights a rebound in global EV sales (driven by China) and discusses potential impacts of US policy shifts, such as the possible repeal of EV tax credits.
